input.content:
# T6 pre-run code audit — knowable-now-fixable defects (before any run)

Read-only source audit of the workshop machinery + corpus ahead of the T6 / TASK-043 full
14-spec `workshop-run-plan-sequence` run. Goal: separate defects discoverable from source
*now* (with an exact fix) from outcomes only the run can reveal. Every load-bearing claim
below is corroborated against current source with file:line citations; the supplied T6
prediction was treated as leads, not ground truth. Scope of this deliverable is a report
only — no machinery, snippet, substrate, grounding, or product file was edited.

Bottom line: the gate plumbing is **consistent end-to-end** — every structural gate the
apply path enforces is backed by (a) a snippet generation contract that instructs the model
to produce the field, (b) a production parser that parses + carries the field, (c) a merge
rule / carrier-lift that writes it to the merged-draft key the evaluator reads, and (d) the
grounding the evaluator needs at apply-time. I found **no defect that would deterministically
fail a correct model output**. I did find two knowable-now correctness/robustness items worth
fixing (one a stale ledger figure that misleads run triage; one a vacuity gap already
acknowledged in code but worth recording), plus several prediction claims that do NOT hold and
should be corrected so run triage starts from the truth.

---

## Corroboration vs the prediction

### Confirmed against source

- **10 MB stdout cap, unchanged.** `MAX_STDOUT_BYTES = 10 * 1024 * 1024` at
  `/Users/david/Projects/workflowsPiExtension/packages/pi-workflows/dist/dispatch.js:18`.
- **Schema disk-read mitigation present.** `dist/step-agent.js:165-189`: when
  `stepSpec.output?.schema` is set and `<runDir>/outputs/<stepName>.json` exists, it reads +
  validates that file and assigns `result.output = rawOutput` (ignoring truncatable stdout);
  only when the file is absent does it fall back to parsing `result.textOutput`.
- **All 14 call steps declare `output.schema`** and the 14 referenced schema files exist:
  `.workflows/workshop-run-plan-sequence.workflow.yaml` (`schema: schemas/<spec>.schema.json`
  on each `call_*`); `.workflows/schemas/` holds all 14 `.schema.json` files. (NB: this
  corrects the older "schema files were never written" framing — they exist and are what the
  WF-13/16 disk-read mitigation depends on.)
- **Retry covers call-steps, NOT apply-steps.** Each of the 14 `call_*` agent steps carries
  `retry: { maxAttempts: 4, onExhausted: fail, steeringMessage }`
  (workflow YAML, 14 occurrences). The `apply_*` steps are `command:` steps with **no retry
  block**. So an apply-path structural `exit 3` aborts the run deterministically and is **not**
  retried — confirming the prediction. (`apply_from_substrate.py:163-170` returns 3 on a
  structural self-check failure; the `command` step has no `retry:`.)
- **Per-spec evaluators reuse `check_draft` functions.** `self_check.py:39` imports
  `check_owner_distribution, check_review_cadence` from `check_draft`; `_eval_distribution`
  calls `check_owner_distribution` (`self_check.py:429`) and `_eval_review_cadence` calls
  `check_review_cadence` (`self_check.py:506`). The final `check_draft --strict` structural
  set (3-8) is `owner_distribution / coverage / review_cadence / three_view / bilingual_views
  / decision_requests` (`check_draft.py:988-995`, `1020-1027`) — the same logic as the per-spec
  gates, so a 42/42 run implies those final checks pass (with the prose-mention caveat the
  prediction names).
- **Gate logic matches the prediction's description.** coverage = universe ⊆ (targeted ∪
  not_addressed) per dimension, structural-FAILs when `grounding is None`
  (`self_check.py:123-183`); three_view over FIXED `{student_outcomes, staff_practice,
  parent_stakeholder}` (`self_check.py:191-256`); bilingual_views over FIXED
  `{staff_english_usage, student_bilingual_progress}` (`self_check.py:267-328`);
  decision_requests well-formedness with **empty list passes by vacuity**
  (`self_check.py:344-405`, docstring 357-360 + the `for ... entries` loop that vacuously
  passes on `[]`); review_cadence = `>=4 checkpoints AND earliest < 1 Dec cycle_year`
  (`self_check.py:471-526`, `check_draft.py:616-651`).
- **No hardcoded year in the cadence gate.** Neither `self_check.py` nor `check_draft.py`
  hardcodes 2025/2024/2026 in any cadence logic (grep: the only "2025" hit is a comment in
  `check_draft.py:162` unrelated to cadence). The anchor year is read live from
  `grounding["cycle"]["starts_on"]` (`self_check.py:453-468`; `check_draft.py:1061-1066`).
  Live grounding returns `cycle.starts_on = "2025-08-01"` → anchor year **2025**, matching the
  prediction. So the prediction's "cadence check hardcoding a year that mismatches 2025"
  hypothesis does **not** hold — there is no such defect.
- **Full gate chain is wired correctly (the highest-value check).** For each structural gate,
  the field the evaluator reads on the merged draft is actually produced by the parser and
  written by the merge/carrier path:
  - coverage: parser `parse_propose_domain_alignment` writes prefill keys
    `learner_outcomes_targeted` (specs.py:3127) + `learner_outcomes_not_addressed` /
    `areas_for_improvement` / `areas_for_improvement_not_addressed` (specs.py:3148-3159,
    3355-3370); `_merge_domain_alignment` does `domain_alignment.update(prefill)`
    (apply.py:75); evaluator reads exactly those keys (`self_check.py:99-103, 163-170`). Match.
  - three_view / bilingual_views: parser carries `measurement_views` / `bilingual_views` as
    `AssistResult` attributes (assist.py:122-123; specs.py:1595-1601); `_apply_carrier_fields`
    lifts them onto the draft via `getattr` (apply.py:316-322); channels merge to draft key
    `feedback_channels` (apply.py:255), which the evaluators index-check
    (`self_check.py:229, 301`). Match.
  - decision_requests: parser carries `decision_requests` attribute (assist.py:124;
    specs.py:1984); `_apply_carrier_fields` lifts it (apply.py:324-326); evaluator reads draft
    `decision_requests` (`self_check.py:366`). Parser enforces EXACTLY the four fields
    (specs.py:1844-1849, 1876-1881), same set the evaluator checks
    (`self_check.py:336-341, 380-397`). Match.
  - review_cadence: parser prefill carries `review_events` (specs.py:3840-3844); `_merge_*`
    appends to draft; `check_review_cadence` reads `review_events` + `milestones`
    (`check_draft.py:626-627`). Match.
  - apply-time grounding is full (`include=None`, apply_from_substrate.py:118) and the live DB
    returns all gate sections: `cycle`, `learner_outcomes` (7), `areas_for_improvement` (7).
    So coverage + cadence can verify their universe rather than failing the grounding-None
    branch.

### Claims that do NOT hold / need correction

- **Prompt sizes (CORRECTED).** Rendered live against the Chiway dev DB with an **empty**
  draft: `propose-responsibilities` ≈ **75,462 chars (~75 KB)**, `decompose-action-steps` ≈
  **35,110 (~35 KB)**, `narrative-draft` ≈ **24,395 (~24 KB)**, `propose-assignments` ≈
  **17,960**, `propose-domain-alignment` ≈ **18,072**. The prediction's ~78 KB decompose / ~67
  KB narrative / ~80 KB responsibilities figures are **draft-state-dependent**: the workflow
  threads each spec's accumulated draft forward as `draft_state`, so the true run-time sizes
  grow above these base figures and are knowable only at run time. The base reality:
  responsibilities is the single heaviest spec (~75 KB before draft_state); decompose is ~35
  KB before draft_state, not ~78 KB.
- **WF-16 ledger figure (STALE — Finding 1).** The prediction calls the ledger's "decompose
  post-trim ~38 KB" stale on the grounds decompose now renders ~78 KB. The correction is the
  inverse: decompose renders **~35 KB against an empty draft** — i.e. the ledger's ~38 KB
  post-trim figure is **approximately accurate at the base**, and it is the prediction's ~78
  KB that conflated base render with draft_state-accumulated render. The genuinely stale part
  of the ledger is the `~84 KB pre-trim` / `~70 KB responsibilities` pair (see Finding 1).
- **Engine vs CLI version.** Confirmed engine package `@davidorex/pi-workflows`
  `"version": "0.31.0"` (package.json). The CLI binary version (prediction: 0.79.3) is not
  determinable from this repo's source and is correctly a run-only variable.

---

## Knowable-now-fixable findings

### Finding 1 — WF-16 ledger carries stale prompt-size figures that misdirect run triage

**Defect.** The WF-16 entry asserts `decompose's pre-trim render ≈84KB, post-trim ≈38KB;
propose-responsibilities retains the full atoms ≈70KB`. Measured live against the Chiway dev DB
today, with the FGAP-022 atom-split in place, the **base** (empty-draft) renders are
responsibilities ≈75 KB and decompose ≈35 KB. The ~38 KB decompose figure is close enough; the
`≈84KB pre-trim` and `≈70KB responsibilities` figures no longer match the current render and
will mislead whoever triages a T6 truncation abort (they will look for ~84 KB on decompose and
~70 KB on responsibilities and find ~35 KB / ~75 KB).

**Evidence.** `prompt-workshop/PI-WORKFLOWS-FINDINGS.md:39` (the WF-16 row, "Evidence" column).
Live render measured via `render_from_substrate.py --spec-key <k> --draft-path` over an empty
draft: responsibilities 75,462 chars; decompose 35,110 chars; narrative 24,395 chars.

**Why knowable now.** The figures are static text in a tracked ledger; the renderer is
deterministic for a fixed DB + empty draft, so the base sizes are measurable without the run.

**Exact fix.** In `prompt-workshop/PI-WORKFLOWS-FINDINGS.md`, the WF-16 row's Evidence column:
replace `decompose's pre-trim render ≈84KB, post-trim ≈38KB; propose-responsibilities retains
the full atoms ≈70KB` with the measured base figures and an explicit note that draft_state
accumulation pushes run-time sizes higher: e.g. `decompose base render ≈35KB (empty draft);
propose-responsibilities base render ≈75KB (the single heaviest spec, retaining the
division_responsibility_atoms section); both grow above these figures by the accumulated
draft_state the workflow threads forward, so run-time size is higher and bounded only at run
time.` Keep the 52 MB stdout-volume note as a run-observed datum (label it as such).

### Finding 2 — `decision_requests` gate passes an empty list by vacuity (record, not a bug)

**Defect (acknowledged-by-design, worth recording for T6 triage).** The `decision_requests`
structural gate passes when the merged-draft `decision_requests` list is empty — completeness
("every unowned precursor was escalated") is explicitly delegated to adversarial review
(DEC-53), not this deterministic gate. The snippet instructs the model to return `[]` when no
precursor is unowned (06-propose-assignments.md:41, :50). Consequence: spec 06 can clear its
structural gate with an empty `decision_requests` even if the action steps DO name unowned
precursors the model failed to escalate. This is intended (the docstring states it), so it is
NOT a code defect to fix — but it is a knowable-now blind spot the T6 evaluation must cover by
adversarial read, not infer from a green gate.

**Evidence.** `self_check.py:357-360` (docstring: "an EMPTY list PASSES (well-formed by
vacuity)…completeness…is ADVERSARIAL per DEC-53, NOT this deterministic gate") and the loop at
`self_check.py:370-405` (vacuous pass on `[]`); snippet contract
`prompt-workshop/snippets/06-propose-assignments.md:41,50`.

**Why knowable now.** The vacuity behavior is in the evaluator source and the snippet contract;
no run needed.

**Exact fix.** No code change (the vacuity is a deliberate DEC-53 boundary). The fix is a
**triage instruction** recorded in the runbook so the green-gate is not over-read: in
`prompt-workshop/RUNBOOK-T6-14spec-run.md`, under the spec-06 / result-evaluation section, add
one line: `decision_requests structural gate is well-formedness only and PASSES on an empty
list (DEC-53 delegates completeness to adversarial review) — when evaluating spec 06, read the

input.prompt:
READ-ONLY research. Goal: gather the CURRENT, authoritative best practices for engineering an LLM prompt that must reliably produce a STRUCTURED JSON output satisfying hard structural constraints — so a downstream deterministic validator does not reject it. You MAY look things up on the web (WebSearch/WebFetch) and via any docs tool available; the user explicitly authorized looking them up. Prefer authoritative sources (Anthropic's prompt engineering guide, OpenAI/cookbook, established prompting research). The prompts here run on the model `deepseek-v4-pro` (a reasoning model, thinkingLevel high) via the `pi` agent — so favor MODEL-GENERAL techniques, and note any that are model-specific.

The concrete situation (so your guidance is targeted, not generic): each prompt is a single-shot instruction that ends with an "OUTPUT CONTRACT" requiring exactly one JSON object with named keys. A separate deterministic checker then HARD-FAILS (aborts) the run if the output violates a structural rule. The rules a model must not violate include: (a) COVERAGE — every item in an enumerated catalogue (e.g. 7 learner outcomes, 7 improvement areas) must appear in exactly one of two arrays (targeted OR a not_addressed array), never both, never neither; (b) DISTRIBUTION — assignments across N divisions such that more than one division is used AND no division owns more than 50% of items; (c) FIXED-KEY MAPS — an object with exactly a fixed set of keys, each value an index-or-{gap}; (d) WELL-FORMED LISTS — each element has exactly K non-empty string fields; (e) CADENCE — at least 4 dated events with the earliest before a date threshold. The model sometimes fails these (omits a catalogue row, concentrates assignments, drops a required key, back-loads dates).

Report, concretely and citably:
1. The highest-leverage techniques to make a model reliably satisfy enumerated-coverage and exact-cardinality constraints (e.g. explicit checklists, "before you output, verify each X is present", restating the constraint at the point of output, count-and-confirm patterns, self-verification/reflection steps, worked examples / few-shot, ordering of instruction vs data, delimiters/structure, positive vs negative phrasing, repetition of critical constraints at top and bottom).
2. What the literature says about WHERE in the prompt a hard constraint should live (primacy/recency — top, bottom, both), and whether an explicit "self-check before emitting" instruction measurably improves structured-output compliance, especially for reasoning models.
3. Techniques specific to JSON-structure fidelity (avoiding extra keys, exact key sets, non-empty fields) and to "account for every item in a list" coverage.
4. Any technique that is counterproductive or risky for a reasoning model (e.g. over-long prompts, conflicting instructions, contradictory permissions).
5. The risk of CONTRADICTORY instructions — where a prompt both permits and forbids the same behavior — and how best practice resolves it.

Return a tight, cited list of techniques with one-line rationale each, grouped by the constraint type they serve, plus the source URLs. This will be applied to tighten ~4 prompt snippets so the model reliably satisfies the gates.

## Context

The user directs a new go-forward workflow rule: work is done per task on a separate branch — the orchestrator creates the branch first, IMPL agents commit on it, and the orchestrator merges only after all success criteria are validated and only on the user's explicit authority. This governs all future tasks (including the paused prompt-tightening). Record it in CLAUDE.md.

## The edit (CLAUDE.md only, scope = this rule)

Add one bullet to the **## Orchestrator discipline** section, immediately after the existing final bullet "You never leave the working tree dirty…" (line 125):

> - **Per-task branching**: each task is done on its own branch. The orchestrator creates it FIRST with `git switch -c <branch>` before any IMPL dispatch. IMPL agents commit on that branch. The orchestrator merges the branch to the main branch only after ALL the task's success criteria are validated, and only on the user's explicit authority.

Faithful to the directive verbatim (mandate-002): per-task branch; orchestrator-first `git switch -c`; IMPL commits; orchestrator merges post-validation; user-authority-only merge. No other CLAUDE.md content touched.

## Verification

- `grep -n "Per-task branching" CLAUDE.md` shows the bullet under Orchestrator discipline.
- Commit the single-file change with a plain `git commit -m` message (no shell-special chars).

The prompt-tightening plan (gated-snippet hardening + the snippet-06 distribution contradiction, via the canonical lockstep edit path: snippet body → mirror to `.workshopping` fragment + re-stamp → verify-render-parity 14/14) is paused and re-planned separately after this rule lands — and it will run under this new branching rule.

Targeted situation: each prompt ends with an "OUTPUT CONTRACT" requiring exactly one JSON object with named keys; a separate checker HARD-ABORTS the run if the output violates a structural rule. The rule classes the model must satisfy:
- COVERAGE: every item of an enumerated catalogue (e.g. 7 outcomes, 7 areas) must appear in exactly one of two arrays (targeted OR not_addressed) — never both, never neither.
- DISTRIBUTION: assign N items across divisions so MORE THAN ONE division is used AND no division owns MORE THAN 50% of items. The four gated snippets are `prompt-workshop/snippets/02-propose-domain-alignment.md`, `06-propose-assignments.md`, `11-suggest-feedback-channels.md`, `14-propose-review-loop.md`.

Establish, with file:line / id evidence:

1. THE SNIPPET↔FRAGMENT MAP. For EACH of the four gated snippets, determine which `.workshopping` prompt-fragment item(s) carry the GATE-INSTRUCTION text that would be edited — i.e. the per-spec unique-prose fragment(s) (FRAG-intro-* / FRAG-contract-* per the WORKSHOPPING-SUBSTRATE-SPEC.md §1.3/§1.4 factoring), NOT the shared grounding-block fragments. Specifically for snippet 06: which fragment id holds the assignment-instruction prose containing the line "Prefer distributing ownership across divisions … When the step set genuinely clusters under one purview, best-fit governs over forced spread." (the contradiction to fix) and the decision_requests instruction; for 02 the COVERAGE RULE prose; for 11 the three-view + bilingual prose; for 14 the cadence prose. Read `.workshopping/prompt-spec.json` (the `fragment_refs` for SPEC-propose-assignments etc.) and `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json` to name the exact fragment ids + show their current body text. Use the pi-context CLI on PATH against the `.workshopping` substrate: `pi-context read-block --block prompt-spec --cwd .workshopping` / `read-block-item` / `filter-block-items` (discover the right block/arrayKey names from `.workshopping/config.json`).

2. THE EDIT + RE-STAMP PROCEDURE. Determine exactly how a fragment body edit is landed in the substrate and its `content_hash` re-stamped (RFC 8785 JCS): which dispatch script or CLI op writes a fragment body and recomputes the hash (look in `prompt-workshop/dispatch/` for a re-stamp/restamp/canonicalize script, and check whether `pi-context update-block-item`/`write-block` on the `.workshopping` substrate recomputes content_hash, or whether a separate JCS re-stamp step is required). 4. ORDERING + CONSTRAINTS. Per the WORKSHOPPING-SUBSTRATE-SPEC.md whitespace-absorption / byte-equivalence rules: what must hold for an edited fragment so parity still passes (e.g. the fragment body must carry the exact surrounding blank lines; a content change must be mirrored identically in both snippet and fragment). Flag any footgun (e.g. a fragment shared across multiple specs — editing it changes every spec that references it; confirm whether the gate-instruction fragments for 02/06/11/14 are per-spec-unique or shared).

import json

with open('.workshopping/prompt-spec.json') as f:
    specs_data = json.load(f)

# For each of the 4 specs, collect fragments used
target_specs = ['propose-domain-alignment', 'propose-assignments', 'suggest-feedback-channels', 'propose-review-loop']

spec_fragments = {}
for spec_key in target_specs:
    spec = next(s for s in specs_data['specs'] if s['spec_key'] == spec_key)
    spec_fragments[spec_key] = set(spec['fragment_refs'])

# Check for shared fragments across these 4 specs
all_fragments = set()
for frags in spec_fragments.values():
    all_fragments.update(frags)

print("Fragment sharing among the 4 gated specs:\n")
for frag_id in sorted(all_fragments):
    specs_with_frag = [sk for sk, frags in spec_fragments.items() if frag_id in frags]
    if len(specs_with_frag) > 1:
        print(f"  {frag_id:50} -> {specs_with_frag}")
    
print("\n\nIntro fragments (the editable ones):")
for spec_key in target_specs:
    intros = [f for f in spec_fragments[spec_key] if 'intro' in f]
    print(f"\n{spec_key}:")
    for intro in intros:
        print(f"  {intro}")

# Tighten the gated snippets so the model meets each structural gate (fix snippet-06's distribution contradiction)

## Context

The next full workshop run (T6) aborts deterministically (apply-path `exit 3`) if the model's output fails a per-spec structural gate. A pre-run audit + a direct read of the gated snippets found the corpus mostly well-instructed, with one real generation/enforcement **contradiction**: snippet 06 (`propose-assignments`) line 39 instructs "When the step set genuinely clusters under one purview, **best-fit governs over forced spread**" — it *licenses* the >50% concentration that the distribution gate (`check_owner_distribution`: >1 owner AND none >50%) hard-aborts on. The other three gated specs (02 coverage, 11 three-view+bilingual, 14 cadence) instruct their gates correctly but lack an at-output self-check, the highest-probability *content* failure being spec-02 coverage.

Goal: tighten the four gated snippet bodies so a capable model satisfies each gate by construction — removing the snippet-06 contradiction and adding at-output self-checks — using prompt-engineering best practice. **The validator/gates/schemas are NOT touched** (the distribution gate is a structural process-blocker; relaxing it — e.g. a `compliance_notes` escape that passes >50% — is explicitly rejected). Work on branch `tighten-gated-snippets`; merge to main only on the user's authority after all success criteria validate.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] Snippet 06 contains no instruction permitting a single division to own a majority of steps; it states distributed ownership as a requirement and directs the model, before output, to verify more than one division owns steps and none owns more than half.
- [ ] Snippet 06 still directs single best-fit assignment per step and does not instruct assigning a step to an ill-fitting division merely to spread (the fix removes the contradiction without inducing mis-assignment); where best-fit genuinely concentrates, it directs re-examination of each step's fit, not silent concentration.
- [ ] Each of the four gated snippets (02, 06, 11, 14) instructs the model, at the point of output, to self-verify the exact structural property its gate enforces: 02 = every catalogue learner-outcome and improvement-area appears exactly once across targeted/not_addressed; 06 = >1 owner and none >half (+ each decision_request has its four non-empty fields); 11 = the fixed three measurement_views keys and two bilingual_views keys each an index-or-gap with a channel for every non-deferred view/side; 14 = ≥4 review checkpoints with the earliest scheduled before 1 December of the cycle's first year.
- [ ] The four edited snippet bodies and their `.workshopping` fragments render byte-identical: `verify-render-parity` reports GATE 14/14 and both cross-checks PASS (exit 0).
- [ ] Each edited `.workshopping` fragment's `content_hash` changed (re-stamped) and the substrate validates clean.
- [ ] The validator, gate logic, and output schemas are unchanged from before this task (`check_draft.py`, `self_check.py`, `.workflows/schemas/*` identical).
- [ ] Working tree clean on the branch; changes committed; the per-spec self-checks are additive prompt text that does not alter the JSON output contract (no new/removed output keys).

## The edits (prompt bodies only; per-spec-unique fragments, no shared-fragment footgun)

Lockstep per snippet — edit the snippet MD **and** its substrate fragment to identical new text (preserving the fragment's exact surrounding blank lines), then re-stamp + parity. Fragment map (verified):

| Snippet | Fragment id | Tightening |
|---|---|---|
| `02-propose-domain-alignment.md` | `FRAG-intro-propose-domain-alignment-8` | Keep the COVERAGE RULE; add a before-output self-check: count every catalogue learner-outcome and area against the targeted ∪ not_addressed arrays; each appears exactly once, never both/neither. |
| `06-propose-assignments.md` | `FRAG-intro-propose-assignments-5` | **Remove** "When the step set genuinely clusters under one purview, best-fit governs over forced spread." Replace with: assign each step its single best-fit division; a school-wide plan's steps span divisions so best-fit distributes; before output verify >1 division owns steps and none owns more than half; if best-fit concentrates >half under one division, re-examine each step's fit rather than forcing or mis-assigning. Keep the decision_requests prose; add a self-check that each entry has its four non-empty fields. |
| `11-suggest-feedback-channels.md` | `FRAG-intro-suggest-feedback-channels-5` | Keep three-view + bilingual prose; add a before-output self-check: measurement_views has exactly its three keys and bilingual_views exactly its two, each an index-or-{gap}, with a channel present for every view/side not explicitly deferred. |
| `14-propose-review-loop.md` | `FRAG-intro-propose-review-loop-9` | Keep cadence prose; add a before-output self-check: at least four review_events, and the earliest scheduled_date before 1 December of the cycle's first year; if not, add/rebalance checkpoints. |

Best-practice basis (from the research pass, model-general for the reasoning model deepseek-v4-pro): resolve the contradiction by removing the permissive clause and stating the constraint as a hard requirement (not by relaxing the validator); add an explicit "verify before you emit" self-check (high-leverage for reasoning models on coverage/cardinality/distribution); restate the structural constraint at the point of output; keep additions terse to avoid over-constraining the chain-of-thought.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline, on branch `tighten-gated-snippets`)

1. **Pre-file** `.context` task TASK-054 (these acceptance_criteria; membership STORY-010 / phase) before any IMPL.
2. **IMPL agent** (self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity): for each of the four snippets, edit the MD body + the matching fragment via `pi-context update-block-item --cwd .workshopping --block prompt-fragment --arrayKey fragments --match '{"id":"FRAG-…"}' --updates @scratch.json` (re-stamps content_hash), to identical text; after all four, run the parity gate; commit on the branch.
3. **Adversarial audit** (separate agent): verify each success criterion by id — the contradiction is gone; best-fit accuracy preserved (no mis-assignment instruction); each gate has an at-output self-check; parity 14/14; validator/schemas byte-unchanged (`git diff` shows only the 4 snippets + prompt-fragment.json); content_hashes changed. Loop re-IMPL → re-audit to zero findings.
4. **Cascade**: VER-NNN + `verification_verifies_item` edge + `complete-task` TASK-054; `context-validate`; LOG + subagent-invocations rows.
5. **Merge**: report green criteria; merge `tighten-gated-snippets` → main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- `DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py` (from `school-improvement-plans/`) → `GATE: 14/14`, cross-checks (a)/(b) PASS, exit 0.
- `git diff main --stat` shows only `prompt-workshop/snippets/0{2,6}-*.md`, `11-*.md`, `14-*.md` and `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json` (+ `.context` task/VER objects) — never `check_draft.py` / `self_check.py` / `.workflows/schemas/*`.
- Re-read each edited fragment; confirm `content_hash` differs from the pre-edit value.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- One atomic step per turn; read-before-write; `pi-context` CLI directly (no `node dist/bin.js`, no echo/cat/loops); commit messages plain `-m` unless shell-special chars require `-F`.
- Observed-green-before-commit; the snippet+fragment edits for one spec land together so parity is never committed red.
- Do NOT touch the validator, gate logic, or schemas. Do NOT add/remove output-contract keys.
- Cleanup via `git restore`/`rm`, never `git reset`.

## Critical files

- `prompt-workshop/snippets/0{2,6}-*.md`, `11-suggest-feedback-channels.md`, `14-propose-review-loop.md` — snippet bodies.
- `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json` — the four `FRAG-intro-*` fragments (via `pi-context … --cwd .workshopping`).
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py` — the parity gate (read-only here).
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/self_check.py`, `dispatch/check_draft.py`, `.workflows/schemas/*` — the gates/schemas (read-only ground truth; must stay unchanged).

IMPL task on git branch `tighten-gated-snippets` (already checked out) in /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan. Tighten four workshop prompt snippets so the model satisfies each per-spec structural gate by construction. Execute the steps; STOP and report if anything is ambiguous or a parity check won't go green. Do NOT interpret beyond this directive.

HARD CONSTRAINTS (violating any = stop):
- Edit ONLY prompt bodies: the four snippet MDs + their matching `.workshopping` fragments. Do NOT touch `prompt-workshop/dispatch/check_draft.py`, `prompt-workshop/dispatch/self_check.py`, `.workflows/schemas/*`, or any validator/gate/schema. The gates' thresholds stay exactly as-is.
- Do NOT add or remove any key in any OUTPUT CONTRACT (no JSON output-shape change). The self-checks you add are PROSE the model reads before emitting — they change instructions, never the output schema.
- Each edit is a LOCKSTEP pair: the snippet MD body AND its `.workshopping` per-spec-unique fragment must end up carrying IDENTICAL text (the fragment body also carries the exact surrounding blank lines it currently has). The parity gate proves they match.
- Stage SPECIFIC paths when committing (never `git add -A`); an untracked file `prompt-workshop/T6-PRERUN-AUDIT.md` exists and must NOT be committed.

THE FOUR EDITS (snippet MD path → fragment id in `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`):

1. `prompt-workshop/snippets/02-propose-domain-alignment.md` → `FRAG-intro-propose-domain-alignment-8`.
   KEEP the existing "COVERAGE RULE" paragraph. ADD, immediately before the "OUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):" line, a terse before-output self-check sentence instructing: before emitting, verify that every catalogue learner outcome and every catalogue area for improvement appears exactly once across its targeted array and its matching not_addressed array — never in both, never in neither — by checking each catalogue item against your two arrays.

2. `prompt-workshop/snippets/06-propose-assignments.md` → `FRAG-intro-propose-assignments-5`. (THE CONTRADICTION FIX.)
   In the bullet that currently reads: "- Prefer distributing ownership across divisions — this is a school-wide plan; assign each step to its best-fit division so ownership spreads across the divisions that carry relevant responsibilities. When the step set genuinely clusters under one purview, best-fit governs over forced spread."
   REMOVE the final sentence "When the step set genuinely clusters under one purview, best-fit governs over forced spread." Rewrite the bullet so it: assigns each step its single best-fit division (best-fit accuracy preserved); states that because a school-wide plan's steps span divisions, the best-fit assignments distribute — more than one division owns steps and no single division owns more than half of them; and directs that you must NOT assign a step to an ill-fitting division merely to spread. Do NOT instruct or permit concentrating a majority under one division.
   Then ADD, immediately before the "OUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):" line, a terse before-output self-check: before emitting, count the assignments per division and verify more than one division owns steps and no division owns more than half; if your best-fit assignments would concentrate more than half under one division, re-examine each step's best fit rather than forcing or mis-assigning; and verify each decision_requests entry carries its four non-empty fields.
   KEEP the existing decision_requests paragraph and the OUTPUT CONTRACT unchanged in shape.

3. `prompt-workshop/snippets/11-suggest-feedback-channels.md` → `FRAG-intro-suggest-feedback-channels-5`.
   KEEP the three-view + bilingual prose. ADD, immediately before the "OUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):" line, a terse before-output self-check: before emitting, confirm measurement_views has exactly its three keys (student_outcomes, staff_practice, parent_stakeholder) and bilingual_views exactly its two (staff_english_usage, student_bilingual_progress), each set to a channel index or a {gap}, and that a channel exists for every view and bilingual side you did not explicitly defer.

4. `prompt-workshop/snippets/14-propose-review-loop.md` → `FRAG-intro-propose-review-loop-9`.
   KEEP the cadence prose. ADD, immediately before the "OUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):" line, a terse before-output self-check: before emitting, confirm review_events contains at least four checkpoints and the earliest scheduled_date falls before 1 December of the cycle's first year; if not, add or rebalance checkpoints so it does.

Keep all additions terse and in the same register/voice as the surrounding prompt; they are reinforcement, not new output structure.

PROCEDURE per snippet (do all four, then the gate):
a. Read the snippet MD and confirm the exact current text you will change.
b. Edit the snippet MD body (Edit tool).
c. Read the matching fragment's current body: `pi-context read-block-item --block prompt-fragment --id <FRAG-id> --cwd .workshopping` (confirm the block/arrayKey names against `.workshopping/config.json`; the array_key is `fragments`). The fragment body carries leading/trailing blank lines (e.g. a leading "\n" and a trailing "\n\nOUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):") — preserve that surrounding spacing exactly; only the inner instruction text changes, mirroring the snippet edit.
d. Write the new fragment body identical to the snippet's new corresponding segment: build the updated object to a scratch JSON file and `pi-context update-block-item --block prompt-fragment --arrayKey fragments --match '{"id":"<FRAG-id>"}' --updates @/tmp/<file>.json --writer '{"kind":"agent","agent_id":"impl"}' --cwd .workshopping` (this re-stamps content_hash).

AFTER all four pairs:
e. Run the parity gate from `school-improvement-plans/`:
   `DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py`
   Require `GATE: 14/14`, cross-checks (a)/(b) PASS, exit 0. If any spec FAILs, the snippet and its fragment differ in bytes — read the emitted diff and fix the fragment body (the surrounding blank lines or the inner text) until 14/14. Do not proceed red.

input.content:
snippets: tighten four gated prompts with before-emit self-checks + fix snippet-06 distribution contradiction

Adds a terse before-output self-check sentence to four workshop prompt
snippets so the model is more likely to satisfy each per-spec structural
gate by construction. Each edit is a lockstep pair: the snippet MD body and
its matching .workshopping intro fragment now carry identical text; the
content-addressed fragments are re-stamped (new content_hash + objects).
No OUTPUT CONTRACT key was added or removed in any snippet; the self-checks
are prose instructions the model reads before emitting, not schema changes.

02 propose-domain-alignment (FRAG-intro-propose-domain-alignment-8):
  keeps the COVERAGE RULE; adds a check that every catalogue learner
  outcome and area for improvement appears exactly once across its targeted
  array and its matching not_addressed array (never both, never neither).

06 propose-assignments (FRAG-intro-propose-assignments-5) -- contradiction fix:
  removes the prior bullet's final sentence ("When the step set genuinely
  clusters under one purview, best-fit governs over forced spread"), which
  contradicted the distribution gate by permitting concentration under one
  division. The bullet now: assigns each step its single best-fit division
  (accuracy preserved); states that because a school-wide plan's steps span
  divisions the best-fit assignments distribute -- more than one division
  owns steps and no division owns more than half; and forbids mis-assigning
  to spread. Adds a before-emit self-check counting assignments per division
  (more than one owner, none over half; re-examine best fit rather than
  force/mis-assign on concentration) and verifying each decision_requests
  entry carries its four non-empty fields. decision_requests paragraph and
  OUTPUT CONTRACT shape unchanged.

11 suggest-feedback-channels (FRAG-intro-suggest-feedback-channels-5):
  keeps the three-view + bilingual prose; adds a check that measurement_views
  has exactly its three keys and bilingual_views exactly its two, each set to
  a channel index or a {gap}, with a channel present for every non-deferred
  view and bilingual side.

14 propose-review-loop (FRAG-intro-propose-review-loop-9):
  keeps the cadence prose; adds a check that review_events has at least four
  checkpoints and the earliest scheduled_date falls before 1 December of the
  cycle's first year, rebalancing if not.

verify-render-parity gate: GATE 14/14, cross-checks (a)/(b) PASS, exit 0 --
the snippet pipeline and the substrate-assembled fragment_refs render
byte-identical for all 14 specs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

A1. **Snippet-06 contradiction gone.** `prompt-workshop/snippets/06-propose-assignments.md` must contain NO instruction permitting a single division to own a majority of steps. Grep the file for "best-fit governs" / "forced spread" / "clusters under one purview" — must be ABSENT. The assignment bullet must instead require distributed ownership (>1 division, none over half). FAIL if any permission-to-concentrate remains.

A2. **No mis-assignment induced.** Snippet 06 must still direct a single best-fit division per step and must explicitly NOT tell the model to assign a step to an ill-fitting division merely to spread. FAIL if the rewrite drops best-fit accuracy or instructs forcing spread.

A3. **At-output self-checks present and correct, all four.** For each snippet, a before-output self-check must instruct verifying the property its gate enforces, and must match the gate's actual logic (read the gate in `prompt-workshop/dispatch/self_check.py` to confirm the self-check describes the SAME property, not a near-miss): 02 = every catalogue learner-outcome and area appears exactly once across targeted/not_addressed; 06 = >1 owner and none over half + decision_request four-non-empty-fields; 11 = the fixed three measurement_views keys and two bilingual_views keys, each index-or-{gap}, channel per non-deferred view/side; 14 = ≥4 review_events, earliest scheduled_date before 1 Dec of the cycle's first year. FAIL a criterion whose self-check is absent, wrong, or describes a different property than the gate checks.

A4. **No output-contract change.** The self-checks are prose only. Diff each snippet's OUTPUT CONTRACT key set against the pre-commit version (`git show f73f6af^:prompt-workshop/snippets/<f>` vs working): no JSON key added or removed. FAIL on any output-shape change.

A5. **Validator/gates/schemas untouched.** `git diff f73f6af^ f73f6af --stat` must show ONLY the four snippet MDs + `.workshopping/` (fragment + objects). It must NOT touch `prompt-workshop/dispatch/check_draft.py`, `prompt-workshop/dispatch/self_check.py`, or `.workflows/schemas/*`. FAIL if any validator/gate/schema file changed.

A6. **Snippet↔fragment byte-parity.** Run the parity gate from `school-improvement-plans/`: `DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py`. Require `GATE: 14/14`, both cross-checks PASS, exit 0. FAIL on any mismatch (it would mean a snippet MD and its fragment diverged).

A7. **Fragment re-stamp.** The four edited fragments (`FRAG-intro-propose-domain-alignment-8`, `-propose-assignments-5`, `-suggest-feedback-channels-5`, `-propose-review-loop-9`) must have content_hash values in the committed `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json` that differ from the pre-commit values (`git show f73f6af^:.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`). FAIL if any is unchanged.

A8. **Pointer hygiene / steady state.** Confirm `.pi-context.json` `contextDir` is `.context` (the IMPL transiently switched it to `.workshopping` to write the substrate, then switched back). Confirm `.pi-context.json` is gitignored (so the switch left no tracked change) and `git status --short` shows only the untracked `prompt-workshop/T6-PRERUN-AUDIT.md`. FAIL if the pointer is left on `.workshopping` or the tree carries an unexplained change.

For each criterion: PASS/FAIL with exact evidence (grep output, the diff, the parity block, the hash pairs, the pointer). Be skeptical — your value is finding what's wrong. Note separately (not as a finding) whether the four self-check texts read naturally in the prompt voice. End with `Total findings: N`.

input.new_string:
The warnings are semantically correct per the invariant, but indistinguishable from a real "forgot to close the gap" violation. | open-upstream (expected-transient here; not blocking); candidate: an `is_closer` / closure-designator attribute on the `task_addresses_gap` edge, or a `gated-closure` invariant class that exempts completed preparers when a non-complete designated closer exists |
| CTX-12 | footgun | A non-active substrate (e.g. `.workshopping`, sibling to the active `.context`) is NOT addressable by `--cwd <dir>`: `pi-context read-config --cwd .workshopping` returns `{config:null}` and `read-block --cwd .workshopping` errors "no `.pi-context.json` bootstrap pointer at `.../.workshopping/.pi-context.json`". The CLI resolves a substrate via a bootstrap pointer (`.pi-context.json`) found at the cwd, not by treating `--cwd` as a substrate-config dir; `.workshopping/` has no own pointer (it is a substrate the ROOT `.pi-context.json` names via `contextDir`). Consequence: every read/write to a non-active substrate requires `context-switch`ing the root pointer to it and back — which transiently violates the project's "`.pi-context.json` stays on `.context`" steady-state rule and, on a crash mid-edit, would strand the pointer on `.workshopping`. Verified directly (pointer on `.context`): both `--cwd .workshopping` ops fail as above; the working substrate edit (TASK-054, the 4 FRAG-intro fragment writes) was only landable via switch-out-and-back. | open-upstream (worked around via transient `context-switch` + restore; `.pi-context.json` is gitignored so the switch leaves no tracked change); candidate: let `--cwd <dir>` resolve a substrate whose `config.json` lives directly at `<dir>` (no pointer needed), OR a `--substrate <dir>` flag that targets a sibling substrate for one op without moving the global pointer |

input.description: Adversarial audit of the T6 run result
input.prompt:
ADVERSARIAL audit, READ-ONLY. The T6 full 14-spec workshop run just completed (exit 0, 42/42) and `check_draft.py --strict` returned RESULT PASS / exit 0. Your job is to FALSIFY that this is a genuinely sound, non-vacuous result that warrants closing TASK-034, re-assessing TASK-026, and closing FGAP-033. Default to FAIL on doubt. End with `Total findings: N`.

Artifacts:
- Final assembled draft: `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` (and the per-spec `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-*.json` snapshots).
- The structural checker: `prompt-workshop/dispatch/check_draft.py` (run it yourself to confirm).
- Run from `school-improvement-plans/` with `DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" uv run python ...`.

Verify each, with evidence from the actual draft JSON:

V1. **Re-confirm the gate independently.** Re-run `check_draft.py --strict --draft /tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` and confirm exit 0 / RESULT PASS. Report the structural-check block verbatim.

V2. **Distribution is real, not degenerate.** Read the draft's assignments. Confirm 5 distinct owning divisions with largest share ~33% is backed by actual distinct action-step→division assignments (not e.g. 1-2 real steps padded). Count the action steps and the per-division assignment spread. FAIL if the distribution pass is vacuous (too few steps, or owners assigned to ill-fitting divisions just to spread).

V3. **Coverage gap is a legitimate explicit deferral, not a silent drop.** Check 4 reported improvement areas 6/7 present, 0 gaps — meaning the 7th area is recorded in `areas_for_improvement_not_addressed` with a reason. Confirm that: read the draft's `domain_alignment`, verify all 7 catalogue areas + all 7 learner outcomes are each in exactly one of targeted/not_addressed, and the deferred one carries a substantive reason. FAIL if any catalogue item is silently missing (in neither) or the not_addressed reason is empty/boilerplate.

V4. **decision_requests = 0 is legitimate, not a hidden miss.** Check 8 passed with 0 entries (vacuous pass per DEC-53). Read the action steps; look for steps whose descriptions name a PRECURSOR that no division can own (a leadership/PO/board decision, a policy authorization, a growth-target, another division's output). If any genuinely-unowned precursor exists but was NOT escalated as a decision_request, that is a finding (the empty list hid an escalation miss). If none exists, 0 is correct.

V5. **The draft is substantive, not empty-passing.** Spot-check that milestones, success_criteria, action steps (with owners + timelines + evidence + responsibilities), feedback channels, accreditation standards, communications, review events, and revision rules are present and non-trivial (counts in the same ballpark as the prior clean draft: ~4 milestones, ~6 criteria, ~6 steps, ~4 channels, ~5 standards, review loop with ≥4 events). FAIL if any major section is empty or a single-item stub that passed its gate vacuously.

V6. **The closures are warranted.** State whether the evidence supports: TASK-034 (whole-draft coverage passes by construction), TASK-026 (the responsibility-map-driven distribution premise is validated — distinct actions spread across divisions per purview), FGAP-033 (the whole-draft invariants now hold in a real assembled run, not check-only). FAIL a closure that the draft does not actually support.

Return per-criterion PASS/FAIL with concrete numbers/excerpts from the draft, an overall verdict on whether T6 is genuinely closeable, and `Total findings: N`. Do not change anything.

input.prompt:
IMPL task on git branch `t6-run` (already checked out) in /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan. Produce ONE new self-contained HTML file that renders the T6 workshop draft as a WASC-facing "canonical" maximalist view, matching the existing precedent. STOP and report if anything is ambiguous; do not invent plan content.

NEW FILE (no overwrite — confirm it does not exist first): `web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html`

PRECEDENT TO MATCH (read it fully first): `web/canonical-chiway-first-draft.html` — a self-contained, inline-CSS, print-ready, WASC-Sample-mirroring "Lens A: Canonical" maximalist render with minimal interaction (glossary/standard popovers, plan selector). Match its document structure, visual style (the same CSS variables / typography / section layout), and self-containment (it must open standalone over file:// — keep CSS inline; if the precedent uses any JS, keep the new file equally self-contained, no external module imports that would break file://). This is a MAXIMALIST full render (mirror the precedent), NOT a distillation — render the whole draft, do not compress or omit sections.

DATA (the only source of content — render faithfully, fabricate nothing): `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json`. Render EVERY populated section:
- `plan` (the plan-level fields: name/title, current_state, desired_state, rationale, etc.), `meta`.
- `domain_alignment` — show BOTH the targeted arrays (learner_outcomes, areas_for_improvement, stakeholder_impact, policies_established/revised, each with its rationale) AND the `*_not_addressed` deferral arrays with their reasons (the coverage accounting is the point — surface the deferred "Bilingual Environment" area with its reason).
- `milestones` (4), `success_criteria` (6), `action_steps` (6 — each with its responsible division/owner, timeline, evidence_artifacts, responsibilities, resources, substeps), `feedback_channels` (7 — stakeholder/owner_division/timing/instrument), `measurement_views` (the 3 fixed keys → channel index or gap), `bilingual_views` (the 2 fixed keys → channel index or gap), `review_events` (4), `communications` (7), `revision_rules` (4), `accreditation_standards` (6 — code + rationale), `responsibilities`.
- `decision_requests` is empty (0) and `phases`/top-level `evidence_artifacts`/`guiding_clauses` are empty arrays — represent these honestly (e.g. omit or show "none this cycle"); do NOT fabricate entries.
- `flags` (25) are advisory NER spans — you may show them in a collapsed/aside "advisory" area or omit them from the main plan view; they are not plan content.

TITLE/HEADER: make the page title and a small header line clearly identify this as the T6 run draft — e.g. "Chiway IHS SLO Plan — T6 Run Draft (2026-06-17, tightened corpus)" — so it is distinguishable in the comparison sequence alongside `canonical-chiway-first-draft.html`. Keep wording factual.

FIDELITY RULES: every text value rendered must come verbatim (or faithfully formatted) from the draft JSON; introduce no plan content not in the draft; preserve catalogue labels verbatim (no paraphrasing of division/SLO/standard labels). Where the draft references channels/milestones by index (measurement_views, bilingual_views, review_event inputs), resolve the index to the referenced item's label so the render is readable.

After authoring: open-check it parses as HTML (it is static; `python3 -c "import html.parser,sys; html.parser.HTMLParser().feed(open('web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html').read())"` or similar sanity check), then commit on the branch staging ONLY that new file: `git add web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html && git commit -m "..."` (plain message). Do not stage anything else.

Return: confirmation the file matches the precedent structure, a list of which draft sections rendered (with counts), anything in the draft you could NOT cleanly render (and how you represented it), and the commit SHA.

ADVERSARIAL fidelity audit, READ-ONLY. A new HTML render was just authored (commit 357e6bd on branch t6-run): `web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html`, rendering the T6 draft `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` in the maximalist style of the precedent `web/canonical-chiway-first-draft.html`. Try to FALSIFY that it is a faithful, self-contained, non-fabricated render. End with `Total findings: N`. FAIL a criterion on substantiated doubt.

F1. **Self-contained / opens standalone.** Confirm the file has no external module imports or network/CDN dependencies that would break `file://` (grep for `import `, `<script src=`, `type="module"`, external `href=`/`src=` to remote URLs). It must render offline. A remote/module dependency = finding.

F2. **Embedded data == the draft, byte-faithful.** The render embeds the draft JSON (the agent claims as `const DRAFT`, byte-equal). Extract the embedded JSON from the HTML and compare it to `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` (e.g. parse both and deep-compare, or normalize+diff). Any divergence (edited/trimmed/hand-transcribed values) = finding.

F3. **No fabricated plan content.** Read the HTML's rendered markup/template. Every plan value displayed must come from the DRAFT data (rendered at runtime from `const DRAFT`, or static text that is purely structural/labels). import json, re
html = open('web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html').read()
# find 'const DRAFT = ' and the JSON object after it
idx = html.find('const DRAFT = ')
print("const DRAFT at byte", idx)
start = html.find('{', idx)
# brace-match to find end of JSON object
depth=0; instr=False; esc=False; end=None
for i in range(start, len(html)):
    c=html[i]
    if esc: esc=False; continue
    if c=='\\': esc=True; continue
    if c=='"': instr=not instr; continue
    if instr: continue
    if c=='{': depth+=1
    elif c=='}':
        depth-=1
        if depth==0: end=i+1; break
embedded = html[start:end]

print("src top keys:", sorted(src.keys()))

import re, json
html = open('/Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html').read()
d = json.load(open('/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json'))
# Take the render() return template + all template literals; find literal text segments (outside ${...})
m = re.search(r'<script>(.*)</script>', html, re.S)
body = m.group(1)
# crude: pull all backtick template-literal contents
tmpls = re.findall(r'`([^`]*)`', body)
# For each, strip ${...} interpolations, strip html tags, look for sentence-like plan content
import html as H
suspicious=[]
for t in tmpls:
    static = re.sub(r'\$\{[^}]*\}', '', t)   # remove interpolations (note: nested braces rare here)
    text = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', ' ', static)
    text = H.unescape(text)
    text = re.sub(r'\s+',' ',text).strip()
    # words that would indicate plan content vs labels
    if len(text)>60:
        suspicious.append(text)
for s in suspicious:
    print("LITERAL:", s[:300])
PY

input.prompt:
READ-ONLY evaluation. Project: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan. Judge the quality of the T6 workshop draft against two targets — the wizard-v2 specimen and the WASC Sample — scored on this project's ESTABLISHED quality framework (not generic quality). Change nothing; produce a structured comparative verdict.

THE DRAFT TO JUDGE:
- `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` (the assembled whole-plan draft from the T6 14-spec run, seed "Improve SLO usage across the school"). For a readable rendering, also open `web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html` (the same draft, maximalist canonical view).

TARGET 1 — the wizard-v2 specimen (what good LLM output should look like):
- `web/wizard-v2.html` + `web/static/js/wizard-v2/*` — a STATIC prototype that hard-codes the target LLM output under the audit-gap corrections + US-DRAFT capabilities. It is the visual specimen the workshop should converge toward.

TARGET 2 — the WASC Sample (the accreditation-format authority):
- Locate it: check `sources/` and `sources/extracted/` for the ACS WASC sample/action-plan source; the audit doc `docs/wizard-v2-coverage-and-prompt-quality-audit-2026-05-28.md` references it; `web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v3-wasc.html` embodies the WASC Sample shape (the 6-column grid + the per-Goal header layer). Use the actual Sample source if present, else the v3-wasc embodiment, and say which you used.

THE ESTABLISHED QUALITY FRAMEWORK (judge against THESE, read them):
- `docs/wizard-v2-coverage-and-prompt-quality-audit-2026-05-28.md` — the DISC-26 audit: the 11 named gaps + the 6 quality axes the corpus was built to close.
- The `.context` research items R-0007 and R-0008 (read via `pi-context read-block-item --block research --id R-0007` and `--id R-0008`, or `filter-block-items`): R-0007 = AUTHORITATIVE reviewer feedback (theme-framed single plan; concrete steps with HOW + frequency; diverse focus across divisions; MEASUREMENT TOOLS = KEY; KPI is an explicit project EXCLUSION — do not reward or penalize on KPIs); R-0008 = AUTHORITATIVE ACS WASC sources (the 6-column grid is official + a mandatory per-Goal HEADER layer: growth-targets / data / SLO-linkage / gap-closure / 3–5yr horizon / annual-review). Also R-0009 (LIVING heuristics: in+out-of-class, always-bilingual, feedback channels, measure→data→revise) if present.

JUDGE — produce a structured verdict:
1. Against the WASC Sample (format/accreditation-fitness): does the draft carry the Sample's structural elements — the 6-column action-plan grid (the action_steps with owner/timeline/resources/evidence/responsibilities), the per-Goal HEADER layer (growth-targets, baseline data, SLO-linkage, gap-closure, 3–5yr horizon, annual-review cadence), standards citations, a coherent goal→milestones→criteria→actions→measurement→review arc? Score each Sample element: PRESENT / PARTIAL / ABSENT, with draft evidence.
2. Against the wizard-v2 target (substantiveness/quality): does the draft match wizard-v2's demonstrated substance — concrete actions with HOW + frequency (not vague), distributed ownership, three-view measurement, bilingual strand, current→desired trajectory coherence, zero-hedging voice, measurement TOOLS named? For each, judge whether the draft meets / approaches / falls short of the wizard-v2 specimen, with specifics from both.
3. Across the DISC-26 11 gaps: for each gap the corpus was built to close, is it closed in THIS draft? (e.g. whole-draft composer coherence, orchestration framing, WASC-evidence orientation, general success criteria, zero-hedging, operational policies, current→desired meta-framing, the others named in the audit). PRESENT/PARTIAL/ABSENT per gap.
4. The honest gap list: where is the draft still short of promotion-ready vs these targets — the highest-value shortfalls (e.g. the missing maximalist→concise distillation that R-0005 names; the C2/A4 standard-consistency nit; any thinness vs wizard-v2's substance; the per-Goal header layer if absent). Be specific and cite the draft.
5. Bottom-line verdict: how close is this draft to the wizard-v2 target and to WASC-Sample promotion-readiness — and what concretely separates it from each. Honor the KPI exclusion (do not judge on KPIs). Do not reward verbosity; the Sample/target value concise high-signal content.

Return the structured verdict (per-element/per-axis PRESENT/PARTIAL/ABSENT with draft evidence), the highest-value shortfalls, and the bottom-line. Note which WASC-Sample source you used. Propose no fixes — this is judgment.

Project: wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan (path /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan). Change nothing.

QUESTION: How were the prior "distilled" plan renders actually produced — the maximalist→concise distillation transform (R-0005) that turned a maximalist whole-plan draft into the concise WASC-Sample-shape HTML? I need the exact METHOD so it can be replicated for a new draft.

The artifacts to trace (web/):
- `chiway-slo-plan-distilled-POC.html`
- `chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v3-wasc.html`
- `chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v4-enriched.html`
- `chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v5.html`
(and possibly `canonical-chiway-first-draft.html` as the maximalist input.)

Determine, with evidence (session ids, timestamps, message excerpts, file_history / git_log rows):
1. WHO/WHAT produced each distilled render — was it (a) a dispatched sub-agent (Task/Agent tool) given a distillation directive, (b) the main orchestrator hand-authoring the HTML directly, or (c) a script? Use `file_history`/`list_files` for those paths to find the creating sessions, then `query_messages`/`search_messages` on those sessions to see the actual process.

3. THE PROCESS / DIRECTIVE — if an agent was dispatched, what was the prompt/directive (the distillation instructions — did it cite R-0005, the coverage floors §5b, verbatim labels, the extrapolation license §5c, the Sample shape as target)? If hand-authored, what guidance/spec the author followed. Quote the operative instruction text if findable.
4. The SEQUENCE across v3→v4→v5 — what changed between versions and why (the iteration), so the "how" includes how it was refined.
5. Whether any REUSABLE tooling was built (a distill script/spec/prompt) or whether each render was a one-off manual/agent transform.

Search hints: search_messages for "distill", "distilled", "maximalist", "concise", "canonical", "R-0005", "extrapolation license", "coverage floor", "chiway-slo-plan-distilled"; file_history for the web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-*.html paths; the relevant sessions are likely in late May / early-to-mid June 2026.

Return: a precise account of the production method (a/b/c) with evidence, the input source, the operative directive/prompt (quoted if available), the v3→v5 iteration, and whether reusable tooling exists — i.e. the concrete recipe for how to distill a draft the way it was done before.

IMPL task on git branch `distill-t6-draft` (already checked out) in /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan. Produce ONE new distilled HTML render of the T6 whole-plan draft, executing the established distillation recipe (R-0013). STOP and report if anything is ambiguous; do not invent plan content beyond the bounded extrapolation license below.

NEW FILE (no overwrite — confirm it does not exist first): `web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v6-t6.html` (continues the distilled-vN sequence; v5 is the prior).

THE RECIPE (R-0013 — read it for full detail: `pi-context read-block-item --block research --id R-0013 --cwd .`):

INPUT (the maximalist source): `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json`. If absent, extract the byte-identical embedded copy `const DRAFT = {...}` from `web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html` (the fidelity-audited verbatim copy). Confirm which you used.

TARGET SHAPE: fill `web/plan-template-blank.html` AS-IS — its exact field set, order, and `.enriched` classes (canonical-derived). Do NOT restructure the template. House style from `web/canonical.html`; static, self-contained, opens over `file://` (no module imports). Plain plan title; NO process/AI/meta language.

CONCISION REGISTER: `data/Sample-Schoolwide-Action-Plan-2.extracted.md` — one to two sentences max per item, WITHOUT loss of signal. The maximalist prose (current_state ~217w, desired_state ~323w, step descriptions) compresses to this register; the structured arrays/coverage are preserved.

REFERENCE (the WASC structure + heuristics + extrapolation license): `docs/wasc-authoritative-sources-research-2026-06-07.md` (R-0008) and `docs/plan-generation-heuristics.md` (R-0009).

FIDELITY RULES (hard):
- VERBATIM catalogue labels — never contract or rename (e.g. keep "SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being)", "AAO - Academic Affairs Office"; do not shorten to "SAO"/"AAO"). (DEC-40)
- NO fabrication — compress/re-present/elaborate-within-catalogued-entities only.
- Numbers ONLY where a `target`-kind success_criterion in the draft carries them (no invented metrics; KPI exclusion holds).

BOUNDED EXTRAPOLATION LICENSE (R-0009): you MAY elaborate/restructure WITHIN the school's catalogued entities + their real responsibilities/SLOs to satisfy the coverage floors — this is elaboration, NOT fabrication.

COVERAGE FLOORS (the verify clause — the distilled output MUST satisfy all; a render below any floor is wrong, re-do):
- SAO owns >=1 EXPLICIT out-of-class step (the T6 draft already assigns SAO an out-of-class step — preserve it, visibly).
- >=1 action carries BILINGUAL dual-purpose framing (serving both an SLO and Chinese-English development) — the T6 draft's bilingual content is measurement-only; under the extrapolation license, surface bilingual dual-purpose in >=1 action, grounded in catalogued entities.
- >2 distinct owning divisions, NONE overloaded (no single division/role leads all or most) — the T6 draft has 5 owners / 33% max; preserve the distribution.
- IN-class AND OUT-of-class work both visible.
- measure -> data -> revise present (the review/revision loop).
- Every division/position/stakeholder with SUBSTANTIVE source involvement stays visibly present (not silently dropped by compression).

PROCEDURE: read R-0013 + R-0008 + R-0009 + the template + the Sample-2 extract + the draft; author the render; then run the verify clause yourself — confirm the HTML parses (e.g. a static HTML parse check) and count: distinct owners (>2, none >50%), SAO out-of-class step present, >=1 bilingual dual-purpose action, in+out-of-class both present, the review->revision loop present, no catalogue label contracted. Commit on the branch staging ONLY the new file: `git add web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v6-t6.html && git commit -m "..."` (plain message). Do NOT `git add -A`.

Return: which input source you used, the verify-clause results (the floor counts + parse check), what you extrapolated (and the catalogued basis for each), the commit SHA, and `git status --short`.

input.content:
Canonical lens WASC-facing view · single plan · read-only Switch to Working lens → Index

Operationalizing the Schoolwide Learner Outcomes Across Every Division

Chiway Repton School Xiamen — Schoolwide Action Plan — Cycle 2025–2026

Theme Turn the seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes from posted display items into the documented, division-owned vocabulary of curriculum, pastoral care, extracurricular work, and school-home communication across IG1 through A2.
Standards Targeted A1: Vision and MissionThe mission and vision actively guide all aspects of the school and are collectively understood and implemented. A4: Continuous ImprovementQualitative and quantitative data support ongoing improvement planning and drive initiatives that improve student outcomes. B1: CurriculumA rigorous, relevant, research-based and standards-aligned curriculum that responds to student needs and prepares them for future success. B2: Teaching and LearningStudent-centered instruction grounded in research-based strategies ensuring engagement, critical thinking, and student agency. B3: Assessment and Data AnalysisVaried, valid assessments that measure student progress and guide instructional adjustments. C1: School CultureA collaborative culture based on trust, respect, and shared responsibility supporting academic and social-emotional growth. C2: Systems of SupportCoordinated systems of academic and personal support for every student.
Rationale The seven SLOs derive from the school's mission and vision clauses, yet curriculum, lesson design, assessment, and pastoral programming do not systematically reference them, leaving the school unable to demonstrate that its proclaimed outcomes shape daily practice. Making the SLOs operational across every division is the bridge between the guiding statements (A1) and the curriculum (B1), instruction (B2), assessment (B3), and culture (C1) the standards require.
SWLOs Addressed Bilingual Communicators Compassionate Confident Engaged Innovative Reflective Responsible
Area of Mission Addressed A coherent developmental framework in which the SLOs become the shared vocabulary connecting classroom instruction, pastoral care, extracurricular programming, and family communication.
Success criteria
  1. The Curriculum and Teaching division publishes SLO-alignment templates, and English, Math, Sciences, Humanities, Art Department, Business and Economics, and Music and Sports Department each file unit-SLO mapping documents mapping every unit to at least two of the seven SLOs.
  2. The IT division deploys an SLO-tracking dashboard in the school information system showing coverage by outcome, year group IG1 through A2, and division, with access for the Director of IT and all division heads.
  3. Students name ≥6 of 7 SLOs and connect at least one to current coursework when surveyed by the AAO - Academic Affairs Office (baseline 0).
  4. The IHS Principal and IHS Assistant complete a termly SLO review cycle, confirming by rubric that each division holds documented SLO-linked evidence for its defined contribution.
  5. The Recruitment and Admissions division publishes admissions materials including all seven SLOs, and the Parent Committee issues a termly SLO-progress summary alongside academic reports.
  6. The AAO - Academic Affairs Office integrates SLO-reflection prompts into examination cycles, and the SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) maintains Homeroom Teacher, Year Leader, and Dormitory Teacher pastoral records that explicitly reference Compassionate, Confident, and Responsible.
Measurement Tools Termly student SLO self-assessment survey (AAO - Academic Affairs Office); SLO-tracking dashboard coverage report (IT); staff SLO-practice classroom observation log (Curriculum and Teaching); parent SLO-awareness survey (Principal's Office (PO)); staff English-usage self-report survey and student bilingual-progress vocabulary-and-skeleton assessment (English); SLO-framework adoption audit (Principal's Office (PO)).
Priority HIGH The posted-vs-practiced SLO gap spans A1, B1, B2, B3, and C1 at once — curriculum, instruction, assessment, culture, and family communication — and no baseline SLO-attainment data currently exists.
Student impact framing Student-focused Students in IG1 through A2 meet the same seven outcomes named in subject rubrics, Homeroom Teacher conversations, Dormitory Teacher evening programming, College Counselor meetings, and parent conferences, building the vocabulary to describe and self-assess their own growth across settings.
Area(s) for Improvement Curriculum and Learning Faculty PD and PLC School Culture Student Data and Well-being
Target Cycle Accreditation cycle 2025–2026. Framework and division mapping by Oct 2025; faculty SLO routines operational by Jan 2026; dashboard, parent, and admissions integration by Apr 2026; termly review cycle institutionalized by Jun 2026.
Departments engaged Principal's Office (PO) Curriculum and Teaching AAO - Academic Affairs Office SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) IT English Math Sciences Humanities Art Department Business and Economics Music and Sports Department College Counseling Department Future Scholar Innovation Center Library Recruitment and Admissions

Policy establishment / revision process

Recipe: Policy revision (6-step consultative) · Applies to policy: SLO Integration Framework (revising the Admissions English Assessment criteria) · Rationale: Every division maps and owns its SLO contribution before the framework binds it, so the framework is drafted, consulted, revised, and ratified across the school rather than imposed.

  1. The IHS Principal and IHS Assistant draft three SLO-framework options anchored to the six targeted standards, and the convened admin body maps each division's touchpoints, identifies gaps in writing, and selects one option.
  2. Curriculum and Teaching, with each teaching department, drafts the SLO-alignment templates — unit-SLO mappings, unit-opening SLO statements, and unit-close student self-assessment prompts.
  3. The draft circulates to pastoral roles, the AAO - Academic Affairs Office examination team, College Counseling, the Future Scholar Innovation Center, the Library, and Recruitment and Admissions, who annotate it for their fit and rewrite sample records within ten school days.
  4. Curriculum and Teaching sorts every feedback item as adopt / revise / decline with a recorded disposition, and the IHS Principal confirms the revised pre-publication framework.
  5. The framework is locked and published; IT builds the SLO-tracking dashboard against it and grants access to all division heads.
  6. The AAO - Academic Affairs Office runs a division-by-division embedding sprint, and the Principal's Office (PO) signs off on the sprint-close status report.

Evaluation cadence going forward: termly SLO review cycle led by the Principal's Office (PO) against A1, B1, B2, B3, and C1 (A4: Continuous Improvement), fed by the SLO-tracking dashboard; under-coverage or missing evidence triggers a targeted coaching cycle, extended dashboard access, or a mandatory division evidence-submission deadline.

Action Steps Department / Person(s) Responsible Timeline Required Resources How will we address this action step? Assessing of Progress Communication of Progress
1. Draft three SLO-framework options anchored to the six targeted standards, then convene the admin body to map each division's SLO touchpoints, record gaps in writing, and select one framework. Lead: Principal's Office (PO) — IHS Principal, IHS Assistant
With: Director of AAO; Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center; all Heads of Department; Director of Student Affairs Office; College Counseling Department Coordinator; Director of IT; Librarian; Music Teacher; Recruitment and Admissions 		18 Aug – 26 Sep 2025 Half-day admin meeting (~4 hrs) for all named attendees; current-state gap analysis and the seven-SLO catalogue. Admin meeting minutes on file recording the selected option, the rationale, and each attendee's written gap analysis. SLO framework launch briefing to staff and faculty (Principal's Office (PO)).
2. In a teaching-department workshop, map sample units to at least two SLOs, write unit-opening SLO statements, and draft unit-close self-assessment prompts, compiling the result into an initial-draft SLO framework. The English department's mapping does double duty: each English unit advances both the Bilingual Communicators SLO and the school's Chinese-English language development, tracked through the staff English-usage and student bilingual-progress assessments. Lead: Curriculum and Teaching — Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, PD Leader
With: Head of English, Head of Math, Head of Sciences, Head of Humanities, Head of Art Department, Head of Business and Economics, Music Teacher, PE Teacher 		29 Sep – 17 Oct 2025 Full-day working session (~6 hrs); one sample unit plan per teaching department. Initial-draft SLO-framework document on file with SLO-alignment templates and sample mappings from every named department. SLO-practice termly bulletin to staff and faculty (Curriculum and Teaching).
3. Distribute the draft framework for structured review across pastoral and examination roles: each Homeroom Teacher rewrites a pastoral check-in to name Compassionate, Confident, or Responsible; each Year Leader re-frames a year-group pastoral activity; each Dormitory Teacher drafts an evening-programming session embedding a pastoral SLO; the AAO examination team, College Counseling, Future Scholar Innovation Center, Library, and Recruitment and Admissions annotate for their fit. Lead: SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) — Director of Student Affairs Office
With: Homeroom Teachers; IG1, IG2, AS, A2 Year Leaders; Dormitory Teachers; Assistant to Student Affairs; AAO - Academic Affairs Office examination team; College Counseling Department; Future Scholar Innovation Center; Library; Recruitment and Admissions 		20 Oct – 7 Nov 2025 Ten school days for stakeholder review and annotation; the initial-draft framework and structured review protocol. Collected feedback package on file with annotated reviews and rewritten pastoral records from every named role, plus examination-cycle feasibility annotations. SLO orientation and self-assessment guide to students (SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being)).
4. Sort every feedback item from step 3 as adopt-as-written, revise-with-changes, or decline-with-reason, record a disposition for each, and produce the locked pre-publication framework with the IHS Principal's confirmation. Lead: Curriculum and Teaching — Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, PD Leader
With: IHS Principal 		10 – 28 Nov 2025 Two full working days for sorting and revision, plus a two-hour confirmation review; the collected step-3 feedback. Revised pre-publication framework on file with a recorded disposition for every feedback item. SLO-practice termly bulletin to staff and faculty (Curriculum and Teaching).
5. Build the SLO-tracking dashboard in the school information system from the locked framework, showing coverage by outcome, year group IG1 through A2, and division, and confirm access for every named role. Lead: IT — Director of IT
With: IHS Principal, IHS Assistant 		1 – 19 Dec 2025 ~5 working days for dashboard build and testing; school information system with dashboard-building capability. Dashboard operational; sample coverage query returns results for all views; access confirmed for every named role. SLO dashboard monthly digest to administration and leadership (IT).
6. Run a two-week embedding sprint: issue a division-by-division embedding checklist from the published framework, embed SLO-reflection prompts into examination cycles, and confirm every division marks its items complete with IHS Principal sign-off. Lead: AAO - Academic Affairs Office — Director of AAO
With: Academic Affairs Officer; Exam Officer; CIE Exam Officer; Edexcel Exam Officer; AP Coordinator; all divisions 		6 – 21 Jan 2026 Two-week sprint window for all divisions; the published SLO framework and operational dashboard. Completed embedding checklist on file with all divisions marked complete; IHS Principal sign-off on the sprint-close status report. Termly SLO-progress summary and Parent Committee SLO attainment report presentation to parents; admissions SLO introductory materials (Recruitment and Admissions).
Review and revision cycle (measure → data → revise)
Framework adoption audit
18 Nov 2025 · Principal's Office (PO)		 Confirms the framework exists and ≥5 of 7 teaching departments have filed unit-SLO mappings; classroom observation logs give early data on SLO naming. If fewer than five comply, Curriculum and Teaching reconvenes the gap departments to file mappings within ten school days.
Faculty embedding checkpoint
30 Jan 2026 · Curriculum and Teaching		 Observation logs cover a full unit cycle across departments; the first student SLO self-assessment survey sets the baseline. If fewer than 60% of lessons name SLOs at unit start, the PD Leader runs a targeted coaching cycle — observe, co-plan, re-observe within four weeks.
Dashboard and communications mid-cycle review
22 Apr 2026 · IT		 The dashboard coverage report and parent SLO-awareness survey provide a term of data against the baseline. If any year group draws from fewer than four departments, IT extends data-entry access and adds a completeness indicator; if parent awareness lags, the Principal's Office (PO) schedules an SLO information evening co-hosted with the Parent Committee.
End-of-cycle institutionalization review
Jun 2026 · Principal's Office (PO)		 All four termly channels give a full-cycle dataset; the IHS Principal and IHS Assistant examine division-level evidence against A1, B1, B2, B3, and C1 to confirm the review cycle is institutionalized. Any division lacking documented SLO evidence is given a mandatory submission deadline in the next cycle, and its SLO role is revised if the gap revealed a structural mismatch.
Chiway Repton School Xiamen · Schoolwide Action Plan v1 · Cycle 2025–2026
