input.description: Adversarial audit of the v6 distillation
input.prompt:
ADVERSARIAL audit, READ-ONLY. A distilled render was just authored (commit b99409d on branch distill-t6-draft): `web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v6-t6.html` — a concise WASC-shape distillation of the T6 maximalist draft, produced per the R-0013 recipe. Try to FALSIFY that it is faithful, non-fabricated, coverage-floor-complete, and self-contained. End with `Total findings: N`. FAIL a criterion on substantiated doubt.

ARTIFACTS:
- The distilled render: `web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v6-t6.html`.
- The maximalist SOURCE: `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` (or the byte-identical `const DRAFT` embedded in `web/canonical-chiway-draft-2026-06-17-t6.html`).
- The recipe + constraints: R-0013 (`pi-context read-block-item --block research --id R-0013 --cwd .`); the extrapolation license in `docs/plan-generation-heuristics.md` (R-0009); the target template `web/plan-template-blank.html`; the concision exemplar `data/Sample-Schoolwide-Action-Plan-2.extracted.md`.

VERIFY each against the actual files:

D1. **Self-contained / opens standalone.** No module imports / remote `src`/`href` / `type="module"`. Renders over file://. Finding if any external/network dependency.

D2. **No fabrication — every fact traces to the source draft.** Read the distilled content and confirm each plan claim (milestones, criteria, action steps, owners, standards, channels, review events, rationales) is present in or faithfully derived from the SOURCE draft. Any plan content with NO basis in the source = a fabrication finding. The ONLY permitted additions are (a) compression/re-presentation and (b) the bounded R-0009 extrapolation — elaboration WITHIN catalogued entities + their real responsibilities/SLOs. Scrutinize the claimed bilingual dual-purpose extrapolation (step 2): is it grounded in catalogued entities (the English department + its two English-owned bilingual channels + the Bilingual Communicators SLO rationale in the source's domain_alignment), or does it invent programs/facts not in the source? Finding if it fabricates.

D3. **Verbatim catalogue labels (DEC-40).** No catalogue label contracted/renamed — division/SLO/standard labels appear verbatim as in the source (e.g. "SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being)", "AAO - Academic Affairs Office", not "SAO"/"AAO"). Finding for any contracted catalogue label (distinguish genuine catalogue labels from the draft's own verbatim position titles like "Director of AAO").

D4. **Numbers only where a `target` criterion carries them.** The only metric should be the source's target-kind success criterion (students name >=6 of 7 SLOs, baseline 0). Any other invented number/metric/KPI = a finding.

D5. **Coverage floors satisfied (the anti-drop check — the core distillation risk).** Independently confirm against the SOURCE: (a) >2 distinct owning divisions, none owning >50% of steps; (b) SAO owns >=1 EXPLICIT out-of-class step; (c) >=1 action carries bilingual dual-purpose; (d) in-class AND out-of-class both visible; (e) measure->data->revise loop present; (f) every division/position/stakeholder with SUBSTANTIVE involvement in the SOURCE remains visibly present in the distillation (compare the source's owner/stakeholder set to the render — a substantive party silently dropped by compression = a finding). This is exactly the R-0005 §5b coverage-floor concern; be rigorous.

D6. **Concision register honored without signal loss.** Items are ~1-2 sentences (per the Sample-2 register), not maximalist paragraphs, AND no load-bearing content was lost in the compression (coverage floors in D5 are the hard check; also spot-check that each milestone/criterion/step retained its substantive meaning).

D7. **No overwrite / correct file.** `git show b99409d --stat` shows only `web/chiway-slo-plan-distilled-v6-t6.html` added; no other file (esp. the v5/prior renders or the source) modified.

For each: PASS/FAIL with concrete evidence (source vs render comparisons, grep results, the owner/stakeholder set diff). The highest-value check is D2 (fabrication) + D5 (silent coverage drop). End with `Total findings: N`.

SKILL_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO="$(cd "$SKILL_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"
DJ="$REPO/school-improvement-plans"          # uv project root; dispatch is ../prompt-workshop from here
: "${DATABASE_URL:=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans}"
export DATABASE_URL

cd "$DJ"

echo "== 1/2 render-parity (snippet == .workshopping substrate, all 14 specs) =="
uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py   # prints GATE: 14/14, exit 0 on pass

echo "== 2/2 single-spec render (boots Django + grounding + Django template) =="
uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/render_from_substrate.py --spec-key narrative-draft \
  | python3 -c 'import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); p=d.get("rendered_prompt") or d.get("prompt",""); assert d.get("spec_key")=="narrative-draft" and len(p)>1000, "render too short / wrong spec"; print(f"render OK: {d[\"spec_key\"]} -> {len(p)} chars")'

echo "SMOKE OK"

input.content:
---
name: run-prompt-workshop
description: Run, launch, build, smoke-test, or verify the prompt-workshop — the 14-spec LLM plan-generation pipeline (run_workflow.mjs / render_from_substrate / check_draft / verify-render-parity) that drives the school-improvement-plan prompt corpus against the dev DB. Use to run the workshop sequence, render a spec, smoke-test the pipeline, or check render-parity.
---

# Run the prompt-workshop pipeline

The prompt-workshop is the dev-time generation pipeline for the school-improvement-plan LLM prompt corpus (DEC-41: same code as production, different orchestration). It is **not** a GUI/server — it is a CLI/dispatch pipeline: Python scripts in `prompt-workshop/dispatch/` that import the production Django (`ai/services/grounding.py`, `planner/specs.py`) to render the 14 prompt specs against the dev DB and run them through an LLM. You drive it from the command line.

Two surfaces:
- **Smoke (fast, backend-free, ~30s)** — `.claude/skills/run-prompt-workshop/smoke.sh`: boots Django against the dev DB, renders the corpus, asserts snippet↔`.workshopping`-substrate render-parity 14/14. No LLM call. This is the quick "is the pipeline alive" check.
- **Full run (the real thing, ~5–8 min, needs the LLM backend)** — `run_workflow.mjs` runs all 14 specs through the LLM into an assembled whole-plan draft, then `check_draft.py --strict` is the structural gate. This is the actual generation.

Paths below are relative to the repo root (`<unit>`). The full run is invoked from `school-improvement-plans/` (so `uv` finds the venv and the `../prompt-workshop` relative paths resolve).

## Prerequisites (environment, already provisioned here — macOS/darwin)

- **Dev Postgres up on `localhost:5433`** (tenant `chiway-repton-xiamen`) — the pipeline reads live grounding from it. Started via the project's docker-compose (see `school-improvement-plans/docker-compose*.yml`).
- **`uv`** (provides the venv python — the dispatch command steps need a real `python`/`node` on PATH, not a bare shell; always invoke under `uv run`, see Gotchas WF-12).
- **`node`** (for `run_workflow.mjs`).
- **`pi` on PATH + a reachable model backend** — only for the **full run**. `pi --mode json` is spawned per spec; the model is `~/.pi/agent/settings.json`'s default (deepseek-v4-pro at the last clean runs). The smoke path needs none of this.

`DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans"` is exported by `smoke.sh`; export it yourself for the full-run commands below.

## Smoke (agent path — run this first)

```bash
bash .claude/skills/run-prompt-workshop/smoke.sh
```

Verified output ends with `GATE: 14/14`, `render OK: narrative-draft -> 67412 chars`, `SMOKE OK`, exit 0. Nonzero = the pipeline doesn't launch / parity broke / a render is empty. Individual pieces, if you want them directly (run from `school-improvement-plans/`):

```bash
# render-parity (snippet == .workshopping substrate, all 14 specs) — backend-free
DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" \
  uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py      # -> GATE: 14/14, exit 0

# render one spec to its fully-grounded prompt — backend-free
DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" \
  uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/render_from_substrate.py --spec-key narrative-draft   # -> JSON {spec_key, rendered_prompt, ...}
```

## Full run (the actual 14-spec generation — needs the LLM backend, ~5–8 min)

From `school-improvement-plans/`. Use a fixed `OUT_DIR` so you can find the draft afterward. Run it in the background (it is long).

```bash
OUT_DIR=/tmp/workshop-out && rm -rf "$OUT_DIR" && mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" \
  uv run node ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/run_workflow.mjs run workshop-run-plan-sequence \
  --input "{\"seed\":\"Improve SLO usage across the school\",\"out_dir\":\"$OUT_DIR\"}" \
  --yes --fresh > /tmp/wf-run.log 2>&1
```

Success = all 42 steps (14 specs × render→call→apply) `completed`, exit 0; the final assembled draft is `$OUT_DIR/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` (one `draft-after-<spec>.json` per spec is the live progress signal while it runs). Then the **structural gate**:

```bash
DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" \
  uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/check_draft.py --strict \
  --draft "$OUT_DIR/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json"               # -> RESULT: PASS, exit 0
```

`--strict` gates on structural checks 3–8 (owner distribution >1/none>50%, coverage, review cadence, three-view, bilingual, decision-request well-formedness); language checks 1–2 are WARN-only (DEC-53). Without `--strict` it is report-only (exit 0 regardless) — a footgun. The verified procedure + the as-practiced run history live in `prompt-workshop/RUNBOOK-T6-14spec-run.md`.

## Gotchas (battle scars)

- **`--fresh` is mandatory** (WF-14): `run` auto-resumes an incomplete prior run by default, replaying stale completed render steps — so a fixed render-source change looks unfixed. Always `--fresh`.
- **`--yes` is mandatory** in a non-TTY shell — `run` is gated and exits 1 without it.
- **Invoke under `uv run`** (WF-12): a `command` step spawns via a non-interactive `sh -c` with no venv/alias; a bare `python`/`node` fails `exit 127`. `uv run node …` puts `.venv/bin/python` on PATH.
- **pi 10MB stdout cap** (WF-13/15/16): `pi --mode json` can emit tens of MB for the heaviest-prompt specs (responsibilities ~75KB, decompose); past the 10MB cap the tail (incl. the agent's output) is dropped. Mitigated wasc-side: each call step declares `output.schema` (disk-read of `<runDir>/outputs/<step>.json`) + a `retry` block. A flaky no-write aborts that spec; re-run with `--fresh`.
- **The full run needs the deepseek backend reachable** (network + `~/.pi/agent/auth.json`); the model is the `settings.json` default — confirm it before a run. The smoke path does not.
- **`render_from_substrate.py` prints a large JSON to stdout** — if you pipe it to a consumer that crashes/exits early, the renderer dies with `BrokenPipeError`. Consume the whole stream (read to EOF) — see `smoke.sh` step 2 for the working pattern.
- **`.workshopping` substrate edits are NOT via `--cwd`** (CTX-12): the pi-context CLI resolves a substrate by its bootstrap pointer; editing `.workshopping` requires a transient `pi-context context-switch` of the root pointer and back. Irrelevant to *running*; relevant if you change the corpus. `.pi-context.json` must end on `.context`.

## Troubleshooting

- `exit 127: python: command not found` (or `node`) → you ran a dispatch step outside `uv run`. Prefix with `uv run` (WF-12).
- `BrokenPipeError` from `render_from_substrate.py` → your stdout consumer exited early; read the full JSON stream.
- render-parity / render fails to connect → the dev Postgres on `:5433` isn't up; start the compose DB.
- A `call_*` step fails "no valid JSON output was produced" mid-run → a truncated/flaky LLM turn (WF-15/16); re-run with `--fresh`.

## The driver

`.claude/skills/run-prompt-workshop/smoke.sh` — the committed fast smoke (parity + single render, backend-free). The full-run drivers are the in-repo dispatch scripts `prompt-workshop/dispatch/{run_workflow.mjs, check_draft.py, render_from_substrate.py, verify-render-parity.py}`; this SKILL.md is their man page. Verified in this container (macOS): `smoke.sh` → `GATE: 14/14` + `render OK … 67412 chars` + `SMOKE OK` exit 0; the full run + `check_draft --strict` PASS per the T6 run (`RUNBOOK-T6-14spec-run.md`).

SELECT te.command, te.tool_name, s.session_id, te.timestamp FROM tool_executions te JOIN sessions s ON te.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND (te.command LIKE '%runserver%' OR te.command LIKE '%manage.py%') ORDER BY te.timestamp DESC LIMIT 80

SELECT DISTINCT te.command, s.session_id, te.timestamp FROM tool_executions te JOIN sessions s ON te.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND (te.command LIKE '%docker compose%' OR te.command LIKE '%docker-compose%' OR te.command LIKE '%5433%' OR te.command LIKE '%DATABASE_URL%') ORDER BY te.timestamp DESC LIMIT 80
SELECT name FROM pragma_table_info('tool_executions')
SELECT name FROM pragma_table_info('sessions')
SELECT name FROM pragma_table_info('messages')

SELECT te.tool_name, json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') AS cmd, m.session_id, m.timestamp, te.is_error FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON te.message_uuid = m.uuid JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND (json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%runserver%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%docker compose%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%docker-compose%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%import_staff_roster%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%createsuperuser%') ORDER BY m.timestamp DESC LIMIT 100

SELECT DISTINCT substr(json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command'),1,300) AS cmd, count(*) AS n FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON te.message_uuid = m.uuid JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%runserver%' GROUP BY cmd ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 40

SELECT DISTINCT substr(json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command'),1,400) AS cmd, count(*) AS n FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON te.message_uuid = m.uuid JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND (json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%/mocks/%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%chromium%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%screenshot%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%curl%localhost:8008%') GROUP BY cmd ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 40

SELECT DISTINCT substr(json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command'),1,400) AS cmd, count(*) AS n FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON te.message_uuid = m.uuid JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND (json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%docker compose%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%docker-compose%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%migrate%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%import_staff_roster%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%createsuperuser%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%pg_isready%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%loaddata%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%pg_dump%') GROUP BY cmd ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 60

SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') AS cmd, m.timestamp, m.session_id FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON te.message_uuid = m.uuid JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%capture.sh%' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%CHROME%headless%' ORDER BY length(json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command')) DESC LIMIT 1

READ-ONLY forensic audit via `claude-history` (the `claude-history` CLI on PATH and/or the claude-history MCP tools — list_sessions, query_messages, search_messages, list_files, file_history, git_log, tool_executions / execute_sql, run_query). Project: wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan (path /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan). Change nothing; report only.

DIRECTIVE (verbatim intent): determine the other CANDIDATES for run skills, and the EXACT SHAPES — from actual session history + real tool use — TO USE FOR CREATING run skills for the surfaced options.

Critical: in THIS project, "creating a run skill" inherently includes the **context-update process** the run triggers — a run that changes state is not complete until it is recorded per the project's discipline (ORCHESTRATOR-LOG event via state.mjs, pending-actions/focus refresh, the .context cascade for graph-touching ops, US-STATUS flips for vivifying runs, subagent-invocations rows for dispatched agents, and explore-then-file for anything a run surfaces — see CLAUDE.md "Orchestrator discipline" + the .context conventions). A prior pass surfaced the launch mechanics but DROPPED this dimension; that diminished the directive. So the "exact shapes for creating run skills" you return MUST include BOTH the launch/build/driver shapes AND the context-update process, each grounded in actual history + tool use. Do not augment beyond the directive (no extra recommendations/scope); do not diminish it (do not drop the context-update dimension).

One run skill already exists: `.claude/skills/run-prompt-workshop/` (the prompt-workshop dispatch pipeline). Do NOT re-propose it; surface the OTHER runnable surfaces.

For EACH surfaced run-skill candidate, return — all from actual claude-history (cite session ids / timestamps / verbatim command strings + tool_executions), not from READMEs:

A. THE UNIT + why it's a run-skill candidate, and whether it was actually run/driven in history.
B. LAUNCH/BUILD/DRIVER SHAPES (verbatim): the exact commands actually run to build/launch/drive it, the env (DSN, ports, tenant, venv/uv, env vars), the driver shape (server→curl smoke / browser-driven→headless-chrome screenshot / DB-bring-up→migrate+seed+assert / etc.), and the real gotchas hit (error→fix) — grounded in history.
C. THE CONTEXT-UPDATE PROCESS the run entails (this is the part the prior pass dropped — recover it from how runs of this surface were ACTUALLY recorded in history + the CLAUDE.md/.context discipline): for THIS candidate's run, what state-changing events does it produce, and exactly what must be recorded — which ORCHESTRATOR-LOG rows (the actual shape/fields used: type/refs/raw via state.mjs append), pending-actions/focus updates, .context cascade (task/VER/edge/complete-task/context-validate) where the run touches the graph (e.g. a vivifying run flipping US-STATUS pending→enabled, a seed/migration landing a DEC/DISC), subagent-invocations rows if the run dispatches agents, and explore-then-file for discoveries the run surfaces. Cite the actual history where such recording was done for this surface (the real LOG/STATE/cascade entries that followed past runs of it), so the run skill can encode the context-update step verbatim, not invented.

Return: the run-skill candidates, each with B (launch/driver shapes) AND C (the context-update process), all verbatim-grounded in claude-history with citations. Flag any candidate never actually run in history. No changes; report only.

SELECT te.tool_name, te.command, COUNT(*) AS n
FROM tool_executions te
JOIN messages m ON te.message_id = m.id
JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id
WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%'
  AND te.tool_name = 'Bash'
  AND (te.command LIKE '%runserver%' OR te.command LIKE '%curl%' OR te.command LIKE '%migrate%'
       OR te.command LIKE '%pytest%' OR te.command LIKE '%manage.py%' OR te.command LIKE '%run_workflow%'
       OR te.command LIKE '%screenshot%' OR te.command LIKE '%chrome%' OR te.command LIKE '%headless%'
       OR te.command LIKE '%seed%' OR te.command LIKE '%import_staff%' OR te.command LIKE '%make %'
       OR te.command LIKE '%dispatch%' OR te.command LIKE '%render%')
GROUP BY te.command
ORDER BY n DESC
LIMIT 80

GROUP BY cmd
ORDER BY n DESC

SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') AS cmd, COUNT(*) AS n, MIN(m.timestamp) AS first_t, MAX(m.timestamp) AS last_t
FROM tool_executions te
JOIN messages m ON te.message_uuid = m.uuid
JOIN sessions s ON m.session_id = s.session_id
WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%'
  AND te.tool_name = 'Bash'
  AND (json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%make test-js%'
       OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%node --test%'

input.query: SELECT COUNT(*) n_gate_chain FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON m.uuid=te.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id=m.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%manage.py check%' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%mypy%' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%pytest%' AND json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%test-js%'

SELECT 
  SUM(CASE WHEN c LIKE '%manage.py runserver 8008%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n_runserver,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c LIKE '%/mocks/%' OR c LIKE '%devmocks%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n_mocks,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c LIKE '%docker compose%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n_docker,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c LIKE '%render-phase-prompt%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n_renderphase,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c LIKE '%state.mjs%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n_statemjs,
  SUM(CASE WHEN c LIKE '%bin.js%' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) n_picontext
FROM (SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') c FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON m.uuid=te.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id=m.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND te.tool_name='Bash')

SELECT op, COUNT(*) n FROM (
  SELECT CASE 
    WHEN c LIKE '%state.mjs filter%' THEN 'filter'
    WHEN c LIKE '%state.mjs tail%' THEN 'tail'
    WHEN c LIKE '%state.mjs append%' THEN 'append'
    WHEN c LIKE '%state.mjs upsert%' THEN 'upsert'
    WHEN c LIKE '%state.mjs read%' THEN 'read'
    WHEN c LIKE '%search.mjs%' THEN 'search.mjs'
    ELSE 'other-state' END op
  FROM (SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') c FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON m.uuid=te.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id=m.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND (c LIKE '%state.mjs%' OR c LIKE '%search.mjs%'))
) GROUP BY op ORDER BY n DESC

SELECT op, COUNT(*) n FROM (
  SELECT CASE
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-validate-relations%' THEN 'context-validate-relations'
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-validate%' THEN 'context-validate'
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-current-state%' THEN 'context-current-state'
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-bootstrap-state%' THEN 'context-bootstrap-state'
    WHEN c LIKE '%read-block-item%' THEN 'read-block-item'
    WHEN c LIKE '%read-block %' OR c LIKE '%read-block-page%' THEN 'read-block'
    WHEN c LIKE '%filter-block-items%' THEN 'filter-block-items'
    WHEN c LIKE '%write-block%' THEN 'write-block'
    WHEN c LIKE '%append-block-item%' THEN 'append-block-item'
    WHEN c LIKE '%update-block-item%' OR c LIKE '%upsert-block%' THEN 'update/upsert-block'
    WHEN c LIKE '%append-edge%' OR c LIKE '%add-edge%' OR c LIKE '%relation%' THEN 'edge/relation'
    WHEN c LIKE '%read-schema%' THEN 'read-schema'
    WHEN c LIKE '%--help%' THEN 'help'
    ELSE 'other-picontext' END op
  FROM (SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') c FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON m.uuid=te.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id=m.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND c LIKE '%bin.js%')
) GROUP BY op ORDER BY n DESC

SELECT op, COUNT(*) n FROM (
  SELECT CASE
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-validate-relations%' THEN 'context-validate-relations'
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-validate%' THEN 'context-validate'
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-current-state%' THEN 'context-current-state'
    WHEN c LIKE '%context-bootstrap-state%' THEN 'context-bootstrap-state'
    WHEN c LIKE '%read-block-item%' THEN 'read-block-item'
    WHEN c LIKE '%read-block %' OR c LIKE '%read-block-page%' THEN 'read-block'
    WHEN c LIKE '%filter-block-items%' THEN 'filter-block-items'
    WHEN c LIKE '%write-block%' THEN 'write-block'
    WHEN c LIKE '%append-block-item%' THEN 'append-block-item'
    WHEN c LIKE '%read-schema%' THEN 'read-schema'
    WHEN c LIKE '%--help%' THEN 'help'
    ELSE 'other-picontext' END op
  FROM (SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') c FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON m.uuid=te.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id=m.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND c LIKE '%bin.js%')
) GROUP BY op ORDER BY n DESC

SELECT json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') c FROM tool_executions te JOIN messages m ON m.uuid=te.message_uuid JOIN sessions s ON s.session_id=m.session_id WHERE s.project_path LIKE '%wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan%' AND te.tool_name='Bash' AND (json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%GRANT %' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%ALTER DEFAULT PRIVILEGES%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%socrates%psql%' OR json_extract(te.input_json,'$.command') LIKE '%psql%socrates%') LIMIT 10

IMPL task on git branch `run-skills-context` (already checked out) in /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan. Author a run skill for the **pi-context `.context` substrate cascade ops** routine (audit candidate #2), per the /run-skill-generator definition-of-done. STOP and report on ambiguity. Do NOT git-commit and do NOT touch .gitignore (the orchestrator handles git); just write the files + verify the driver by running it in this container. CREATE (new dir): `.claude/skills/validate-context/` containing `SKILL.md` + `validate-context.sh` (the driver). WHAT THE ROUTINE IS (verbatim shapes from session-history audit — this is the material; ground the skill in it): - The query/validate/cascade surface for this repo's active-phase `.context` PM substrate, via the **`pi-context` CLI on PATH** (NOT `node …/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js` — that raw-dist form is the legacy invocation; CLAUDE.md line 21 now mandates `pi-context --cwd .`). - Read/query (no auth): `pi-context read-block-item --block --id --cwd .`, `filter-block-items --block --field --op --value --cwd .`, `read-block`/`read-block-page`, `read-schema --schemaName [--path

] --json`, `find-references --itemId --cwd .`, `context-current-state --cwd .`, `gather-execution-context --unitId --kind --cwd .`. - Validate/cascade: `pi-context context-validate --cwd .` (the cross-block referential gate; the error-count check form is `pi-context context-validate --cwd . --json 2>&1 | grep -c '"severity": "error"'`), `pi-context context-validate-relations --cwd .`. - Writes: `append-block-item --block --arrayKey --item @file --writer '{"kind":"agent","agent_id":"orchestrator"}' --cwd .`, `update-block-item --block --arrayKey --match '{"id":"…"}' --updates @file …`, `append-relation --parent --child --relation_type …`, `complete-task --taskId --verificationId …`. GOTCHAS (real, from history + this repo's memory — include them, grounded): - Use the `pi-context` PATH CLI, not `node dist/bin.js`; never wrap calls in `echo "==="` header theatre; never pipe CLI output through ad-hoc `python3 -c`/`jq` parsers — use `--json` / `read-schema --path` / the CLI's own output. - `--cwd .` (or omit) keeps it on THIS repo's `.context` substrate; a non-active sibling substrate like `.workshopping` is NOT reachable by `--cwd` — it requires a transient `pi-context context-switch` of the root pointer and back, and `.pi-context.json` must end on `.context` (finding CTX-12 in prompt-workshop/PI-WORKFLOWS-FINDINGS.md). - Write-op `--block`/`--arrayKey`: `--block` resolves by the data-file stem and writes need an explicit `--arrayKey` (e.g. `--block framework-gaps --arrayKey gaps`, `--block features` not `feature`); read config.json for each block's array_key (CTX-3/CTX-7). - **KNOWN CURRENT REGRESSION**: `context-current-state` presently returns `{"focus":"state-derivation not configured", …}` (empty) — since pi-context engine commit 99f45de config-gated derivation on a top-level `state_derivation` key absent from this project's `.context/config.json`. So the skill must NOT assert current-state returns rich state; it documents this as a known-degraded op + the fix path (`pi-context update` brings `state_derivation` from the conception + `resolve-blocked conventions`; see ORCHESTRATOR-LOG). `context-validate`/`validate-relations` are unaffected and ARE the reliable gate. - VERIFY-DON'T-NARRATE: after any `.context` write, re-run `context-validate` (+ `current-state`) and confirm the result reconstructs what you created — never assert state from memory. THE DRIVER `validate-context.sh` (author it, then RUN it to verify it works in this container): a fast, read-only smoke that exercises the canonical CLI verification — runs `pi-context context-validate --cwd .` and asserts 0 errors (warnings allowed; print the warning count), runs `pi-context context-validate-relations --cwd .`, and runs `pi-context context-current-state --cwd .` reporting its result (and explicitly noting if it is the known "state-derivation not configured" degraded output — do NOT fail the smoke on that, since it's the documented open regression). Exit 0 = the substrate validates clean (0 errors); nonzero only on a real validate error. Self-locating (resolve repo root from the script path; run from repo root). Make it executable. RUN IT and capture the output. SKILL.md (SHORT, frontmatter name: validate-context; description with the verbs an agent types — "validate context", "check the .context substrate", "query/read/write the context cascade", "run context-validate", "context-current-state"): intro (the .context substrate + the pi-context CLI), the driver as the primary path, the canonical query/validate/write commands (each a command form that works), the gotchas above, and the known current-state regression + fix. Every code block must be a command that runs (you ran the driver; the validate/read commands are real). Return: the files written (paths), the verbatim output of running `validate-context.sh`, the context-validate error+warning counts, and anything you flagged. Do NOT commit; do NOT edit .gitignore.

Author a run skill for the **decomposed-JSON context-discipline update (`state.mjs`)** routine (audit candidate #3) — the discipline of recording a state-changing action into the project's context spine. Per the /run-skill-generator definition-of-done. STOP and report on ambiguity. Do NOT git-commit and do NOT touch .gitignore (the orchestrator handles git); just write the files + verify the driver by running it in this container.

CREATE (new dir): `.claude/skills/update-context/` containing `SKILL.md` + `update-context.sh` (the driver).

WHAT THE ROUTINE IS (verbatim shapes from session-history audit — this is the material; ground the skill in it). The spine lives in `context-migration/decomposed/*.json`, operated via `node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs` (reads via `search.mjs`). The discipline: a state-changing action (a commit, a gate pass, a seed/migration, a decision settled, a gap surfaced, an agent dispatched) is NOT complete until it is recorded.
- Append an event to the log: `node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs append ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json @scratch.json` — the row shape is `{timestamp, type (the substantive description, may carry verbatim evidence: gate output / commit SHAs / counts), text, refs:[…], continuation:[], raw (one-line summary)}`.
- Upsert the focus/open work: `node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs upsert ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.pending-actions.json @scratch.json`.
- Append a dispatch: `… append ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.subagent-invocations.json @scratch.json` (shape `{timestamp, task, agents, outcome, raw}`).
- Append a discovery: `… append discoveries.json @scratch.json` (DISC).
- Session-start / verify orientation trio: `… filter ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.pending-actions.json status eq open` (focus = the item with `"priority":"next"`), `… tail ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json`, `… tail ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.subagent-invocations.json`.

GOTCHAS (real, from history + this repo's memory — include them, grounded):
- `upsert` REPLACES the whole matched element (no field-merge): to amend one field, read the whole item, change that field, write the WHOLE object back via `@scratch.json` (build it to a scratch file). A partial object silently drops the unspecified fields.
- `append`/`upsert` auto-detect the key (`id` if present, else `seq`) and PRINT `context-migration/write-policies.json` before writing — the write must satisfy those policies (no performative noise, no meta-preamble, no unwarranted certainty, signal-dense, no stale-duplication of git-derivable content).
- `pending-actions.status` is exactly `open` | `done` — never a third value, or the session-start `filter status eq open` focus query misses active work. Mark `done` when committed (add an `evidence` field); move `priority:"next"` to the new focus.
- `tail` returns the last PHYSICAL rows = newest only for append-chronological files (ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json and anything written via `append`).
- Commit messages / row bodies with shell-special chars (backticks, `$()`) go through `git commit -F /tmp/file` (a SYSTEM /tmp file), never `-m "…"`.
- VERIFY-DON'T-NARRATE: after appending/upserting, RE-RUN the orientation trio and confirm they reconstruct the reality you just created — the JSON is the source of truth, never assert state from memory. (This is the discipline's whole point: "flagging is not persistence"; the run isn't done until the spine reflects it and you've read it back.)

THE DRIVER `update-context.sh` (author it, then RUN it to verify it works in this container): a fast, READ-ONLY verify of the spine — the "verify-don't-narrate" read-back. It runs the orientation trio (`filter pending-actions status eq open`, `tail ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json`, `tail …subagent-invocations.json`), asserts each parses + returns at least one row, prints the current focus (the `priority:next` open item's seq/title) and the newest LOG event's `raw`, so an agent can confirm the spine reconstructs current reality. It MUST be read-only (no append/upsert — it verifies, it does not write). Exit 0 = spine reads back coherently; nonzero on a parse failure / empty spine. Self-locating (resolve repo root from script path). Make it executable. RUN IT and capture output.

SKILL.md (SHORT, frontmatter name: update-context; description with the verbs an agent types — "record a state change", "append the ORCHESTRATOR-LOG event", "update the context spine", "upsert pending-actions / focus", "log a subagent dispatch", "verify don't narrate"): intro (the decomposed-JSON spine + state.mjs + that a state-changing run isn't done until recorded), the driver (the read-back verify) as the primary verify path, the append/upsert command forms (each a real form), the gotchas above, and the closing rule that this routine is the mandatory closing step of every other state-changing run. Every code block must be a command that runs (you ran the driver; the trio commands are real).

Return: the files written (paths), the verbatim output of running `update-context.sh`, and anything flagged. Do NOT commit; do NOT edit .gitignore.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# update-context.sh — read-only "verify-don't-narrate" read-back of the decomposed-JSON
# context spine. Runs the session-start orientation trio against
# context-migration/decomposed/*.json (via state.mjs), asserts each parses and returns
# at least one row, and prints the current focus + newest LOG event so an agent can
# confirm the spine reconstructs current reality after a state-changing write.
#
# This driver NEVER writes. It verifies. Recording a state change is done with
# `state.mjs append|upsert` (see SKILL.md); this is the read-back that proves it landed.
#
# Exit 0  → spine reads back coherently (all three reads parse, each non-empty,
#           a priority:next focus item exists).
# Exit !=0 → a read failed to parse, a file was empty, or no focus item was found.

set -euo pipefail

# Self-locate the repo root from this script's path:
#   <repo>/.claude/skills/update-context/update-context.sh  → up 3 dirs
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"
STATE="$REPO_ROOT/context-migration/scripts/state.mjs"

if [[ ! -f "$STATE" ]]; then
  echo "FAIL: state.mjs not found at $STATE" >&2
  exit 2
fi

fail() { echo "FAIL: $1" >&2; exit 1; }

# --- 1. focus / open work --------------------------------------------------
# filter prints JSON to stdout, a count line to stderr; capture stdout only.
OPEN_JSON="$(node "$STATE" filter ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.pending-actions.json status eq open 2>/dev/null)" \
  || fail "could not run the pending-actions filter (state.mjs errored)"

node -e '
  const a = JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(0, "utf8"));
  if (!Array.isArray(a) || a.length === 0) { console.error("no open pending-actions"); process.exit(1); }
  const f = a.find(x => x.priority === "next");
  if (!f) { console.error("no open item carries priority:next (the session-start focus query would miss active work)"); process.exit(1); }
  const label = f.item || f.action || f.title || f.task || f.summary || "(no label field)";
  console.log("focus      seq=" + f.seq + "  " + String(label).replace(/\s+/g, " ").slice(0, 100));
  console.log("open work  " + a.length + " item(s)");
' <<<"$OPEN_JSON" || fail "pending-actions did not parse or has no priority:next focus"

# --- 2. newest LOG event ---------------------------------------------------
# tail returns the last PHYSICAL rows = newest for append-chronological files
# (ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json is written via state.mjs append, so tail = newest).
LOG_JSON="$(node "$STATE" tail ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json 1)" \
  || fail "could not tail ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json"

node -e '
  const a = JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(0, "utf8"));
  if (!Array.isArray(a) || a.length === 0) { console.error("ORCHESTRATOR-LOG is empty"); process.exit(1); }
  const e = a[a.length - 1];
  console.log("log newest " + (e.raw || e.text || e.type || "(no raw)").replace(/\s+/g, " ").slice(0, 120));
' <<<"$LOG_JSON" || fail "ORCHESTRATOR-LOG did not parse or is empty"

# --- 3. newest subagent dispatch -------------------------------------------
INVOC_JSON="$(node "$STATE" tail ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.subagent-invocations.json 1)" \
  || fail "could not tail ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.subagent-invocations.json"

node -e '
  const a = JSON.parse(require("fs").readFileSync(0, "utf8"));
  if (!Array.isArray(a) || a.length === 0) { console.error("subagent-invocations is empty"); process.exit(1); }
  const e = a[a.length - 1];
  console.log("dispatch   " + (e.raw || e.task || "(no raw)").replace(/\s+/g, " ").slice(0, 120));
' <<<"$INVOC_JSON" || fail "subagent-invocations did not parse or is empty"

echo "OK: spine reads back coherently (focus + LOG + dispatch all present)"

input.content:
---
name: update-context
description: Record a state change into this project's decomposed-JSON context spine. Use to append the ORCHESTRATOR-LOG event, update the context spine, upsert pending-actions / the focus item, log a subagent dispatch, file a discovery, and verify-don't-narrate (read the spine back). The mandatory closing step of every state-changing run — a commit, gate pass, seed/migration, settled decision, surfaced gap, or agent dispatch is not done until it is recorded here and read back.
---

# update-context

Project state lives in the decomposed JSON at `context-migration/decomposed/*.json` — one lossless array per former MD spine — operated through `node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs` (full-text reads via `search.mjs`). A state-changing action is **not complete until it is recorded**: a commit, a gate pass, a seed/migration, a decision settled, a gap surfaced, an agent dispatched. Flagging it in prose preserves nothing; the spine is the source of truth.

This routine is the **mandatory closing step of every other state-changing run.** Record the event, then read the spine back.

## Verify the spine reads back (the read-back driver)

After any write — and at session start to orient — run the read-only driver. It runs the orientation trio, asserts each parses and is non-empty, and prints the current focus + newest LOG event so you confirm the spine reconstructs the reality you just created. Exit 0 = coherent; nonzero = a parse failure / empty spine. It never writes.

```bash
.claude/skills/update-context/update-context.sh
```

## Orientation trio (what the driver runs; also session-start)

```bash
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs filter ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.pending-actions.json status eq open
```
The current focus is the open item carrying `"priority": "next"`; the rest are the open backlog.

```bash
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs tail ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs tail ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.subagent-invocations.json
```

## Recording forms

`append`/`upsert` auto-detect the key (`id` if present, else `seq`) and **print `context-migration/write-policies.json` before writing** — re-read your filing against each policy before it lands (no performative noise, no meta-preamble, no unwarranted certainty, signal-dense, no git-reproducible cruft). Build the row to a scratch file and pass `@scratch.json`.

Append an event to the log — row shape `{timestamp, type, text, refs:[…], continuation:[], raw}` (`type` carries the substantive description, may include verbatim evidence: gate output, commit SHAs, counts; `raw` is the one-line summary):

```bash
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs append ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json @scratch.json
```

Append a subagent dispatch — row shape `{timestamp, task, agents, outcome, raw}`:

```bash
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs append ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.subagent-invocations.json @scratch.json
```

Append a discovery (DISC):

```bash
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs append discoveries.json @scratch.json
```

Upsert the focus / open work — read the whole item, change the one field, write the **whole object** back:

```bash
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs read ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.pending-actions.json 6 > scratch.json
# edit scratch.json: e.g. status open→done + add an evidence field, or move priority:"next"
node context-migration/scripts/state.mjs upsert ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.pending-actions.json @scratch.json
```

## Gotchas

- **`upsert` REPLACES the whole matched element — it does not field-merge.** To amend one field, read the whole item, change that field, write the entire object back via `@scratch.json`. A partial object silently drops the unspecified fields.
- **`pending-actions.status` is exactly `open` or `done` — never a third value** (e.g. `in-progress`), or the session-start `filter status eq open` focus query misses active work. Mark `done` when committed (add an `evidence` field); move `priority:"next"` to the new focus.
- **`tail` returns the last PHYSICAL rows** = newest only for append-chronological files. `ORCHESTRATOR-LOG.json` and anything written via `append` are append-chronological, so `tail` = newest for them; a newest-first file returns oldest under `tail`.
- **`filter` prints JSON to stdout and the match count to stderr** — capture stdout only when piping.
- **Commit messages / row bodies with shell-special chars** (backticks, `$()`, `${}`) go through `git commit -F /tmp/file` (a file in the system `/tmp`, written via a quoted heredoc) — never `git commit -m "…"` (bash substitutes/drops those words silently). Keep the file out of the repo's tracked `tmp/`.
- **Verify, don't narrate.** After appending/upserting, RE-RUN the orientation trio (the driver above) and confirm it reconstructs the reality you just created. The run is not done until the spine reflects it and you have read it back — flagging is not persistence.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Fast, read-only smoke for this repo's active .context pi-context substrate.
# Exercises the canonical verification surface via the `pi-context` PATH CLI:
#   1. context-validate          — cross-block referential gate; asserts 0 errors
#                                  (warnings are allowed; the warning count is printed).
#   2. context-validate-relations — substrate-relations gate; asserts status "clean".
#   3. context-current-state     — reported, NOT gated: it is a known-degraded op
#                                  here ("state-derivation not configured", see SKILL.md).
#
# Usage:  bash .claude/skills/validate-context/validate-context.sh
# Exit 0 = substrate validates clean (0 referential errors AND relations clean).
# Nonzero ONLY on a real validate error (a referential error, or a non-clean
# relations status). Warnings and the current-state regression never fail it.
set -uo pipefail

# Resolve repo root from this script's location (skill dir is <repo>/.claude/skills/validate-context/).
SKILL_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO="$(cd "$SKILL_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"
cd "$REPO"

rc=0

echo "== 1/3 context-validate (cross-block referential integrity) =="
# Default (non --json) output is pretty-printed JSON, so `"severity": "error"`
# carries a space and grep -c counts it. The --json form is COMPACT
# (`"severity":"error"`, no space) and the spaced grep would miss it — so this
# driver intentionally does NOT use --json for the count (see SKILL.md gotcha).
validate_out="$(pi-context context-validate --cwd .)"
errors="$(printf '%s' "$validate_out" | grep -c '"severity": "error"')"
warnings="$(printf '%s' "$validate_out" | grep -c '"severity": "warning"')"
echo "context-validate: errors=$errors warnings=$warnings"
if [ "$errors" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: context-validate reported $errors error(s):"
  printf '%s\n' "$validate_out" | grep -A3 '"severity": "error"'
  rc=1
fi

echo
echo "== 2/3 context-validate-relations (substrate relations) =="
relations_out="$(pi-context context-validate-relations --cwd .)"
if printf '%s' "$relations_out" | grep -q '"status": "clean"'; then
  echo "context-validate-relations: clean"
else
  echo "FAIL: context-validate-relations is not clean:"
  printf '%s\n' "$relations_out"
  rc=1
fi

echo
echo "== 3/3 context-current-state (reported, NOT gated) =="
state_out="$(pi-context context-current-state --cwd .)"
if printf '%s' "$state_out" | grep -q 'state-derivation not configured'; then
  echo "context-current-state: KNOWN-DEGRADED ('state-derivation not configured')."
  echo "  This is the documented open regression (no top-level state_derivation key"
  echo "  in .context/config.json since engine commit 99f45de gated derivation on it)."
  echo "  The reliable gate is steps 1-2 above; current-state is not part of the gate."
else
  echo "context-current-state:"
  printf '%s\n' "$state_out"
fi

echo
if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "SMOKE OK: substrate validates clean (0 errors, $warnings warning(s) allowed; relations clean)."
else
  echo "SMOKE FAIL: a real validate error stands (see above)."
fi
exit "$rc"

input.content:
--- name: validate-context description: Validate context, check the .context substrate, query/read/write the context cascade, run context-validate, or read context-current-state for this repo's active-phase pi-context PM substrate. Use to gate the .context substrate (referential + relations integrity), query its tasks/features/gaps/verification blocks, or perform cascade writes. --- # Validate & query the .context pi-context substrate `.context/` is this repo's active-phase PM substrate: typed-JSON blocks (`tasks`, `features`, `story`, `phase`, `framework-gaps`, `verification`, `decisions`, `conventions`, …) with per-block schemas in `.context/schemas/`, cross-block edges in `.context/relations.json`, and config in `.context/config.json`. You operate it through the **`pi-context` CLI on PATH** (`which pi-context` → `/opt/homebrew/bin/pi-context`). Use `pi-context --cwd .` (or omit `--cwd`) to stay on THIS repo's substrate. Do **not** use the legacy `node …/pi-context-cli/dist/bin.js` raw-dist form — the PATH CLI is the mandated invocation. ## The driver (run this first) ```bash bash .claude/skills/validate-context/validate-context.sh ``` A fast, read-only smoke: runs `context-validate` (asserts 0 referential errors; prints the warning count), `context-validate-relations` (asserts `clean`), and reports `context-current-state` (NOT gated — it is a known-degraded op here, see below). Self-locating (resolves repo root from its own path; runnable from any cwd). Verified output in this container ends with `SMOKE OK: substrate validates clean (0 errors, 5 warning(s) allowed; relations clean).`, exit 0. **Exit 0 = the substrate validates clean. Nonzero only on a real validate error** (a cross-block referential error or a non-clean relations status); warnings and the current-state regression never fail it. ## The canonical gate (what the driver runs) ```bash # Cross-block referential integrity. Default (non --json) output is pretty-printed # JSON, so the error/warning count is greppable on the spaced severity token: pi-context context-validate --cwd . | grep -c '"severity": "error"' # -> 0 (clean) pi-context context-validate --cwd . | grep -c '"severity": "warning"' # -> 5 here # Substrate relations (config + items). status "clean" == pass: pi-context context-validate-relations --cwd . # -> {"status":"clean","issues":[]} ``` A completed task with no `verification_verifies_item` edge fails `context-validate` (the `completed-task-has-verification` invariant) — so the audit's verification is structurally required to mark a task done. After ANY `.context` write, re-run `context-validate` and confirm the result reconstructs what you wrote — verify, don't narrate. ## Query / read (no auth) ```bash pi-context read-block-item --block tasks --id TASK-052 --cwd . pi-context filter-block-items --block tasks --field status --op eq --value completed --cwd . pi-context read-block --block features --cwd . pi-context read-block-page --block framework-gaps --cwd . --json pi-context read-schema --schemaName tasks --cwd . --json # whole schema; add --path

to address one node pi-context find-references --itemId FGAP-007 --cwd . pi-context context-current-state --cwd . # see regression below pi-context gather-execution-context --unitId TASK-052 --kind task --cwd . pi-context read-config --cwd . # vocabulary, lenses, relation_types, per-block array_key/data_path ``` ## Cascade / write Writes need an explicit `--arrayKey` and resolve `--block` by the **data-file stem** (read `read-config` for each block's `array_key`). Build the item to a scratch file and pass `@file`: ```bash # append a framework-gap (block stem "framework-gaps", array_key "gaps"): pi-context append-block-item --block framework-gaps --arrayKey gaps \ --item @/tmp/gap.json --writer '{"kind":"agent","agent_id":"orchestrator"}' --cwd . # update one item (match by id, updates from a file): pi-context update-block-item --block tasks --arrayKey tasks \ --match '{"id":"TASK-052"}' --updates @/tmp/updates.json \ --writer '{"kind":"agent","agent_id":"orchestrator"}' --cwd . # add a cascade edge, then complete a task against its verification: pi-context append-relation --parent FEAT-003 --child TASK-052 --relation_type feature_contains_task --cwd . pi-context complete-task --taskId TASK-052 --verificationId VER-052 --cwd . ``` ## Gotchas (battle scars) - **Use the `pi-context` PATH CLI, not `node …/dist/bin.js`** — the raw-dist form is legacy; CLAUDE.md mandates `pi-context --cwd .`. - **No echo-header theatre, no ad-hoc parsers** — never wrap a check in `echo "==="`/`&& echo ok`; never pipe CLI output through ad-hoc `python3 -c`/`jq`. Use `--json` / `read-schema --path` / `read-config` / the CLI's own output. (The driver greps the *spaced* severity token in the default pretty-printed output for the count — a sanctioned parser-free count, not a data extraction.) - **The CLAUDE.md `--json | grep -c '"severity": "error"'` form is broken** — `--json` emits COMPACT JSON (`"severity":"error"`, no space), so the spaced grep matches nothing and silently returns 0 even when issues exist. Count on the **default (non-`--json`) pretty-printed** output, which has the space (what the driver does). The `--json` machine signal is the top-level `"status"` field (`ok` / `warnings` / `errors`). - **`--cwd .` stays on the active `.context`** — a non-active sibling substrate (e.g. `.workshopping`) is NOT reachable by `--cwd

`; the CLI resolves a substrate by its bootstrap pointer (`.pi-context.json`), so a sibling requires a transient `pi-context context-switch` of the root pointer and back, and `.pi-context.json` must end on `.context` (finding CTX-12). - **Write `--block`/`--arrayKey` resolve differently from reads** — writes resolve `--block` by the data-file stem AND need an explicit `--arrayKey` (e.g. `--block framework-gaps --arrayKey gaps`, `--block features` not `feature`, `--block story --arrayKey stories`); reads accept the canonical_id. Read `read-config` for each block's `{data_path stem, array_key}` (findings CTX-3 / CTX-7). - **Verify, don't narrate** — after any write, re-run `context-validate` (and `context-current-state`) and confirm it reconstructs what you created; never assert substrate state from memory. ## Known current regression: context-current-state ```bash pi-context context-current-state --cwd . # -> {"focus":"state-derivation not configured","inFlight":[],"nextActions":[],"blocked":[],"milestones":[]} ``` `context-current-state` returns **empty/degraded** here: since pi-context engine commit `99f45de` config-gated state derivation on a top-level `state_derivation` key, and this project's `.context/config.json` has none, the deriver short-circuits to `"state-derivation not configured"`. The fix path is to bring `state_derivation` in from the canonical conception (`pi-context update` + `resolve-blocked conventions`); see ORCHESTRATOR-LOG. Until then, **do not rely on current-state for rich status** — `context-validate` and `context-validate-relations` are unaffected and ARE the reliable gate. The driver reports current-state but never fails on this output. ## The driver, plainly `.claude/skills/validate-context/validate-context.sh` — the committed read-only smoke (validate + validate-relations gate, current-state reported). Verified in this container (macOS): exit 0, `context-validate: errors=0 warnings=5`, `context-validate-relations: clean`, current-state KNOWN-DEGRADED, `SMOKE OK`.

input.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Fast, read-only smoke for this repo's active .context pi-context substrate,
# via the `pi-context` PATH CLI:
#   1. context-validate           — cross-block referential gate; asserts 0 errors (warnings allowed).
#   2. context-validate-relations — substrate-relations gate; asserts status "clean".
#   3. context-current-state      — reported, not gated.
# Error detection parses the structured --json envelope (.output.status / the
# .output.issues[] severity field) with jq, so it is independent of output formatting.
#
# Usage:  bash .claude/skills/validate-context/validate-context.sh
# Exit 0 = 0 referential errors AND relations clean. Nonzero only on a real error.
set -uo pipefail

SKILL_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
REPO="$(cd "$SKILL_DIR/../../.." && pwd)"
cd "$REPO"

rc=0

echo "== 1/3 context-validate (cross-block referential integrity) =="
validate_json="$(pi-context context-validate --cwd . --json)"
status="$(printf '%s' "$validate_json" | jq -r '.output.status')"
errors="$(printf '%s' "$validate_json" | jq '[.output.issues[]|select(.severity=="error")]|length')"
warnings="$(printf '%s' "$validate_json" | jq '[.output.issues[]|select(.severity=="warning")]|length')"
echo "context-validate: status=$status errors=$errors warnings=$warnings"
if [ "$status" = "invalid" ] || [ "$errors" -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "FAIL: context-validate reported $errors error(s):"
  printf '%s' "$validate_json" | jq -r '.output.issues[]|select(.severity=="error")|"  \(.code) \(.field): \(.message)"'
  rc=1
fi

echo
echo "== 2/3 context-validate-relations (substrate relations) =="
relations_json="$(pi-context context-validate-relations --cwd . --json)"
rel_status="$(printf '%s' "$relations_json" | jq -r '.output.status')"
echo "context-validate-relations: status=$rel_status"
if [ "$rel_status" != "clean" ]; then
  echo "FAIL: relations not clean:"
  printf '%s' "$relations_json" | jq '.output.issues'
  rc=1
fi

echo
echo "== 3/3 context-current-state (reported, NOT gated) =="
state_json="$(pi-context context-current-state --cwd . --json)"
printf '%s' "$state_json" | jq -r '.output.focus // .output.data.focus // .output // "?"' | sed 's/^/context-current-state focus: /'

echo
if [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "SMOKE OK: substrate validates clean (status=$status, 0 errors, $warnings warning(s) allowed; relations clean)."
else
  echo "SMOKE FAIL: a real validate error stands (see above)."
fi
exit "$rc"

skills: add run-skills for validating + updating this repo's context substrates

Adds two project-functional run-skills under .claude/skills/ (tracked via an
extended .gitignore exception, joining run-prompt-workshop), each a SKILL.md man
page over a committed, self-locating, read-only driver verified green in this
container this session. They graduate two routines this orchestration runs by
hand every session into native, /-invokable skills.

validate-context/ — gates the active .context pi-context PM substrate:
  - validate-context.sh runs context-validate (cross-block referential),
    context-validate-relations (substrate relations), and reports
    context-current-state (not gated). Error detection parses the structured
    --json envelope with jq: .output.status is the machine verdict and each
    .output.issues[] carries a severity; the gate trips on status=="invalid"
    or any error-severity issue. This parse form is independent of output
    formatting (it does not depend on default-vs---json spacing).
  - SKILL.md documents the driver, the canonical jq gate, the query/read and
    cascade/write surfaces, the substrate-resolution gotcha (CTX-12), and the
    current context-current-state degradation (engine 99f45de gated state
    derivation on a top-level state_derivation key absent from this config).
  - Verified this session: status=warnings, 0 errors, 5 warnings; relations
    clean; current-state focus "state-derivation not configured"; SMOKE OK,
    exit 0.

update-context/ — reads back the decomposed-JSON orchestration spine:
  - update-context.sh prints the current focus (pending-actions priority:next),
    the open-work count, the newest ORCHESTRATOR-LOG row, and the newest
    subagent-invocations row, via context-migration/scripts/state.mjs.
  - SKILL.md documents the state.mjs append/upsert/filter/tail contract
    (upsert replaces the whole element; read-modify-write the full object),
    the focus/open-work/LOG/dispatch maintain discipline, and verify-don't-
    narrate read-back.
  - Verified this session: focus seq=6, 6 open items, LOG + dispatch newest
    rows present; OK, exit 0.

Intent: make the per-session validate/update routines reproducible from a clean
clone and self-documenting, not carried only in CLAUDE.md prose. The drivers are
the deliverable; the SKILL.md files are their man pages. No product code or
substrate data is changed by either skill (validate is read-only; update reads
the spine read-only).

input.prompt:
You are a forensic git + session-history auditor for the repository at /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan. Establish every fact yourself from primary sources. Do not rely on any prior summary, report, or conclusion from the main session — treat all of it as unverified.

## The symptom to investigate
This repo has accumulated multiple local git branches whose merge/disposition state is unclear. Some may be fully merged into `main`, some may hold unmerged work, some may be stacked on top of each other, some may be abandoned. The current state is not trusted. Determine the true state and the exact ordered sequence of operations to recover to a correct, clean state.

## Authoritative standard to audit against
Read these in the repo and audit the branch state against them — do not invent rules:
- `/Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/CLAUDE.md` — in particular the "Per-task branching" rule (each task on its own branch; orchestrator creates it before IMPL; merges to `main` only after all the task's success criteria validate AND only on the user's explicit authority), the "never leave the working tree dirty" rule, and the orchestrator-discipline rules about the ORCHESTRATOR-LOG / pending-actions / subagent-invocations state spine being kept current.

## Tools to use
- Plain `git` via Bash for the live branch topology: enumerate every local branch; for each, its tip SHA + subject, ahead/behind vs `main` (`git rev-list --left-right --count main...<b>`), merge-base relationships between branches (detect stacking), and whether its commits are already contained in `main`. Also check for worktrees (`git worktree list`) and any branches checked out in other worktrees. Check the working tree status and whether `main` is ahead of `origin/main` (unpushed).
- `claude-history` (CLI on PATH; run `claude-history --help`, and `claude-history queries list`) to reconstruct, per branch, WHAT task/work it was created for, when, whether it was ever presented to the user for merge, whether the user ever authorized a merge, and its intended disposition. Cross-reference git operations in history (`claude-history git-log` or the git_log facility) with the live branch tips.

## What to determine, per branch
1. What work/task the branch holds and when it was created.
2. Whether its commits are already in `main` (fully merged → redundant pointer) or not (unmerged work).
3. Stacking: is it based on another unmerged branch rather than on `main`.
4. Whether the user ever explicitly authorized merging it (history evidence). The per-task rule forbids merging to `main` without explicit user authority — so your recovery steps must mark which merges require the user's authorization and must NOT assume that authorization.
5. Whether it is abandoned/orphaned (work deprioritized, branch left dangling).

## Deliverable
A single report containing:
- A table of every local branch: tip SHA, ahead/behind main, contained-in-main yes/no, base branch (stacking), the task it holds, and evidence (commit SHAs + claude-history references / timestamps).
- The exact, ordered, copy-pasteable sequence of git + context-spine operations to recover to a clean correct state: which branches to merge (and in what order, given stacking), which to delete as already-contained redundant pointers, which require an explicit user merge-authorization decision before anything happens to them, which are abandoned and need a user disposition decision, plus any ORCHESTRATOR-LOG / pending-actions spine entries that are missing for completed work. For each step state its precondition and what it leaves true.
- Explicitly flag every step that must NOT be executed without the user's explicit authorization (per the per-task-branching rule), versus steps that are safe mechanical cleanup (e.g. deleting a branch whose commits are provably already in main).

Cite primary evidence for every claim. Where git and claude-history disagree, say so and trust git for current state, history for intent.

input.new_string:
| CTX-12 | footgun | A non-active substrate (e.g. `.workshopping`, sibling to the active `.context`) is NOT addressable by `--cwd <dir>`: `pi-context read-config --cwd .workshopping` returns `{config:null}` and `read-block --cwd .workshopping` errors "no `.pi-context.json` bootstrap pointer at `.../.workshopping/.pi-context.json`". The CLI resolves a substrate via a bootstrap pointer (`.pi-context.json`) found at the cwd, not by treating `--cwd` as a substrate-config dir; `.workshopping/` has no own pointer (it is a substrate the ROOT `.pi-context.json` names via `contextDir`). Consequence: every read/write to a non-active substrate requires `context-switch`ing the root pointer to it and back — which transiently violates the project's "`.pi-context.json` stays on `.context`" steady-state rule and, on a crash mid-edit, would strand the pointer on `.workshopping`. Verified directly (pointer on `.context`): both `--cwd .workshopping` ops fail as above; the working substrate edit (TASK-054, the 4 FRAG-intro fragment writes) was only landable via switch-out-and-back. | open-upstream (worked around via transient `context-switch` + restore; `.pi-context.json` is gitignored so the switch leaves no tracked change); candidate: let `--cwd <dir>` resolve a substrate whose `config.json` lives directly at `<dir>` (no pointer needed), OR a `--substrate <dir>` flag that targets a sibling substrate for one op without moving the global pointer |
| CTX-13 | footgun | `pi-context context-validate --json` emits COMPACT JSON (`"severity":"error"`, no space after the colon), while the default (non-`--json`) output is pretty-printed (spaced). So an error count taken as `--json \| grep -c '"severity": "error"'` (a space after the colon, the spaced token) matches NOTHING on the compact stream and silently returns `0` — a false-clean even when error-severity issues exist. Verified against the installed CLI: compact `--json` at `pi-context-cli/src/cli.ts:1136`, pretty default via `JSON.stringify(r.json,null,2)` at `pi-context/src/ops-registry.ts:183`. Same family as CTX-1 (`--json` envelope shape surprises). The robust read is structural, not textual: parse the `--json` envelope `{ok,op,output:{status,issues[]}}` with `jq` — gate on `.output.status == "invalid"` or `[.output.issues[]\|select(.severity=="error")]\|length`. | closed-our-side (the `validate-context` run-skill parses the `--json` envelope with `jq` on `.output.status`/`.output.issues[].severity`, format-independent); candidate upstream/doc: never document the spaced-grep count; point at the structured `--json` fields |

The CLI resolves a substrate via a bootstrap pointer (`.pi-context.json`) found at the cwd, not by treating `--cwd` as a substrate-config dir; `.workshopping/` has no own pointer (it is a substrate the ROOT `.pi-context.json` names via `contextDir`). Consequence: every read/write to a non-active substrate requires `context-switch`ing the root pointer to it and back — which transiently violates the project's "`.pi-context.json` stays on `.context`" steady-state rule and, on a crash mid-edit, would strand the pointer on `.workshopping`. Verified directly (pointer on `.context`): both `--cwd .workshopping` ops fail as above; the working substrate edit (TASK-054, the 4 FRAG-intro fragment writes) was only landable via switch-out-and-back. | open-upstream (worked around via transient `context-switch` + restore; `.pi-context.json` is gitignored so the switch leaves no tracked change); candidate: let `--cwd <dir>` resolve a substrate whose `config.json` lives directly at `<dir>` (no pointer needed), OR a `--substrate <dir>` flag that targets a sibling substrate for one op without moving the global pointer |

input.content:
# Language-polish pass on the prompt corpus (DEC-53 final copy-edit)

## Context

The prompt-workshop arc's structural work is done — the T6 run (2026-06-17) passed `check_draft.py --strict` and produced a draft whose coverage/distribution/three-view/bilingual/cadence/decision-request checks all PASS. The remaining arc-quality item is the DEC-53 "single final whole-draft language polish": the WARN-level, never-blocking copy-edit of register/voice, coined-terms, labels, strand casing, and wording.

Grounded against current source (this plan-mode session):
- **The machine-checked language surface is already clean.** Running `check_draft.py` on the current T6 draft (`/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json`) shows Check 1 (process-method terms) **0 hits**, Check 2 (scaffolding labels) **0 hits**, ambiguous terms **0**, strand prefixes **0**. So there is no corpus edit derivable from the harness WARN output — the preamble's register rules (`shared/preamble.md` voice antipatterns, verbatim-label rule, no-method-terms rule) are already holding on those axes.
- **The one recorded language item is the C2/A4 cross-field nit** (VER-050): step-0 narrative names standard `C2` while that step's structured `accreditation_standards` carries `A4` (both valid codes). **No automated check covers cross-field code consistency** (confirmed in `check_draft.py` — checks 1-2 scan prose for coined terms/labels only; check 4 validates code existence, not prose↔field agreement). It is non-deterministic and root-causes to a corpus-instruction gap (the prompt never tells the model to keep prose-named standard codes consistent with the structured field), not to a draft defect.
- **Scope is singular, not a fork** (resolved against mandates): DEC-41 makes the T6 draft a throwaway, non-deterministic `/tmp` artifact — copy-editing it fixes nothing systemic, so a draft-only pass is a mandate-004 negligent option (leaves the generating prompts, the root cause, untouched). The pass therefore targets the **prompt corpus** (`shared/preamble.md` + any implicated snippet), the durable fix that improves every future run and per DEC-41 becomes the production-prompt-migration spec.
- **Posture is light, per DEC-53**: ONE additive copy-edit pass, surfaced not gated, **NO iterate-to-zero audit loop** (language never blocks). This is NOT the full structural canonical pipeline; it is a single eval-surface → root-cause → terse-edit → parity-verify pass.

Outcome: the corpus carries a terse instruction for each genuine, register-rule-relevant gap a whole-draft register read surfaces that no automated check covers (the C2/A4-class cross-field consistency foremost); render-parity stays 14/14; the machine language-WARN surface stays 0; nothing structural regresses.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] Every language/register/cross-field-consistency nit present in the current whole-draft that no automated `check_draft.py` check covers has been surfaced by a register read and root-caused to either a corpus-instruction gap or a one-off non-deterministic slip.
- [ ] Each surfaced nit that root-causes to a corpus-instruction gap is addressed by a terse, additive instruction in `shared/preamble.md` and/or the single implicated snippet — stated as a register requirement, naming no coined method-term/label; one-off slips with no instruction gap are recorded, with the reason no corpus change is warranted, and left unedited.
- [ ] The cross-field standard-code consistency case (C2/A4-class: a standard named in a step's prose matches that step's structured `accreditation_standards`) is covered by a corpus instruction.
- [ ] Edited snippet/preamble bodies and their `.workshopping` fragments render byte-identical: `verify-render-parity` reports GATE 14/14 and both cross-checks PASS (exit 0); each edited fragment's `content_hash` is re-stamped.
- [ ] On a confirming generation, `check_draft.py` language checks 1-2 + ambiguous-term + strand-prefix scans remain at 0 hits, and the structural checks 3-8 remain PASS under `--strict` (no regression).

## The path (corpus-targeted, DEC-53-light)

1. **Pre-file the `.context` task** (TASK-NNN) with the success criteria above as `acceptance_criteria`, membership under the active story/feature/phase, before any agent dispatch. Create the task branch first (`git switch -c language-polish-pass`).
2. **Surface (single eval read, not a loop)** — dispatch ONE eval/Explore agent to read the current whole draft (`/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json`) against the `shared/preamble.md` register rules + DEC-53 language scope (register/voice, coined-terms, scaffolding labels, strand casing, wording/diction, AND cross-field standard-code consistency). It returns a flat list of findings — each with location (field path), the verbatim text, and which register rule / consistency property it touches. It proposes no fixes. Give it the symptom + where to look, not a hypothesis.
3. **Root-cause each finding** — the orchestrator corroborates each finding against `shared/preamble.md` + the implicated snippet source: is it a corpus-instruction gap (the prompt never directs the behavior) or a one-off non-deterministic slip (the prompt already directs it; the model lapsed). Only instruction-gap findings get a corpus edit.
4. **IMPL (terse additive edits)** — for each instruction-gap finding, add a terse register instruction to `shared/preamble.md` (if it applies across specs, e.g. the cross-field code-consistency rule) or to the single implicated snippet (if spec-local). Mirror each edited body to its `.workshopping` fragment via `pi-context update-block-item --cwd .workshopping` (re-stamps `content_hash`; reach `.workshopping` via the transient `context-switch` per CTX-12, restore the pointer to `.context`). Run the parity gate; commit on the branch.
5. **Verify (light, no loop)** — `verify-render-parity` GATE 14/14 + cross-checks PASS; one confirming full run (or, if a run is out of budget, the current draft) shows `check_draft.py` language WARN at 0 and structural 3-8 PASS under `--strict`. File a verification record (`criteria_results` per id), add the `verification_verifies_item` edge, `complete-task`, run `context-validate`. Do NOT loop re-IMPL→re-audit on language.
6. **Land + merge** — report green criteria; merge `language-polish-pass` → main only on the user's explicit authority. Production landing via an `ai/00NN_update_<slug>_template.py` migration is the downstream DEC-41 bridge, gated on DISC-26 closure — noted, not part of this pass.

## Discipline

input.prompt:
READ-ONLY register read. Change no files. Propose no fixes. Your output is a findings list, not edits.

Repo: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan

TASK: Read the assembled whole-plan draft at `/tmp/t6-out/draft-after-propose-review-loop.json` and surface every LANGUAGE / REGISTER / CROSS-FIELD-CONSISTENCY nit in it — the WARN-level, judgment-layer issues that no automated check catches.

THE RUBRIC (read it from source, do not work from my paraphrase): read `prompt-workshop/shared/preamble.md` in full — its "Plan-register rules", the voice directive + CONCRETE VOICE ANTIPATTERNS list, the verbatim-catalogue-label rule, the no-generation-side-method-terms rule, and the reference-by-identity rules. Those rules ARE the register standard. Judge the draft's prose against them.

WHAT IS ALREADY MACHINE-COVERED (do NOT re-report these — `prompt-workshop/dispatch/check_draft.py` already scans them and they are at zero on this draft): coined process-method terms; the OPEN/CORE/EVIDENCE/CLOSE scaffolding labels; bare ambiguous terms; GROUNDED/ROUNDED/UNBOUNDED strand prefixes; and the structural checks (coverage, owner distribution, review cadence, three-view, bilingual, decision-request well-formedness). Spend your attention on what those checks do NOT cover.

FOCUS — the judgment layer no check covers. For each, scan the whole draft and report instances:
1. Voice/register antipatterns from the preamble list actually present in prose: em-dash chains, rhetorical tetrads/triplets, "across all four/five X" cumulative-emphasis, rhythm flourishes, abstract-noun stacks, hedged transitions ("It is worth noting", "Importantly", "Notably"), summative meta-claims ("This plan represents a holistic approach").
2. Spelling/diction consistency: British vs American spelling mixed within the draft (e.g. "behaviour" vs "behavior", "programme" vs "program", "-ise" vs "-ize"). Report the mixed pairs with locations.
3. Catalogue-label fidelity in PROSE: a division/position/standard/outcome named in narrative prose that paraphrases, expands, or contracts the catalogue label rather than using it verbatim.
4. Cross-field consistency: where a step's PROSE names accreditation-standard codes (e.g. "A1", "C2") , does the SAME step's structured `accreditation_standards` field carry the same code(s)? Report any step where a standard named in prose is absent from that step's structured standards, or vice-versa. (This is one property to verify across all steps — report whatever you find, including "consistent throughout" if so.)
5. Reference-by-identity: any place prose quotes a learner-outcome gloss instead of its label, or writes a bare GROUNDED:/ROUNDED:/UNBOUNDED: prefix.
6. Any other register/wording nit that violates a preamble rule.

OUTPUT FORMAT: a flat numbered list. Each finding: (a) the JSON field path / location in the draft, (b) the verbatim offending text (short quote), (c) which preamble rule or consistency property it touches, (d) one line on why it is a nit. End with a count. If a focus area is clean, say so explicitly. Do not propose corpus edits or rewrites — surfacing only.
