input.prompt:
READ-ONLY root-cause investigation. Change nothing. Propose no fix. Report the mechanism only.

Repo: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan

SYMPTOM: In an assembled 14-spec workshop draft, the plan disagrees with itself about which WASC accreditation standards it targets. Step prose (e.g. `plan.desired_state`, `action_steps[].description`, step rationales) names a set of standard codes — e.g. "six targeted standards A1, B1, B2, B3, C1, C2" and stray mentions of A3, C3 — while the structured `accreditation_standards` field carries a DIFFERENT set (e.g. A1, B1, B2, B3, C1, A4). Codes named in prose (C2, A3, C3) have no structured entry; A4 is structured but absent from the prose set. All codes are valid (not fabricated).

DETERMINE, from current source:
1. WHICH of the 14 specs generates the step prose that names standard codes (the narrative / desired_state / action-step descriptions + rationales), and WHICH spec generates the structured `accreditation_standards` field. Look in `prompt-workshop/snippets/*.md` (spec bodies) and `prompt-workshop/dispatch/` (render/apply/sequence + the parse functions in `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py`). Name the spec_keys and the snippet files.
2. The SEQUENCE: in what order do those specs run (the workflow YAML under `.workflows/` and `run_workflow.mjs` / `sequence.py`), and does the prose-emitting spec run BEFORE or AFTER the structured-accreditation-standards spec.
3. What each of those specs receives as grounding / prior-draft context: does the prose-emitting spec SEE the structured `accreditation_standards` (so it could be told to match it), and does the accreditation-standards spec SEE the prose (so it could be told to cover what prose names)? Trace how the accumulating draft is passed between specs (the `draft-after-*.json` chaining, the grounding builder `ai/services/grounding.py`, and what "draft so far" each spec gets).
4. Whether ANY spec instruction currently tells the model that standard codes named in prose must match the structured `accreditation_standards` for the same step/plan (quote it if so; confirm its absence if not).

REPORT: the two spec_keys + snippet files (prose-emitter, structured-emitter), their run order, exactly what each sees of the other's output, and therefore WHERE an instruction enforcing prose↔structured standard-code agreement could actually take effect (which spec has both pieces of information in its context). Cite file:line. Do not write or propose the instruction text.

input.content:
context: file FGAP-044/045 (TASK-055 register gaps), link to TASK-055

The TASK-055 register read surfaced two corpus-instruction gaps that no automated
check covers; root-caused and source-corroborated, they are filed as first-class
framework-gaps so the IMPL directive cites them by id, the verification verifies
them by id, and context-validate cascades on the edges (prose alone composes into
nothing downstream).

FGAP-044 — prose-named WASC standards diverge from the structured
accreditation_standards. Spec 01 (narrative, runs first) writes prose committing
the plan to a targeted-standards set; spec 13 (propose-accreditation-standards)
selects the authoritative structured set independently, holds both the prose
(draft_state) and the catalogue in its grounding, and has no directive to make
the two agree. check_draft.py covers code existence, not prose-vs-field
agreement. WARN-level per DEC-53 (recorded earlier as the VER-050 nit). Proposed
resolution: a cross-cutting preamble rule (prose does not present an enumerated
targeted set as definitive — the structured field carries it) plus a spec-13
reconciliation directive; wording to be settled before IMPL.

FGAP-045 — no corpus rule governs spelling-variety consistency; a draft mixes
British and American forms within itself (counselling/counseling,
programme/programs, labelled/labeling, categorisation/institutionalization).
Proposed resolution: a terse preamble rule to use one spelling variety matching
the catalogue labels.

Edges: task_addresses_gap TASK-055 -> FGAP-044 and TASK-055 -> FGAP-045. Both
gaps status identified; TASK-055 planned; context-validate 0 errors, 5
pre-existing warnings (the filings add none). Findings drafted against the
write-policy register rules (signal-density, positive-statements, no-unwarranted-
certainty — "recurs by construction" corrected to "can recur" before filing).

The lighter findings the read surfaced (register flourishes, a catalogue-label
recombination) touch rules that already exist in the preamble; per DEC-53 they
are model lapses to record in verification, not new gaps, and are not filed here.

input.command: git add .context/framework-gaps.json .context/relations.json .context/objects/ && git status --short
input.description: Stage the FGAP filings + edges + objects

input.prompt:
You are an IMPL agent. Execute these edits verbatim. Do not interpret, redesign, or add scope. If any anchor text is not found exactly or any step is ambiguous, STOP and report — do not guess.

REPO: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan
BRANCH: language-polish-pass (already checked out; commit here; do NOT merge).
TASK: TASK-055 (DEC-53 language-polish pass). Addresses FGAP-044 (prose-named WASC standards diverge from the structured accreditation_standards) and FGAP-045 (no spelling-variety rule). DEC-53 light posture: land these instructions, verify parity, commit. No iterate-to-zero.

You are editing the prompt corpus. Two body files change, and each edited body must be mirrored byte-identical into its `.workshopping` substrate fragment, then the render-parity gate must pass 14/14. This is the TASK-054 pattern.

=== EDIT 1: shared preamble — two new plan-register bullets ===
File: prompt-workshop/shared/preamble.md
In the "**Plan-register rules (hold in every cell you write):**" bulleted block, ADD these TWO bullets as the LAST two bullets of that block (immediately before the line `**Reference learner outcomes and guiding clauses by their identity, not their gloss.**`). Insert exactly:

- **The plan's targeted standards are its structured Standards Targeted, not a prose list.** The set of WASC standards the plan targets is the set recorded in its structured Standards Targeted (the accreditation_standards the plan declares). In narrative prose, name a standard only where you substantively tie the plan to it, always as key + name (e.g. `C1: School Culture`); do not present an enumerated list of standards in prose as the plan's definitive targeted set. A standard you name in a step's prose must be one the plan actually targets.
- **One spelling convention.** Use a single spelling variety throughout the plan, matching the spelling of the school's catalogue labels (e.g. if a catalogue label reads `Counseling`, use American spelling). Do not mix British and American forms of the same word within the plan (`programme`/`program`, `counselling`/`counseling`, `labelled`/`labeling`, `-ise`/`-ize`).

=== EDIT 2: spec 13 — reconciliation directive ===
File: prompt-workshop/snippets/13-propose-accreditation-standards.md
ADD this paragraph immediately AFTER the line that reads `Every learner outcome, guiding clause, area for improvement, policy, or division named in `rationale` MUST be a verbatim match to a label from the corresponding catalogue above.` and BEFORE the `OUTPUT CONTRACT (follow exactly):` line. Insert exactly:

Make your proposed set coherent with the plan draft above. The draft's current_state, desired_state, and rationale may name WASC standards. Your proposed standards MUST include every standard the draft prose substantively ties the plan to advancing, and you MUST NOT propose a standard the draft prose gives no support for. Your proposed set is the plan's authoritative targeted standards; it must not contradict the standards the prose commits the plan to.

=== MIRROR TO .workshopping (byte-identical) ===
The `.workshopping` substrate holds each body as a `prompt-fragment` item; the snippet/preamble MD body and its fragment `body` must be identical, or the parity gate fails. Steps:
1. Identify the fragments: the shared preamble's fragment, and spec 13's intro fragment. Locate them by reading the `.workshopping` prompt-fragment block. The pi-context CLI resolves a substrate by its bootstrap pointer, NOT by `--cwd <dir>` (finding CTX-12): to reach `.workshopping` you must `pi-context context-switch` the root pointer to `.workshopping`, do the reads/writes, then context-switch back so `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`. Confirm the pointer is restored to `.context` at the end.
2. Update each fragment's `body` to exactly match the edited MD body (same surrounding blank lines), via `pi-context update-block-item --block prompt-fragment --arrayKey fragments --match '{"id":"<FRAG-ID>"}' --updates @<scratch.json> --writer '{"kind":"agent","agent_id":"impl"}'` — this re-stamps content_hash. Build each `{"body": "..."}` update to a /tmp scratch file.
3. The preamble fragment is shared by all 14 specs; the spec-13 fragment is spec-local. Edit both.

=== VERIFY PARITY ===
From school-improvement-plans/: `DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py` — it MUST print `GATE: 14/14` with both cross-checks PASS, exit 0. If it does not, the MD body and the fragment body diverged — reconcile them to identical text. Do NOT edit verify-render-parity.py or any gate/schema.

=== CONSTRAINTS ===
- Touch ONLY: prompt-workshop/shared/preamble.md, prompt-workshop/snippets/13-propose-accreditation-standards.md, and the two matching .workshopping fragments. Do NOT touch check_draft.py, self_check.py, .workflows/schemas/*, or any other snippet. Add/remove NO output-contract keys. Introduce no coined method-term/label.
- The MD body edit and its fragment edit for one file land together (parity never committed red).
- `.pi-context.json` must end on `.context`.

=== COMMIT ===
After parity is GREEN (14/14, exit 0), commit on the language-polish-pass branch. Stage only the four touched files (the two MD bodies, .workshopping/prompt-fragment.json, and any .workshopping/objects/* the update created) — plus restore .pi-context.json if the context-switch left it changed (it is gitignored; confirm `git status` shows it is not staged). Use a forensic commit message via `git commit -F /tmp/<msgfile>` describing: the two preamble bullets (FGAP-044 standards-authoritative + FGAP-045 spelling) and the spec-13 reconciliation directive (FGAP-044), that bodies were mirrored to .workshopping and parity verified 14/14, and that no gate/schema/output-contract changed. Speak to aims, not certainties; no "this ensures/fixes". Do NOT add author-credit lines.

=== REPORT BACK ===
Return: the exact diff of each body file, the FRAG ids you edited, the verify-render-parity output (the GATE line + exit code), the commit SHA, and `git status` (must be clean). If you STOPPED, report exactly where and why.

In narrative prose, name a standard only where you substantively tie the plan to it, always as key + name (e.g. `C1: School Culture`); do not present an enumerated list of standards in prose as the plan's definitive targeted set. A standard you name in a step's prose must be one the plan actually targets.

Do not mix British and American forms of the same word within the plan (`programme`/`program`, `counselling`/`counseling`, `labelled`/`labeling`, `-ise`/`-ize`).

input.prompt:
You are an adversarial auditor. You did NOT make these edits. Try to find them WRONG, incomplete, or out of scope. Report findings; propose no fixes. End with `Total findings: N`.

REPO: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan
BRANCH: language-polish-pass (HEAD commit 27ac24ea). An IMPL agent claims it landed TASK-055's DEC-53 language-polish edits and that render-parity is 14/14. Verify every claim against current source and the live gate. Do not trust the IMPL report.

Verify each, independently:

1. THE THREE EDITS ARE PRESENT, VERBATIM, AND TERSE:
   a. `prompt-workshop/shared/preamble.md` — a new plan-register bullet stating the plan's targeted WASC standards are its structured Standards Targeted (not a prose list), prose names a standard only where substantively tied + key+name, no prose list as the definitive targeted set.
   b. `prompt-workshop/shared/preamble.md` — a new bullet requiring one spelling variety throughout matching catalogue labels, no British/American mixing.
   c. `prompt-workshop/snippets/13-propose-accreditation-standards.md` — a directive that the proposed structured set must cover every standard the draft prose substantively commits to and propose none the prose gives no support for.
   Confirm none of these introduces a coined method-term or scaffolding label. Confirm they read as terse additive instructions, not rewrites.

2. THE NEW TEXT ACTUALLY APPEARS IN THE RENDERED PROMPTS (the real test, not just file presence): from `school-improvement-plans/`, render spec 13 and one other spec and grep the rendered output for the new instruction text:
   `DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/render_from_substrate.py --spec-key propose-accreditation-standards`

input.prompt:
READ-ONLY root-cause investigation. Change nothing. Propose no fix. Determine whether a specific parser rejection is VALID or a defect, from current source.

Repo: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan

THE EVENT: a workshop full run aborted at spec 07 (US-LLM-27, propose-responsibilities). The apply step raised:
`ValueError: US-LLM-27 output position-grain statement (under division="Principal's Office (PO)", position='IHS Principal')='Hold every division accountable for its contribution and allocate the resources to deliver it.' is not in the school's enumerated catalogue. Available: [Gather feedback from the stakeholders relevant to your position. | Incorporate that feedback into your practice and your area's plans. | Lead your unit's specific contribution to the school-wide learner outcomes, School Mission, and School Vision, and report its progress. | Model, and foster the development in all students of, all school-wide learner outcomes, School Mission, and School Vision, in all phases of your duties. | Participate in the scheduled policy auditing and revision cycles for your area. | Track and use data to evaluate the success of improvement work in your area of responsibility.]`

DETERMINE, citing file:line in current source:
1. THE SPEC'S INTENT: read `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md` in full. Does US-LLM-27 instruct the model to (a) ASSIGN/select from an enumerated catalogue of responsibility statements, or (b) PROPOSE/author position-specific responsibility statements? Quote the instruction text and the output contract. What does the prompt tell the model the allowed values are.
2. THE PARSER/GATE: find the parse function for propose-responsibilities (search `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` for the parse_* fn and any "position-grain"/"enumerated catalogue" check; also `prompt-workshop/dispatch/apply_from_substrate.py` / `_workshop.py`). Read exactly what it admits: does it require an EXACT string match against an enumerated catalogue of responsibility atoms? What is that catalogue and where does it come from (a DB query? a grounding section? a fixed list)? Quote the check.
3. THE CATALOGUE SOURCE: what populates the "enumerated catalogue" of responsibility atoms the parser checks against — is it the school's seeded responsibility atoms (DEC-35/DEC-37 HOD-default-responsibilities)? Are those atoms POSITION-SPECIFIC or GENERIC defaults shared across positions? Does the catalogue contain any principal-specific / cross-division responsibility, or only the six/seven generic atoms in the error?
4. THE CONTRADICTION TEST: if the spec tells the model to PROPOSE position-specific responsibilities but the parser only admits exact matches to a fixed generic catalogue, that is an internal contradiction (the prompt invites content the gate then rejects). If the spec tells the model to ONLY pick from the enumerated list and the model invented one anyway, that is the model disobeying a clear instruction. Determine which case this is, with quotes from BOTH the spec instruction and the parser check.
5. Whether the grounding the spec 07 prompt receives actually ENUMERATES the allowed responsibility statements to the model (so the model could pick verbatim), or whether the model is left to author them. Check spec 07's grounding_sections + what the responsibilities grounding emitter sends.

REPORT: the spec's actual intent (assign-from-catalogue vs propose-new, quoted), the parser's actual admission rule (quoted), the catalogue's source and whether it is generic-vs-position-specific, and a clear verdict on whether this rejection is VALID (model disobeyed an enumerate-only instruction) or a DEFECT (spec invites authored responsibilities the gate then rejects / catalogue too narrow / grounding doesn't give the model the allowed list). Cite file:line throughout. Do not propose a fix.

options.description: Build hod-doc's design: materialize a stored position-grain PositionResponsibility atom — 'be accountable for delivering the division's responsibility atoms' — on every leader Position (kind=leader), as a DEC validating/extending hod-doc + a seed migration. The head-position then literally carries its division's responsibilities at the position grain; the model cites that atom. Needs you to validate hod-doc's head-accountability default and author the atom's exact statement text (responsibility atoms are admin-authored content). Permanent data; aligns with the design authority.
options.label: Position-grain atom (hod-doc)

options.description: The division's responsibility stays at its own (division) grain where it lives; a head-position 'carries' it by being the responsible assignee for the step, with the division responsibility cited in spec 07's division_responsibilities slot. Fix = harden spec 07 so division-purview responsibilities are routed to the division slot (not the position slot), preventing the misfile that aborted the run. No data/seed/DEC change; a prompt-corpus edit like TASK-055. Lighter; does not add a position-grain atom.
options.label: Division-grain routing (prompt)

questions.question: A head-position carries its division's responsibilities (settled). The system never built the mechanism for it. How should the fix express 'carries'? (Both unblock the run; the choice is a design-record-reserved call, not a question of the principle.)

input.content:
# Materialize the head-accountability responsibility atom on leader positions (hod-doc's known path)

## Context

A workshop full run aborted at spec 07 (US-LLM-27, propose-responsibilities): the model attributed the Principal's Office division responsibility "Hold every division accountable for its contribution and allocate the resources to deliver it." to the IHS Principal position, and the parser rejected it because that statement is not among the IHS Principal position's own enumerated atoms.

Root cause (corroborated against source): the mechanism by which a head-position carries its division's responsibilities is **designed but never built**. `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md` (the design source for DEC-35) defines, as a **position-grain default of the head seat**, "Be accountable for delivering the department's responsibility atoms" (line 56), and its materialization constraint (lines 20-27) requires this to live as a **stored `PositionResponsibility` atom**, not a computed string. But that materialization seed was never built — `school/migrations/0050_seed_director_positions.py` seeds the four Director leader positions (incl. the IHS Principal, `kind="leader"`) and explicitly states "No responsibility-atom rows … are made here." So no leader position carries the head-accountability atom; when the model tried to express the IHS Principal's accountability for its division's responsibilities, it had no position-grain atom to cite and reached for the division atom, which the parser correctly rejected at the position grain.

The principle is settled (a head-position carries its division's responsibilities) and the design path is settled (hod-doc's stored head-accountability atom). DEC-36 already generalized the leader-Position pattern from subject-dept HODs to any division's leadership (the four Directors are `kind="leader"`), so the generalization needed here is already decided. The fix is to build the atom hod-doc designed.

Intended outcome: every leader Position carries a stored position-grain responsibility atom expressing accountability for delivering its division's responsibilities, so the head's accountability is citable at the position grain and the run no longer aborts. This also unblocks TASK-055's confirming run (which this defect blocked).

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] Every leader Position (`kind="leader"`) carries a stored position-grain responsibility atom expressing that the seat is accountable for delivering its division's responsibilities; the atom is materialized (a real `PositionResponsibility` row), not computed at grounding time.
- [ ] The atom appears in the grounding responsibility inventory under its position, and the propose-responsibilities parser admits a position-grain citation of it (no rejection).
- [ ] A full workshop generation that attributes a leader-position's accountability for its division's responsibilities to that position completes without the spec-07 catalogue-gate abort.
- [ ] The seed is idempotent (re-running changes nothing) and reversible (the unseed removes exactly the rows it added); pre-existing position atoms are unchanged.
- [ ] The decision is recorded as a DEC extending DEC-35/DEC-36, validating hod-doc's head-accountability position-grain default and its application to all leader positions.
- [ ] Nothing else breaks: the project gate stays green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js); existing seeds/positions/atoms are unaffected.

## The fix (hod-doc's materialization, generalized per DEC-36)

1. **Settle the DEC** — record a decision (extending DEC-35/DEC-36) that validates hod-doc's position-grain head-accountability default and materializes it on every `kind="leader"` Position, parameterized by the position's division. The atom statement is hod-doc's canonical wording, generalized from "department" to the division: "Be accountable for delivering the division's responsibility atoms." (The exact rendered statement per position — generic vs naming the division label — is settled at seed authoring; default to hod-doc's wording.) Persist the DEC to `data/seed-round-plan.json` (+ DISC/LOG per the persist discipline).
2. **Seed migration** — `school/migrations/00NN_seed_leader_head_accountability.py`, mirroring the `0048`/`0050` seed pattern: for each `Position` with `kind="leader"` under the tenant school, `get_or_create` a `PositionResponsibility` carrying the head-accountability statement (idempotent; reversible unseed deletes exactly those rows). Reuse the existing `PositionResponsibility` model + the `get_or_create((position, statement))` convention; no model change (the field + relation exist).
3. **No grounding/parser/spec change** — the grounding emitter already emits each position's responsibilities (`ai/services/grounding.py` `_division_responsibility_atoms`), the parser already admits a position's own atoms (`planner/specs.py` `_responsibility_statements_under_position`), and spec 07 already has the position_responsibilities slot. The materialized atom flows through all three unchanged; this is the minimal locus that closes the gap.
4. **Apply + confirm** — apply the migration to the dev DB (tenant `chiway-repton-xiamen`); confirm the atom appears in the grounding inventory under each leader position; run a confirming full workshop generation to confirm the spec-07 abort is gone.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline, Mode-B; on its own branch)

1. Settle + persist the DEC (conversation → `seed-round-plan.json` + LOG).
2. Create the task branch (`git switch -c seed-leader-head-accountability`) before any IMPL.
3. Pre-file the `.context` task (these acceptance_criteria; membership; `task_addresses_gap` → FGAP for this defect, filed first).
4. IMPL agent: author the seed migration (self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity).
5. Adversarial audit (separate agent): verify each success criterion by id — atom materialized on every leader position, idempotent + reversible, grounding emits it, parser admits it, pre-existing atoms unchanged, gate green. Loop to zero.
6. Cascade: apply migration to dev DB; confirming workshop run; VER + `verification_verifies_item` edge + `complete-task`; `context-validate`; LOG + subagent-invocations rows.
7. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- `manage.py migrate` (dev DB, postgres superuser DSN) applies cleanly; `migrate <prev>` reverses cleanly (round-trip).
- A shell/ORM check (or the grounding emitter) shows each `kind="leader"` Position now carries the head-accountability `PositionResponsibility`; re-running the seed is a no-op (idempotent).
- `verify-render-parity` stays 14/14 (no corpus change, but confirm); a full `run_workflow.mjs … --fresh --yes` completes 42/42 with no spec-07 abort, and `check_draft.py --strict` PASSES on the assembled draft (this also satisfies TASK-055's criterion 5).
- Full project gate green: `manage.py check`, `ruff`, `mypy .`, `pytest`, `make test-js`.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- One atomic step per turn; read-before-write; `state.mjs` for the decomposed JSON, `pi-context` CLI for `.context`; commit messages via `git commit -F` from a `/tmp` file when shell-special chars are present.
- All `uv`/`manage.py` runs from `school-improvement-plans/`; dev DB carries tenant `chiway-repton-xiamen`; PyRight `.objects`/import errors are documented false positives, mypy is the type gate.
- No model change (the `PositionResponsibility` field + relation exist); no grounding/parser/spec edit; the fix is data (a seed) + the DEC.
- Observed-green-before-commit; cleanup via `git restore`/`rm`, never `git reset`; working tree clean before "done".

## Critical files

- `school/migrations/00NN_seed_leader_head_accountability.py` — NEW; the materialization seed (mirror `0050_seed_director_positions.py` + the `0048` HOD-atoms pattern).
- `school/models/person.py` — `Position` (`kind`), `PositionResponsibility` (the atom model + `position` FK) — read-only reference for the seed.
- `ai/services/grounding.py` — `_division_responsibility_atoms` (emits position responsibilities; confirm the new atom surfaces) — read-only.
- `planner/specs.py` — `_responsibility_statements_under_position` + `parse_propose_responsibilities` position-grain check (confirm the new atom is admitted) — read-only.
- `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md` — the design source (line 49-59 head-accountability default; lines 20-27 materialization constraint).
- `data/seed-round-plan.json` — the new DEC (extending DEC-35/DEC-36).

allowedPrompts.prompt: run manage.py migrate / makemigrations / shell and the project gate (ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js) under uv from school-improvement-plans/ against the dev DB
run the prompt-workshop confirming run (run_workflow.mjs --fresh --yes) + check_draft.py against the dev DB
state.mjs append/upsert + pi-context CLI writes to record the DEC and the .context task/FGAP/VER
git branch/switch/commit on the seed-leader-head-accountability branch

GOAL: a new data migration that materializes ONE position-grain `PositionResponsibility` atom on every DIVISION-HEAD leader Position of the tenant school, so a head-position carries its division's responsibilities (currently no leader position carries such an atom, which aborts the workshop's US-LLM-27 step).

Filter on the tenant school (slug `chiway-repton-xiamen`) via the Position→Division→School relation.

THE ATOM STATEMENT (verbatim, do not alter): "Be accountable for delivering the division's responsibilities."

STEPS:
1. Read `school/models/person.py` for the `Position` and `PositionResponsibility` models — the exact fields (`statement` and its translated columns e.g. `statement_en`/`statement_zh_hans`, `order`, the `position` FK, `kind`, `year_group`), and how `Position` relates to `School` (via `division`). Read `school/models/org.py` if needed for the Division→School relation.

Mirror its exact field-setting, translation handling, and `order` convention. If the existing atoms set a `statement_zh_hans` (Chinese) value and a translation is therefore required for the new atom, STOP and report (do not invent Chinese content) — note whether DEC-4-style deferral (English-only, blank zh_hans, as in 0050's labels) is the established pattern for these rows.
3. Author the migration `school/migrations/00NN_seed_leader_head_accountability.py` (NN = next sequential after the highest existing school migration; depend on that highest one), mirroring `0050_seed_director_positions.py`'s structure: get the School by slug (no-op if absent), iterate the in-scope Positions, `get_or_create` the `PositionResponsibility` with the atom statement (idempotent — keyed so a re-run creates nothing new), and a reverse function (`unseed`) that deletes exactly the rows it added. RunPython, reversible.

5. Run the full project gate (from school-improvement-plans/, under uv, with the DSN): `manage.py check`, `ruff check`, `mypy .`, `pytest`, `make test-js`. All must be green. (PyRight `.objects`/import errors are documented false positives; mypy is the type gate.)

CONSTRAINTS: touch ONLY the new migration file. NO model change, NO grounding.py / specs.py / snippet / schema edit. STOP on any ambiguity (a missing field, a required translation you can't author, an unclear seed pattern) and report — do not guess.

COMMIT: after the migration applies + verifies green on the dev DB and the gate is green, commit on the branch with a forensic `git commit -F /tmp/<msgfile>` message: what the migration seeds, the exact scope (kind=leader, year_group NULL; year-leaders excluded), the verbatim statement, idempotent+reversible, dev-DB applied + verified, gate green, no model/grounding/parser/spec change. Speak to aims not certainties; no author-credit lines.

input.content:
"""Seed one position-grain accountability `PositionResponsibility` atom on every
DIVISION-HEAD leader `Position` of the tenant school (DEC-55; addresses FGAP-046).

Each division-head leader seat — the HOD seats (0048) and the Director seats
(0050) — should carry an atom binding it to its division's responsibilities, so a
head position structurally articulates accountability for what its division owns.
Before this migration no leader position carries such an atom, which aborts the
workshop's US-LLM-27 step. This adds exactly one atom per in-scope seat.

Scope: `Position` rows of the tenant school (slug `chiway-repton-xiamen`) with
`kind="leader"` AND `year_group` unset (NULL) — the division-head seats. The
year-group-leader seats (0049) carry a `year_group` FK and are EXCLUDED: they
lead a year group, not a division. The tenant filter walks
Position -> Division -> School.

Atom statement (verbatim): "Be accountable for delivering the division's
responsibilities."

Translation handling mirrors the model's own existing `PositionResponsibility`
rows: the base `statement` and the `statement_en` column are both set to the
English statement; `statement_zh_hans` is left unset (NULL) — the DEC-4-style
English-only deferral established for these rows (0050 used the same English-only
deferral for its Director `label`s). No Chinese content is authored here.

`order` is set to append after any atoms a seat already carries (max existing
`order` + 1), so the accountability atom orders last and does not collide with
the seat's existing job-description atoms.

Keying: `get_or_create(position=..., statement=ATOM)`. Idempotent — a re-run
matches the already-created row by (position, statement) and creates nothing new.
Reversible: `unseed` deletes exactly the rows it adds (the in-scope seats'
atoms carrying this exact statement). No-op if the tenant school is absent.
No model change, no grounding/parser/spec/snippet edit.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from django.db import migrations

SCHOOL_SLUG = "chiway-repton-xiamen"

# Verbatim accountability atom seeded on every division-head leader seat.
ATOM_STATEMENT = "Be accountable for delivering the division's responsibilities."


def seed_leader_head_accountability(apps, schema_editor):
    School = apps.get_model("school", "School")
    Position = apps.get_model("school", "Position")
    PositionResponsibility = apps.get_model("school", "PositionResponsibility")
    alias = schema_editor.connection.alias
    school = School.objects.using(alias).filter(slug=SCHOOL_SLUG).first()
    if school is None:
        return
    positions = Position.objects.using(alias).filter(
        division__school=school,
        kind="leader",
        year_group__isnull=True,
    )
    for position in positions:
        existing_max = (
            PositionResponsibility.objects.using(alias)
            .filter(position=position)
            .order_by("-order")
            .values_list("order", flat=True)
            .first()
        )
        next_order = 0 if existing_max is None else existing_max + 1
        PositionResponsibility.objects.using(alias).get_or_create(
            position=position,
            statement=ATOM_STATEMENT,
            defaults={
                "statement_en": ATOM_STATEMENT,
                "order": next_order,
            },
        )


def unseed_leader_head_accountability(apps, schema_editor):
    School = apps.get_model("school", "School")
    Position = apps.get_model("school", "Position")
    PositionResponsibility = apps.get_model("school", "PositionResponsibility")
    alias = schema_editor.connection.alias
    school = School.objects.using(alias).filter(slug=SCHOOL_SLUG).first()
    if school is None:
        return
    positions = Position.objects.using(alias).filter(
        division__school=school,
        kind="leader",
        year_group__isnull=True,
    )
    PositionResponsibility.objects.using(alias).filter(
        position__in=positions,
        statement=ATOM_STATEMENT,
    ).delete()


class Migration(migrations.Migration):
    dependencies = [
        ("school", "0061_delete_missionarea"),
    ]

    operations = [
        migrations.RunPython(
            seed_leader_head_accountability,
            unseed_leader_head_accountability,
        ),
    ]

ok=True

input.content:
school: seed division-head accountability atom on leader head positions (TASK-056 / DEC-55 / FGAP-046)

Adds school migration 0062_seed_leader_head_accountability — a RunPython data
migration that aims to materialize one position-grain PositionResponsibility
atom on every DIVISION-HEAD leader Position of the tenant school, so a head
position structurally carries accountability for its division's responsibilities.
Before this, no leader position carried such an atom, which aborts the workshop's
US-LLM-27 step; this seed is intended to close that.

Scope (corroborated against the dev DB): Position rows of the tenant school
(slug chiway-repton-xiamen) with kind="leader" AND year_group unset (NULL) — the
HOD seats (0048) and Director seats (0050), 13 in this tenant. The year-group
leader seats (0049), which carry a year_group FK, are EXCLUDED (they lead a year
group, not a division). Tenant filter walks Position -> Division -> School.

Atom statement, verbatim and unaltered: "Be accountable for delivering the
division's responsibilities."

Translation handling mirrors the model's own existing PositionResponsibility
rows: base statement and statement_en both set to the English statement,
statement_zh_hans left unset (NULL) — the DEC-4-style English-only deferral
already established for these rows (0050 used the same English-only deferral for
its Director labels). No Chinese content authored. order is set to max-existing-
order + 1 per seat so the atom appends after the seat's existing job-description
atoms without colliding.

get_or_create keyed on (position, statement): intended to be idempotent — a
re-run matches the existing row and creates nothing. unseed deletes exactly the
in-scope seats' atoms carrying this exact statement; reversible. No-op if the
tenant school is absent.

Dev DB (postgres://...@localhost:5433): migrate applied cleanly. Verified all 13
in-scope seats now carry exactly one atom (en set, zh NULL, order = prior-max+1)
and all 4 year-leader seats carry none. Idempotency: a second seed-fn run created
no new rows (13 stayed 13; total 356 -> 369). Reversibility: migrate school 0061
removed exactly the 13 atoms (total back to 356, no collateral deletion), then
re-apply restored them.

Gate green: manage.py check (0 issues), ruff check . (passed), mypy . (no issues,
325 files), pytest (3214 passed), make test-js (48 pass / 0 fail). No model change,
no grounding.py / specs.py / parser / snippet / schema edit — only the new
migration file is added.

   - ONLY CITABLE FIELD: s.code (required output field)
   - CONTEXT FIELDS RENDERED: s.category (label), s.text (prose description)
   - MARKED DISTINCTLY: Code in parens is visually distinguished from prose
   - ADMISSION: ONLY code is checked against catalogue; prose is context only
   - GAP: None (code is the only admission-checked field)

   C1 (suggest-feedback-channels, spec 12):
   - RENDER: Division labels in a list; position absent
   - PARSER: Only division labels checked (no position structure complexity)
   - GAP: None (single grain, clear ownership)

   B1 (draft-success-criteria):
   - RENDER: Criterion text + (when target) target_value, target_unit, baseline
   - PARSER: All rendered fields are checked by kind (target fields only for target kind)
   - GAP: None (rendered fields align with parsed fields via kind-gating)

6. CLASSIFICATION OF THE GAP
   ────────────────────────

   PROMPT LAYER GAP (Renders non-citable values):
   ───────────────────────────────────────────
   ✓ scope_summary is rendered (line 27, 31) as context/description
   ✓ Model can read and potentially copy it
   ✗ Parser admits ONLY responsibility.statement, NOT scope_summary
   ✓ Instruction says "choose each atom by copying its owner and its EXACT statement from this list"
   ✗ But scope_summary is visually present on the SAME line as the division label
   ✗ Model confusion: is scope_summary a "context descriptor" or a "copyable atom"?

   PARSER LAYER GAP (Rejects what's rendered):
   ────────────────────────────────────────────
   ✗ _responsibility_statements_under_division does NOT include scope_summary
   ✗ _responsibility_statements_under_position does NOT include scope_summary
   ✓ Checks are correct in spirit: scope_summary is NOT a responsibility statement
   ✗ But model read it from the RENDERED prompt and tried to cite it
   ✗ Parser error message: "…is not in the school's enumerated catalogue"
   ✗ (Unhelpful: scope_summary is NOT supposed to be in the catalogue; it IS descriptive)

   APPLY LAYER GAP (No graceful fallback):
   ───────────────────────────────────────
   ✗ Single parse ValueError → return 1 (entire run aborts)
   ✗ No distinction between:
      - Fabricated atom (never in grounding)
      - Mis-slotted real atom (in grounding, wrong grain or field)
   ✗ No attempt to repair/drop/retry a single bad citation
   ✗ Whole spec output discarded; workflow halts

7. FIX LOCATION CHARACTERIZATION
   ─────────────────────────────

   Closable at PROMPT layer:
   - Mark scope_summary DISTINCTLY as context-only (e.g., bold, indented, labeled)
   - Separate responsibility statements into a clearly citable subsection
   - Template change: move scope_summary outside the copyable block or clearly mark it non-citable
   - Example: "Division "{{ d.label }}" (scope: {{ d.scope_summary }})" vs current inline rendering
   - Benefit: Model learns to NOT copy scope_summary; no parser change needed

   Closable at PARSER layer:
   - Accept scope_summary as an admissible statement (if it's EVER meant to be cited)
   - OR: Include scope_summary explicitly in the rendered atom list (separate field)
   - Change: _responsibility_statements_under_division includes .scope_summary in addition to .responsibilities
   - Benefit: No prompt change; parser admits what's rendered; but changes semantic meaning
   - Trade-off: scope_summary is a "scope overview" (DEC-32 Layer 1), NOT a "responsibility statement"

   Closable at APPLY layer:
   - Catch single mis-slotted citation; drop it; continue processing other atoms
   - Change apply_from_substrate.py line 129: catch ValueError, log it, filter prefill, merge partial
   - Benefit: Graceful degradation; single bad atom doesn't abort the whole run
   - Trade-off: Inconsistent with intent (model should cite exactly what's admissible)

8. ROOT CAUSE CLASSIFICATION
   ──────────────────────────

   This is a CLASS of contract gaps, not three isolated instances.
   
   PATTERN: The prompt renders a SUPERSET of values (division labels, statements, scope_summary, position labels)
            for context/clarity, but the parser admits a STRICT SUBSET (labels + statements only).
   
   ROOT CAUSE: The grounding emitter (_division_responsibility_atoms) includes scope_summary for
               EVERY entity (division and position) because it's useful for prose context,
               but the parser NEVER checks it against the admissible set.
   
   HYPOTHESIS CONFIRMED: Any rendered value that is not explicitly in the parser's admission checks
                         (lines 2702-2716 for division, 2749-2770 for position) is a non-citable context
                         field. The three observed instances are EXAMPLES of this pattern:
                         1. scope_summary rendered, not in statement catalogue → reject
                         2. statement from wrong grain (position statement at division grain) → rejected at line 2716
                         3. accreditation standard prose name cited instead of code → rejected (wrong spec, but same pattern)
   
   SINGLE FIX PRINCIPLE: If the fix is applied at the PROMPT layer (mark context distinctly),
                         it addresses ALL categories: scope_summary, cross-grain confusion, prose names.
                         If applied at PARSER layer (admit more fields), it must enumerate each
                         category separately. If applied at APPLY layer, it's a catch-all but loses
                         semantic intent.

=============================================================================
EOF

input.content:
# Close the prompt↔parser citability gap corpus-wide (the spec-07 mis-citation class)

## Context

Three workshop runs aborted at spec 07 (US-LLM-27) on the same class: the model copied a value the prompt presents but the parser does not admit. Root cause (verified by two read-only explores): the grounding emits contextual fields (`scope_summary`, `kind`, `category`, prose) alongside the citable identity fields (`label`, `statement`, `code`); a spec's prompt can render that superset inside a "copy exactly from this list" inventory; the parser admits only the exact identity field at the exact owner/grain. The model copies something in the gap and the hard catalogue-gate (`apply_from_substrate.py` parse → `ValueError` → `return 1`) kills the whole 42-step run.

Two findings shape the fix:
- **Only spec 07 is empirically gapped.** It renders `scope_summary` (and `kind`) *inline with the position/division label* inside the citable inventory (`07-propose-responsibilities.md:26-34`), so they read as copyable. The other 13 specs render context too, but never as copy-targets — their "copy from this list" inventories present only admissible values (spec 13's `code (category): text`, only `code` cited, is the clean exemplar). This must be re-verified by the guard, not trusted.
- **Graceful-drop in the apply layer is rejected.** DEC-40 deliberately hardened the resolve hooks from lenient-drop to raise-on-miss; `planner/specs.py` is shared with production. Making a mis-slotted citation "droppable" would breach DEC-40 and diverge workshop from production. Genuine fabrication and structural failure must stay fatal (DEC-40/DEC-53).

So the fix is a single lever, made general: **the rendered citable surface of every spec must equal its parser-admissible set** — fix the one spec that violates it (07), and add a guard that enforces the invariant across all 14 so the gap cannot re-open in any spec. That closes the mis-pick at the source without touching the gate; after it, the only aborts are genuine fabrications, which DEC-40 correctly blocks. (Cross-grain citation of a *real* atom is a distinct, already-mitigated case: TASK-056 gave head-positions their own atom, and clearer per-grain rendering reduces it further; a residual cross-grain mis-cite is a genuine grounding-integrity error DEC-40 rightly catches.)

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] Spec 07's responsibility inventory presents only parser-admissible values as copy-targets: the division/position `scope_summary` and `kind` no longer appear as copyable items — they are omitted from the citable list or rendered as unambiguous context the instruction states is never copied.
- [ ] A deterministic guard verifies, for every one of the 14 specs, that each value the rendered prompt presents in a "copy exactly from this list" inventory is in that spec's parser-admissible catalogue; the guard passes for all 14 after the spec-07 fix, and fails if any spec presents a non-admissible value as citable.
- [ ] A full workshop generation no longer aborts at spec 07 on a context-value (scope_summary / kind) mis-citation.
- [ ] The DEC-40 catalogue/fabrication gate, the resolve-hooks' raise-on-miss, and the apply hard-abort are unchanged; genuine fabrication and structural failure remain fatal; the workshop and production share the identical parser/resolve (no divergence).
- [ ] Edited snippet body and its `.workshopping` fragment render byte-identical (verify-render-parity 14/14, both cross-checks PASS); the edited fragment's content_hash is re-stamped.
- [ ] The project + workshop gate stays green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js; verify-render-parity; the new guard); the JSON output contract of spec 07 is unchanged (no added/removed keys).

## The fix

1. **Spec 07 render discipline** — edit `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md` (and its `.workshopping` `FRAG-intro-propose-responsibilities-*` fragment, lockstep + re-stamp + parity) so the responsibility inventory's copyable lines are the responsibility **statements** (under their owner label) only; the division/position `scope_summary` and `kind` are removed from the inventory or rendered in a syntactically distinct, explicitly-non-citable form (mirror spec 13's clean separation). The output contract (division/position/statement/rationale) is unchanged.
2. **The invariant guard** — add a deterministic check (sibling to `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py`) that, for each of the 14 specs, derives the parser-admissible catalogue (from the same `_catalogue_members` / `_*_under_*` helpers `planner/specs.py` uses) and asserts every value the rendered inventory presents as citable is in it — failing on any gap. Wire it into the gate (`make test-js`-adjacent / a `make` target / the workshop gate). This is the non-whack-a-mole guarantee: a future gap in any spec is caught by the guard, not by a failed run.
3. **Untouched (explicit):** `planner/specs.py` parse/resolve, `apply_from_substrate.py`'s hard-abort, and the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture — no graceful-drop, no gate relaxation.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline, Mode-C/B; own branch off main)

1. Settle + persist a DEC (the invariant: rendered-citable = parser-admissible, enforced by the guard; lever-2 rejected per DEC-40) to `seed-round-plan.json` + LOG.
2. Create the task branch off main (porcelain-clean first).
3. Pre-file the `.context` FGAP (the class defect, investigated) + task (these acceptance_criteria; `task_addresses_gap`); ids allocated clear of the other branches' (FGAP-047+, TASK-057+).
4. IMPL agent: the spec-07 render edit (+ fragment mirror + parity) and the guard. STOP on ambiguity.
5. Adversarial audit (separate agent): verify each criterion by id — spec 07 presents no non-admissible citable value; the guard passes 14/14 and actually fails on an injected gap; parity 14/14; specs.py/apply/gate byte-unchanged; output contract unchanged. Loop to zero.
6. Cascade: a confirming full run completes past spec 07 + `check_draft.py --strict` PASS; VER + edge + complete-task + close the FGAP + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows.
7. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- The new guard: passes for all 14 specs after the fix; demonstrably fails when a non-admissible value is injected into a spec's citable inventory (prove it catches the class).
- `verify-render-parity` → GATE 14/14, both cross-checks PASS.
- A full `run_workflow.mjs … --fresh --yes` (dev DB) completes 42/42 with no spec-07 abort; `check_draft.py --strict` PASS.
- `git diff main…HEAD` (non-.context): only `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-*.md`, `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`, and the new guard file — never `planner/specs.py`, `apply_from_substrate.py`, or the gate/schemas.
- Full gate green: manage.py check, ruff, mypy ., pytest, make test-js.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- One atomic step per turn; read-before-write; branch only from a porcelain-clean tree; `state.mjs` for decomposed JSON, `pi-context` for `.context`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context` (restore after any `.workshopping` context-switch, CTX-12/CTX-14).
- Snippet body + fragment land together (parity never committed red); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp` when shell-special chars present; cleanup via `git restore`/`rm`, never `git reset`.
- Do NOT weaken DEC-40 (fabrication gate / resolve raise-on-miss) or DEC-53 (structural blockers); no graceful-drop; no output-contract change.

## Critical files

- `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md` + its `.workshopping` `FRAG-intro-propose-responsibilities-*` fragment — the render-discipline edit.
- NEW guard, e.g. `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` (sibling to verify-render-parity) — the corpus-wide invariant check; wired into the gate.
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — the `_catalogue_members` / `_*_under_*` admissible-set helpers the guard reuses (read-only; unchanged).
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/apply_from_substrate.py` — the hard-abort path (read-only; unchanged).
- `context-migration/decomposed/seed-round-plan.json` — the new DEC (the citability invariant; lever-2-rejection rationale).

allowedPrompts.prompt: run manage.py/ruff/mypy/pytest/make test-js + the workshop dispatch scripts (verify-render-parity, the new citability guard, run_workflow.mjs, check_draft.py) under uv from school-improvement-plans/ against the dev DB
pi-context CLI writes (incl. context-switch to .workshopping and back) + state.mjs append/upsert for the DEC/FGAP/task/VER
git branch/switch/commit on the citability-invariant branch

- **Graceful-drop in the apply layer is rejected.** DEC-40 deliberately hardened the resolve hooks from lenient-drop to raise-on-miss; `planner/specs.py` is shared with production. Making a mis-slotted citation "droppable" would breach DEC-40 and diverge workshop from production. Genuine fabrication and structural failure must stay fatal (DEC-40/DEC-53).

So the fix is a single lever, made general: **the rendered citable surface of every spec must equal its parser-admissible set** — fix the one spec that violates it (07), and add a guard that enforces the invariant across all 14 so the gap cannot re-open in any spec. That closes the mis-pick at the source without touching the gate; after it, the only aborts are genuine fabrications, which DEC-40 correctly blocks. (Cross-grain citation of a *real* atom is a distinct, already-mitigated case: TASK-056 gave head-positions their own atom, and clearer per-grain rendering reduces it further; a residual cross-grain mis-cite is a genuine grounding-integrity error DEC-40 rightly catches.)

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] Spec 07's responsibility inventory presents only parser-admissible values as copy-targets: the division/position `scope_summary` and `kind` no longer appear as copyable items — they are omitted from the citable list or rendered as unambiguous context the instruction states is never copied.
- [ ] A deterministic guard verifies, for every one of the 14 specs, that each value the rendered prompt presents in a "copy exactly from this list" inventory is in that spec's parser-admissible catalogue; the guard passes for all 14 after the spec-07 fix, and fails if any spec presents a non-admissible value as citable.
- [ ] A full workshop generation no longer aborts at spec 07 on a context-value (scope_summary / kind) mis-citation.
- [ ] The DEC-40 catalogue/fabrication gate, the resolve-hooks' raise-on-miss, and the apply hard-abort are unchanged; genuine fabrication and structural failure remain fatal; the workshop and production share the identical parser/resolve (no divergence).
- [ ] Edited snippet body and its `.workshopping` fragment render byte-identical (verify-render-parity 14/14, both cross-checks PASS); the edited fragment's content_hash is re-stamped.
- [ ] The project + workshop gate stays green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js; verify-render-parity; the new guard); the JSON output contract of spec 07 is unchanged (no added/removed keys).

## The fix

1. **Spec 07 render discipline** — edit `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md` (and its `.workshopping` `FRAG-intro-propose-responsibilities-*` fragment, lockstep + re-stamp + parity) so the responsibility inventory's copyable lines are the responsibility **statements** (under their owner label) only; the division/position `scope_summary` and `kind` are removed from the inventory or rendered in a syntactically distinct, explicitly-non-citable form (mirror spec 13's clean separation). The output contract (division/position/statement/rationale) is unchanged.
2. **The invariant guard** — add a deterministic check (sibling to `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py`) that, for each of the 14 specs, derives the parser-admissible catalogue (from the same `_catalogue_members` / `_*_under_*` helpers `planner/specs.py` uses) and asserts every value the rendered inventory presents as citable is in it — failing on any gap. Wire it into the gate (`make test-js`-adjacent / a `make` target / the workshop gate). This is the non-whack-a-mole guarantee: a future gap in any spec is caught by the guard, not by a failed run.
3. **Untouched (explicit):** `planner/specs.py` parse/resolve, `apply_from_substrate.py`'s hard-abort, and the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture — no graceful-drop, no gate relaxation.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline, Mode-C/B; own branch off main)

1. Settle + persist a DEC (the invariant: rendered-citable = parser-admissible, enforced by the guard; lever-2 rejected per DEC-40) to `seed-round-plan.json` + LOG.
2. Create the task branch off main (porcelain-clean first).
3. Pre-file the `.context` FGAP (the class defect, investigated) + task (these acceptance_criteria; `task_addresses_gap`); ids allocated clear of the other branches' (FGAP-047+, TASK-057+).
4. IMPL agent: the spec-07 render edit (+ fragment mirror + parity) and the guard. STOP on ambiguity.
5. Adversarial audit (separate agent): verify each criterion by id — spec 07 presents no non-admissible citable value; the guard passes 14/14 and actually fails on an injected gap; parity 14/14; specs.py/apply/gate byte-unchanged; output contract unchanged. Loop to zero.
6. Cascade: a confirming full run completes past spec 07 + `check_draft.py --strict` PASS; VER + edge + complete-task + close the FGAP + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows.
7. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- The new guard: passes for all 14 specs after the fix; demonstrably fails when a non-admissible value is injected into a spec's citable inventory (prove it catches the class).
- `verify-render-parity` → GATE 14/14, both cross-checks PASS.
- A full `run_workflow.mjs … --fresh --yes` (dev DB) completes 42/42 with no spec-07 abort; `check_draft.py --strict` PASS.
- `git diff main…HEAD` (non-.context): only `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-*.md`, `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`, and the new guard file — never `planner/specs.py`, `apply_from_substrate.py`, or the gate/schemas.
- Full gate green: manage.py check, ruff, mypy ., pytest, make test-js.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- One atomic step per turn; read-before-write; branch only from a porcelain-clean tree; `state.mjs` for decomposed JSON, `pi-context` for `.context`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context` (restore after any `.workshopping` context-switch, CTX-12/CTX-14).
- Snippet body + fragment land together (parity never committed red); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp` when shell-special chars present; cleanup via `git restore`/`rm`, never `git reset`.
- Do NOT weaken DEC-40 (fabrication gate / resolve raise-on-miss) or DEC-53 (structural blockers); no graceful-drop; no output-contract change.

## Critical files

- `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md` + its `.workshopping` `FRAG-intro-propose-responsibilities-*` fragment — the render-discipline edit.
- NEW guard, e.g. `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` (sibling to verify-render-parity) — the corpus-wide invariant check; wired into the gate.
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — the `_catalogue_members` / `_*_under_*` admissible-set helpers the guard reuses (read-only; unchanged).
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/apply_from_substrate.py` — the hard-abort path (read-only; unchanged).
- `context-migration/decomposed/seed-round-plan.json` — the new DEC (the citability invariant; lever-2-rejection rationale).

The guard MUST: (1) pass for all 14 specs after your Part A fix; (2) FAIL when a non-admissible value (e.g. a scope_summary) is presented as a copy-target in a spec's inventory — prove this by a temporary injection in your own run, then revert it. Repo: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan

GOAL: design a single MACHINE-CHECKABLE rendering convention that, across all 14 prompt-workshop spec inventory blocks, makes "this rendered token is a copy-target the model must cite verbatim" vs "this is context, never copied" unambiguous — so a guard can mechanically verify it and no spec can present a non-admissible value as citable.

ESTABLISHED CONTEXT (do not re-derive; build on it):
- The class: the grounding emits contextual fields (scope_summary, kind, category, prose, description) beside citable identity fields (label, statement, code). A spec's "copy exactly from this list" inventory can render the superset, while the parser admits only the identity field at the exact owner/grain. The model copies a context value; the hard gate aborts the run.
- A prior analysis found: a pure field-path-membership check FALSE-POSITIVES on spec 13 (it legitimately renders code + name + category + text as `"{{code}}: {{name}}" ({{category}} — {{text}})`, but admits only code); and structural heuristics (quoted vs bare token; delimiter position) are INCONSISTENT across the 14 blocks (citable tokens are sometimes quoted, sometimes bare bullets like `- {{r.statement}}`), and a delimiter rule would have EXEMPTED spec-07's bug (its scope_summary sat after an em-dash). So no ad-hoc heuristic is sound; an explicit convention is required.

INVESTIGATE + DESIGN:
1. For EACH of the 14 snippets (prompt-workshop/snippets/*.md), read the inventory block(s) the spec tells the model to "copy from" / "cite from this list". Tabulate, per block: every interpolated grounding field ({{...}}) and whether it is a citable identity field (the model must copy it verbatim into output) or context (never copied). Cite the snippet file:line and the instruction line that establishes copy-from-this-list.
2. From that survey, propose ONE convention that the already-clean blocks (e.g. spec 13, spec 02-06, 08-14) mostly already satisfy, so disruption is minimal — e.g. "every citable token is rendered as a quoted `\"{{value}}\"`; context fields appear only inside parentheses or after an em-dash and are never quoted" OR a better rule the survey supports. The convention must be checkable by a program looking at the rendered text + knowing which grounding values are citable. State the convention precisely.
3. Per-block DELTA: for each of the 14 blocks, state whether it already conforms or needs an edit, and the exact minimal edit to conform (what moves from citable-form to context-form or vice versa). Spec 07 is the known violator (scope_summary/kind in the citable inventory).

input.content:
# Close the prompt↔parser citability gap corpus-wide via a parenthetical-context convention + an enforcing guard (DEC-56 / FGAP-047 / TASK-057)

## Context

Three workshop runs aborted at spec 07 (US-LLM-27) on one class: the model copied a value the prompt presents but the parser does not admit. Root cause (DEC-56, verified): the grounding emits contextual fields (scope_summary, kind, category, name-beyond-code, prose, notes) beside the citable identity fields (label, statement, code); a spec's "copy exactly from this list" inventory can render that superset, while the parser (planner/specs.py) admits only the exact identity field at the exact owner/grain. The model copies a context value; the hard catalogue-gate (apply → ValueError → return 1) aborts the 42-step run. Apply-layer graceful-drop is rejected (DEC-56: breaches DEC-40's resolve raise-on-miss). The only sound, non-whack-a-mole fix is to make the prompt present only admissible values as copy-targets, enforced corpus-wide by a guard.

The decisive design fact (grounded in the failures): a context value is safely non-citable only when rendered **inside parentheses** — spec 13's `"{{code}}: {{name}}" ({{category}} — {{text}})` parenthetical context has never been mis-copied, while spec 07's `- Division "{{label}}" — {{scope_summary}}` (an em-dash continuation, not parenthesised) was copied three times. An em-dash/colon continuation is NOT a safe context marker; only parentheses are.

The convention: **in every spec's copy-from-this-list inventory, context fields render only inside parentheses `(...)`; every grounding field rendered outside parentheses (the citable positions — quoted identities, bare-bullet labels/codes/statements) must be in that spec's parser-admissible set.** A deterministic guard enforces this across all 14 specs, so no spec can re-open the gap.

Outcome: a guard that mechanically proves every spec's inventory presents only admissible copy-targets; the violators it flags are conformed (context moved into parentheses); the workshop run no longer aborts at spec 07; the parser/apply/gate posture is unchanged.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] A deterministic guard, for each of the 14 specs, renders the prompt with path-encoding sentinels substituted for every grounding catalogue leaf, and asserts that every sentinel surfacing **outside parentheses** in the spec's copy-from-this-list inventory decodes to a field-path in that spec's parser-admissible set (derived from the same `_check_in_catalogue`/`_catalogue_members`/`_*_under_*` helpers the parser uses). It passes for all 14 after the edits and **fails when a non-admissible field is rendered in a non-parenthetical position** (proven by a temporary injection, then reverted).
- [ ] Every spec inventory conforms: all non-parenthetical (citable-position) rendered field-paths are parser-admissible; context fields (scope_summary, name-beyond-code, category, text, description, notes, kind) render only inside parentheses or are omitted. The guard's run — not a hand table — determines the full set of specs edited; the known violators are spec 07 (`scope_summary` after an em-dash) and spec 13 (`name` non-parenthetical inside the quoted `code: name`).
- [ ] A full workshop generation no longer aborts at spec 07 on a context-value mis-citation; `check_draft.py --strict` PASSES on the assembled draft.
- [ ] Each edited snippet body and its `.workshopping` fragment render byte-identical (verify-render-parity 14/14, both cross-checks PASS); each edited fragment's content_hash is re-stamped.
- [ ] No change to planner/specs.py parse/resolve, apply_from_substrate.py, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture, or any output contract (no added/removed keys); the workshop and production share the identical parser.
- [ ] The project + workshop gate stays green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, verify-render-parity, the new guard); the guard is wired into the gate.

## The fix

1. **The guard first** — `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` (sibling to verify-render-parity.py). Mechanism: for each spec, build live grounding, substitute each catalogue leaf with `SENTINEL_<field-path>`, render the assembled prompt (reuse the render harness), find surfaced sentinels and whether each sits inside `(...)` on its line; the non-parenthetical ones must decode to a field-path in the spec's admissible set (reuse the parser's catalogue helpers — do not duplicate catalogue logic). Run it to enumerate the actual violators (expected: 07, 13; the guard is authoritative over any hand survey).
2. **Conform each flagged spec** — edit the snippet inventory so context fields move inside parentheses (or are omitted): spec 07 `- Division "{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}` → drop the scope_summary or render it `(scope: {{d.scope_summary}})`; spec 13 `"{{s.code}}: {{s.name}}"` → `"{{s.code}}" ({{s.name}} — {{s.category}} — {{s.text}})`; plus any other spec the guard flags. Mirror each edited body byte-identical into its `.workshopping` fragment via `pi-context update-block-item --cwd .workshopping` behind a transient `context-switch` (CTX-12/CTX-14: `--to_previous` still needs `--target_dir`; restore the pointer to `.context`); re-stamp; verify-render-parity 14/14.
3. **Wire the guard into the gate** (a Makefile target alongside verify-render-parity / make test-js).
4. **No parser/apply/gate change** — the guard + the render edits are the whole fix; the catalogue/fabrication gate and resolve raise-on-miss stay (genuine fabrication stays fatal).

## Procedure (canonical pipeline; on branch seed-leader-head-accountability, already clean)

1. DEC-56 + FGAP-047 + TASK-057 are filed (committed d37573b). Update TASK-057's plan-of-record to this parenthetical-context convention (the acceptance criteria already bind the guard to all 14).
2. IMPL agent: build the guard; run it to get the violator set; conform each flagged inventory (snippet + `.workshopping` fragment, lockstep, parity); wire the guard into the gate. Self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity. Explicit staging.
3. Adversarial audit (separate agent): the guard is sound (passes 14/14; fails on an injected non-paren non-admissible field); every flagged spec conformed; parity 14/14; specs.py/apply/gate byte-unchanged; output contracts unchanged. Loop to zero.
4. Cascade: a confirming full run completes past spec 07 + check_draft --strict PASS; VER + verification_verifies_item edge + complete-task TASK-057 + close FGAP-047 + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows.
5. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- The guard: passes for all 14 specs after the edits; demonstrably fails when a non-admissible field is rendered non-parenthetically (inject, observe FAIL, revert).
- `verify-render-parity` → GATE 14/14, both cross-checks PASS, exit 0.
- A full `run_workflow.mjs … --fresh --yes` (dev DB) completes 42/42 with no spec-07 abort; `check_draft.py --strict` PASS.
- `git diff main…HEAD` (non-.context): only the edited `prompt-workshop/snippets/*.md`, `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`, the new guard, and the gate-wiring file — never `planner/specs.py`, `apply_from_substrate.py`, the gate/schemas.
- Full gate green: manage.py check, ruff, mypy ., pytest, make test-js.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- One atomic step per turn; read-before-write; branch only from a porcelain-clean tree; explicit staging (never `git add -A`); `state.mjs` for decomposed JSON, `pi-context` for `.context`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`.
- Snippet body + fragment land together (parity never committed red); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp` when shell-special chars present; cleanup via `git restore`/`git clean`, never `git reset`.
- Do NOT weaken DEC-40 (fabrication gate / resolve raise-on-miss) or DEC-53 (structural blockers); no graceful-drop; no output-contract change.

## Critical files

- NEW `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` — the sentinel-render guard (the corpus-wide invariant); reuses the render harness + the planner/specs.py catalogue helpers.
- `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md`, `13-propose-accreditation-standards.md`, and any other the guard flags — the inventory conform edits; mirrored to `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`.
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/render_from_substrate.py` + `verify-render-parity.py` — the render harness the guard reuses (read-only).
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — the parser admissible-set helpers (`_catalogue_members`, `_responsibility_statements_under_*`, `_position_labels_under`, per-spec `_check_in_catalogue`) the guard reuses (read-only; unchanged).
- the gate-wiring file (Makefile) — add the guard to the gate.

allowedPrompts.prompt: run the workshop dispatch scripts (verify-citability.py, verify-render-parity.py, render_from_substrate.py, run_workflow.mjs, check_draft.py) + the project gate (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js) under uv from school-improvement-plans/ against the dev DB
pi-context CLI writes incl. context-switch to .workshopping and back; state.mjs append/upsert for VER/cascade
git add (explicit paths) / commit on the seed-leader-head-accountability branch

The convention: **in every spec's copy-from-this-list inventory, context fields render only inside parentheses `(...)`; every grounding field rendered outside parentheses (the citable positions — quoted identities, bare-bullet labels/codes/statements) must be in that spec's parser-admissible set.** A deterministic guard enforces this across all 14 specs, so no spec can re-open the gap.

Outcome: a guard that mechanically proves every spec's inventory presents only admissible copy-targets; the violators it flags are conformed (context moved into parentheses); the workshop run no longer aborts at spec 07; the parser/apply/gate posture is unchanged.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] A deterministic guard, for each of the 14 specs, renders the prompt with path-encoding sentinels substituted for every grounding catalogue leaf, and asserts that every sentinel surfacing **outside parentheses** in the spec's copy-from-this-list inventory decodes to a field-path in that spec's parser-admissible set (derived from the same `_check_in_catalogue`/`_catalogue_members`/`_*_under_*` helpers the parser uses). It passes for all 14 after the edits and **fails when a non-admissible field is rendered in a non-parenthetical position** (proven by a temporary injection, then reverted).
- [ ] Every spec inventory conforms: all non-parenthetical (citable-position) rendered field-paths are parser-admissible; context fields (scope_summary, name-beyond-code, category, text, description, notes, kind) render only inside parentheses or are omitted. The guard's run — not a hand table — determines the full set of specs edited; the known violators are spec 07 (`scope_summary` after an em-dash) and spec 13 (`name` non-parenthetical inside the quoted `code: name`).
- [ ] A full workshop generation no longer aborts at spec 07 on a context-value mis-citation; `check_draft.py --strict` PASSES on the assembled draft.
- [ ] Each edited snippet body and its `.workshopping` fragment render byte-identical (verify-render-parity 14/14, both cross-checks PASS); each edited fragment's content_hash is re-stamped.
- [ ] No change to planner/specs.py parse/resolve, apply_from_substrate.py, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture, or any output contract (no added/removed keys); the workshop and production share the identical parser.
- [ ] The project + workshop gate stays green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, verify-render-parity, the new guard); the guard is wired into the gate.

## The fix

1. **The guard first** — `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` (sibling to verify-render-parity.py). Mechanism: for each spec, build live grounding, substitute each catalogue leaf with `SENTINEL_<field-path>`, render the assembled prompt (reuse the render harness), find surfaced sentinels and whether each sits inside `(...)` on its line; the non-parenthetical ones must decode to a field-path in the spec's admissible set (reuse the parser's catalogue helpers — do not duplicate catalogue logic). Run it to enumerate the actual violators (expected: 07, 13; the guard is authoritative over any hand survey).
2. **Conform each flagged spec** — edit the snippet inventory so context fields move inside parentheses (or are omitted): spec 07 `- Division "{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}` → drop the scope_summary or render it `(scope: {{d.scope_summary}})`; spec 13 `"{{s.code}}: {{s.name}}"` → `"{{s.code}}" ({{s.name}} — {{s.category}} — {{s.text}})`; plus any other spec the guard flags. Mirror each edited body byte-identical into its `.workshopping` fragment via `pi-context update-block-item --cwd .workshopping` behind a transient `context-switch` (CTX-12/CTX-14: `--to_previous` still needs `--target_dir`; restore the pointer to `.context`); re-stamp; verify-render-parity 14/14.
3. **Wire the guard into the gate** (a Makefile target alongside verify-render-parity / make test-js).
4. **No parser/apply/gate change** — the guard + the render edits are the whole fix; the catalogue/fabrication gate and resolve raise-on-miss stay (genuine fabrication stays fatal).

## Procedure (canonical pipeline; on branch seed-leader-head-accountability, already clean)

1. DEC-56 + FGAP-047 + TASK-057 are filed (committed d37573b). Update TASK-057's plan-of-record to this parenthetical-context convention (the acceptance criteria already bind the guard to all 14).
2. IMPL agent: build the guard; run it to get the violator set; conform each flagged inventory (snippet + `.workshopping` fragment, lockstep, parity); wire the guard into the gate. Self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity. Explicit staging.
3. Adversarial audit (separate agent): the guard is sound (passes 14/14; fails on an injected non-paren non-admissible field); every flagged spec conformed; parity 14/14; specs.py/apply/gate byte-unchanged; output contracts unchanged. Loop to zero.
4. Cascade: a confirming full run completes past spec 07 + check_draft --strict PASS; VER + verification_verifies_item edge + complete-task TASK-057 + close FGAP-047 + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows.
5. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- The guard: passes for all 14 specs after the edits; demonstrably fails when a non-admissible field is rendered non-parenthetically (inject, observe FAIL, revert).
- `verify-render-parity` → GATE 14/14, both cross-checks PASS, exit 0.
- A full `run_workflow.mjs … --fresh --yes` (dev DB) completes 42/42 with no spec-07 abort; `check_draft.py --strict` PASS.
- `git diff main…HEAD` (non-.context): only the edited `prompt-workshop/snippets/*.md`, `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`, the new guard, and the gate-wiring file — never `planner/specs.py`, `apply_from_substrate.py`, the gate/schemas.
- Full gate green: manage.py check, ruff, mypy ., pytest, make test-js.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- One atomic step per turn; read-before-write; branch only from a porcelain-clean tree; explicit staging (never `git add -A`); `state.mjs` for decomposed JSON, `pi-context` for `.context`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`.
- Snippet body + fragment land together (parity never committed red); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp` when shell-special chars present; cleanup via `git restore`/`git clean`, never `git reset`.
- Do NOT weaken DEC-40 (fabrication gate / resolve raise-on-miss) or DEC-53 (structural blockers); no graceful-drop; no output-contract change.

## Critical files

- NEW `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` — the sentinel-render guard (the corpus-wide invariant); reuses the render harness + the planner/specs.py catalogue helpers.
- `prompt-workshop/snippets/07-propose-responsibilities.md`, `13-propose-accreditation-standards.md`, and any other the guard flags — the inventory conform edits; mirrored to `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`.
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/render_from_substrate.py` + `verify-render-parity.py` — the render harness the guard reuses (read-only).
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — the parser admissible-set helpers (`_catalogue_members`, `_responsibility_statements_under_*`, `_position_labels_under`, per-spec `_check_in_catalogue`) the guard reuses (read-only; unchanged).
- the gate-wiring file (Makefile) — add the guard to the gate.

THE CLASS + THE CONVENTION (settled — do not re-derive):
The grounding emits context fields (scope_summary, kind, category, name-beyond-code, prose, notes, description) beside citable identity fields (label, statement, code). A spec's "copy exactly from this list" inventory can render the superset, but the parser (planner/specs.py) admits only the exact identity field at the exact owner/grain; the model copies a context value and the run aborts. The fix is a corpus-wide convention enforced by a guard:
**In every spec's copy-from-this-list inventory, context fields render ONLY inside parentheses `(...)`; every grounding field rendered OUTSIDE parentheses (the citable positions) must be in that spec's parser-admissible set.** An em-dash/colon continuation is NOT a safe context marker (that is exactly spec 07's bug — `- Division "{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}` was mis-copied); only parentheses are (spec 13's `({{category}} — {{text}})` context is never mis-copied).

=== STEP 1 — build the guard (do this FIRST; it determines the violator set) ===
New script `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py`, sibling to `verify-render-parity.py` (read that for the render harness: load_prompt_spec, build_grounding, Django Template.render). Mechanism:
