Render the assembled spec body with the sentinel-substituted grounding.
- Find every surfaced sentinel and whether it sits INSIDE parentheses `(...)` on its rendered line. The sentinels OUTSIDE parentheses are the citable-position field-paths.
- Build each spec's parser-admissible field-path set by REUSING planner/specs.py helpers (`_catalogue_members`, `_responsibility_statements_under_division`/`_under_position`, `_position_labels_under`, and the per-spec `_check_in_catalogue` calls) — do NOT duplicate catalogue logic; import/derive from the parser. Map each to its grounding field-path (section + identity field).
- ASSERT: every non-parenthetical surfaced field-path is in the spec's admissible set. Report violators as `spec | field-path | rendered line`. Exit nonzero on any violation.
Run it. It enumerates the actual violators (expected at least spec 07 scope_summary and spec 13 `name`, but the guard is authoritative — conform whatever it flags).

=== STEP 2 — conform each flagged spec ===
For each spec the guard flags, edit its snippet inventory so the non-admissible field moves INSIDE parentheses or is omitted, leaving only admissible values in citable (non-paren) positions. E.g. spec 07: drop `— {{d.scope_summary}}` or render `(scope: {{d.scope_summary}})`; spec 13: `"{{s.code}}: {{s.name}}"` → `"{{s.code}}" ({{s.name}} — {{s.category}} — {{s.text}})`. Do NOT change any output contract or instruction meaning. For EACH edited snippet, mirror the body byte-identical into its `.workshopping` fragment: read the .workshopping prompt-fragment block to find the fragment carrying the edited bytes, `pi-context context-switch --target_dir .workshopping`, `update-block-item --block prompt-fragment --arrayKey fragments --match '{"id":"FRAG-..."}' --updates @/tmp/x.json` (re-stamps content_hash), then `pi-context context-switch --to_previous --target_dir .context`; confirm `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`.
Verify parity: from school-improvement-plans/, `DATABASE_URL=<dsn> uv run python ../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-render-parity.py` → GATE 14/14, both cross-checks PASS, exit 0.
Re-run the guard → must now pass all 14.

=== STEP 3 — prove the guard catches the class + wire it into the gate ===
Temporarily inject a non-admissible field into a non-parenthetical position in one spec; run the guard → it MUST fail and name that field-path; revert the injection. Wire the guard into the gate (a Makefile target alongside verify-render-parity / make test-js — match existing wiring).

=== CONSTRAINTS ===
Touch ONLY: the new guard script, the flagged `prompt-workshop/snippets/*.md`, their `.workshopping` fragments (+ .workshopping/objects), and the gate-wiring file. Do NOT change planner/specs.py, apply_from_substrate.py, .workflows/*, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture, or any output contract. `.pi-context.json` ends on .context. No `git add -A`.

=== VERIFY (observed-green before commit) ===

- full gate from school-improvement-plans/ under uv + DSN: manage.py check; ruff check .; mypy .; pytest; make test-js. All green.

=== COMMIT ===
After all green, commit on the branch via `git commit -F /tmp/<msg>` (forensic; aims not certainties; no author-credit). Stage EXPLICIT paths.

=== REPORT BACK ===
The guard's design (sentinel encoding, paren detection, admissible-set derivation); the violator set it found; the conform edits per spec (snippet diff + fragment id); parity output; the injection-failure proof; the gate results per command; the commit SHA; `git status` (clean). cd /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/school-improvement-plans && DATABASE_URL="postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/school_improvement_plans" uv run python - <<'PY'
import sys, re, json
from pathlib import Path
WS = Path('/Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan/prompt-workshop')
sys.path.insert(0, str(WS))
from dispatch._workshop import (_PARSE_FN_NAMES, assemble_spec_body, flatten_draft_for_grounding,
    get_tenant_school, load_draft, load_fragments, load_prompt_spec, setup_django)
setup_django()
from django.template import Context, Template
from ai.services.grounding import build_grounding
from ai.services.prompt_sanitizer import PromptSanitizer

school = get_tenant_school(); draft = load_draft()
flat = flatten_draft_for_grounding(draft); seed = (draft.get("meta") or {}).get("seed_text","") or ""
fragments = load_fragments()

# admissible (section, identity) per spec — derived from parser _check_in_catalogue call sites
ADM = {
 "narrative-draft": set(),
 "propose-domain-alignment": {("learner_outcomes","label"),("stakeholder_groups","label"),("areas_for_improvement","label"),("policies","label")},
 "propose-milestones": {("improvement_types","code"),("planning_methods","name")},
 "draft-success-criteria": set(),
 "decompose-action-steps": {("planning_methods","name")},
 "propose-assignments": {("divisions","label")},
 "propose-responsibilities": {("division_responsibility_atoms","label")},  # + nested handled below
 "propose-timelines": set(),
 "propose-step-resources": set(),
 "propose-evidence": {("divisions","label")},
 "suggest-feedback-channels": {("stakeholder_groups","label"),("divisions","label"),("frequencies","code")},
 "bind-measurement-channels": set(),
 "propose-accreditation-standards": {("accreditation_standards","code")},
 "propose-review-loop": {("stakeholder_groups","label"),("divisions","label"),("frequencies","code")},
}

def sentinelize(obj, path):
    if isinstance(obj, dict):
        return {k: sentinelize(v, path+[k]) for k,v in obj.items()}
    if isinstance(obj, list):
        return [sentinelize(v, path+[str(i)]) for i,v in enumerate(obj)]
    if isinstance(obj, str):
        return "SENT__" + "__".join(path)
    return obj

SENT_RE = re.compile(r"SENT__[A-Za-z0-9_]+")
# paren detection: is the sentinel inside (...) on its rendered line
def outside_paren(line, m):
    s=m.start(); e=m.end()
    before=line[:s]; after=line[e:]
    depth_before = before.count("(")-before.count(")")
    return depth_before<=0  # not currently inside an open paren

import collections
report=collections.OrderedDict()
for spec_key in _PARSE_FN_NAMES:
    spec=load_prompt_spec(spec_key)
    g=build_grounding(school, draft_state=flat, include=spec.get("grounding_sections") or None)
    g=PromptSanitizer.sanitize_data_dict(g)
    # sentinelize only catalogue sections (lists/dicts)
    g2={}
    for k,v in g.items():
        g2[k]=sentinelize(v,[k])
    g2["draft_state"]=g.get("draft_state")  # leave draft as-is
    body=assemble_spec_body(spec,fragments)
    rendered=Template(body).render(Context({**g2,"seed":seed},autoescape=False))
    viol=[]
    for line in rendered.splitlines():
        for m in SENT_RE.finditer(line):
            tok=m.group(0)[len("SENT__"):]
            parts=tok.split("__")
            section=parts[0]
            identity=parts[-1]
            # field path identity = last key
            if outside_paren(line,m):
                pair=(section,identity)
                # responsibilities nested admissibility: statement under positions/divisions
                adm=ADM[spec_key]
                ok = pair in adm
                if spec_key=="propose-responsibilities":
                    if identity in ("label","statement"): ok=True  # nested grain admissible
                if not ok:
                    viol.append((section,identity,line.strip()[:90]))
    if viol:
        report[spec_key]=viol

for sk,v in report.items():
    print("====",sk,"====")
    seen=set()
    for s,i,ln in v:
        key=(s,i)
        if key in seen: continue
        seen.add(key)
        print(f"  {s}.{i}  |  {ln}")
PY

A naive "every non-parenthetical grounding field rendered must be parser-admissible" predicate OVER-FLAGS ~80 field-paths because it conflates two distinct gates: the STRUCTURED parser (`_check_in_catalogue` in specs.py — a value COPIED VERBATIM into a structured output field) vs the FREE-TEXT audit (`_freetext_audit.py` — entity names cited in prose rationale). school.name, cycle, and reference-catalogue labels cited only in free-text are NOT structured-parser citations and must NOT be flagged by this guard.

DERIVE AND SPECIFY (all from source, file:line):
1. THE TWO GATES — from specs.py and _freetext_audit.py, define precisely what the STRUCTURED parser validates (the `_check_in_catalogue`/`_catalogue_members`/`_*_under_*` sites that gate a value copied INTO a structured output field) vs what the free-text audit governs (entity names in prose). The guard's concern is ONLY the structured-parser citations.
2. PER-SPEC STRUCTURED-ADMISSIBLE MAP — for each of the 14 specs, the grounding section(s) whose values the parser checks via `_check_in_catalogue` and the exact admissible identity field-path(s) within each (e.g. spec 13: accreditation_standards→code; spec 07: division_responsibility_atoms→label, responsibilities[].statement, positions[].label, positions[].responsibilities[].statement; specs with no structured catalogue citation → out-of-scope). Cite each call site.
3. THE PREDICATE — the guard's exact rule, scoped to (2): for each spec, within the rendered inventory, for each grounding section that spec STRUCTURALLY checks, every field of that section rendered in a citable (non-parenthetical) position must be the admissible identity field-path; any OTHER field of that section (its context fields) rendered non-parenthetically is a violation. Sections the spec does not structurally check are out of scope. Define "citable (non-parenthetical) position" MECHANICALLY (a sentinel-substituted render — replace each grounding leaf with a path-encoding sentinel, render, detect whether each surfaced sentinel sits inside `(...)` on its line) so it is unambiguous and implementable.
4. THE COMPLETE VIOLATOR SET — apply the predicate (render the 14 specs against the dev DB grounding to confirm) and list EVERY spec+field-path that violates it, with the snippet file:line. Confirm whether it is exactly {spec 07 scope_summary, spec 13 name} or more.
5. PER-VIOLATOR CONFORM EDIT — the exact minimal snippet edit that conforms each (move the context field into parentheses, or omit) WITHOUT changing the output contract or instruction meaning.
6. EDGE CASES the implementer must handle (e.g. `name` is a context field for accreditation_standards but the identity field for planning_methods — disambiguate by section; bare-bullet vs quoted citable tokens; multi-grain sections; the guard's reuse of specs.py helpers vs duplicating catalogue logic).

REPORT tightly: the two-gate distinction, the per-spec structured-admissible map, the precise mechanical predicate, the confirmed complete violator set, the per-violator conform edit, the edge cases, the gate-wiring point — all file:line-cited. Do not write guard code or edit files.

input.content:
# Close the prompt↔parser citability gap corpus-wide: a structurally-scoped guard + 4 conform edits (DEC-56 / FGAP-047 / TASK-057)

## Context

Three workshop runs aborted at spec 07 (US-LLM-27): the model copied a value the prompt presents in a citable position but the STRUCTURED parser does not admit. Root cause (DEC-56): the grounding emits context fields (scope_summary, name, category, kind, prose) beside the citable identity fields (label, statement, code); a spec's "copy exactly from this list" inventory can render a context field where the model treats it as copyable; the hard catalogue-gate aborts the 42-step run. Apply-layer graceful-drop is rejected (DEC-56: breaches DEC-40's resolve raise-on-miss).

This plan's design is agent-derived from source (not authored), and the load-bearing facts corroborated against current source:

- **Two distinct gates.** The STRUCTURED parser (`planner/specs.py` `_check_in_catalogue`/`_catalogue_members`/`_*_under_*`) gates a value COPIED VERBATIM into a structured output field. The FREE-TEXT audit (`planner/_freetext_audit.py`) governs entity names cited in prose rationale. `school.name`, cycle, and reference-catalogue labels cited only in free-text are NOT structured-parser citations. The guard's concern is ONLY the structured-parser citations — scoping it to "every rendered field" over-flags ~80 paths and is wrong.
- **The predicate (scoped).** For each spec, for each grounding section that spec STRUCTURALLY checks via `_check_in_catalogue`, every field of that section rendered in a citable (non-parenthetical) position must be the admissible identity field-path; any OTHER field of that section (a context field) rendered non-parenthetically is a violation. Sections the spec does not structurally check are out of scope. Parenthetical context `(...)` is safe (spec 13's parenthetical context never mis-copied); an em-dash/colon continuation is NOT (it is the spec-07 bug).
- **The complete violator set is 4** (corroborated against the snippet source): spec 06 (`06-propose-assignments.md:26` `"{{division.label}}" — {{division.scope_summary}}`, divisions checked), spec 07 (`07-propose-responsibilities.md:27` `Division "{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}`, atoms/divisions checked), spec 10 (`10-propose-evidence.md` divisions block `"{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}`, divisions checked), spec 13 (`13-propose-accreditation-standards.md:28` `"{{s.code}}: {{s.name}}"`, accreditation_standards checked, admits code only). Each is one line.

Outcome: a deterministic guard that proves, corpus-wide, that no structurally-checked section renders a context field in a citable position; the 4 violators conformed; the workshop run no longer aborts at spec 07; the parser/apply/gate posture unchanged.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] A deterministic guard (`prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py`), for each of the 14 specs, renders the prompt with path-encoding sentinels substituted for each grounding leaf, detects which surfaced sentinels sit OUTSIDE parentheses, and — restricted to the grounding sections that spec structurally checks via `_check_in_catalogue` (reusing the specs.py helpers; not duplicating catalogue logic) — asserts every non-parenthetical surfaced field-path of those sections is the admissible identity field-path. It passes for all 14 after the edits and FAILS when a context field of a checked section is moved into a non-parenthetical position (proven by a temporary injection, reverted).
- [ ] The 4 violators (specs 06, 07, 10, 13) are conformed: the context field (`scope_summary`/`name`) moves inside parentheses; only the admissible identity (`label`/`code`) remains in the citable position. No output contract or instruction meaning changes.
- [ ] A full workshop generation no longer aborts at spec 07 on a context-value mis-citation; `check_draft.py --strict` PASSES on the assembled draft.
- [ ] Each edited snippet body and its `.workshopping` fragment render byte-identical (verify-render-parity 14/14, both cross-checks PASS); each edited fragment's content_hash re-stamped.
- [ ] No change to `planner/specs.py` parse/resolve, `apply_from_substrate.py`, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture, or any output contract; workshop and production share the identical parser.
- [ ] Project + workshop gate green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, verify-render-parity, the new guard); the guard wired into the gate.

## The fix

1. **Build the guard** `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` — sentinel-substituted render (reuse `render_from_substrate.py`'s harness) + paren-detection; per spec, derive the structurally-checked sections + admissible identity field-paths by reusing `planner/specs.py` `_catalogue_members`/`_*_under_*` (import/derive — do not re-encode catalogues); assert per the scoped predicate. Run it to confirm the violator set (expected 06/07/10/13; the guard is authoritative).
2. **Conform the 4** — `— {{scope_summary}}` → `({{scope_summary}})` in 06/07/10; `"{{s.code}}: {{s.name}}"` → `"{{s.code}}" ({{s.name}} — {{s.category}} — {{s.text}})` in 13. For each, mirror the body byte-identical into its `.workshopping` fragment via `pi-context update-block-item --cwd .workshopping` behind a transient `context-switch` (CTX-12/14: `--to_previous` needs `--target_dir`; restore pointer to `.context`); re-stamp; verify-render-parity 14/14.
3. **Wire the guard into the gate** (a Makefile `test-citability` target alongside test-js / verify-render-parity).
4. **Untouched (explicit):** specs.py parse/resolve, apply_from_substrate.py, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate; no graceful-drop; no output-contract change.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline; branch seed-leader-head-accountability, clean)

1. DEC-56/FGAP-047/TASK-057 filed (committed d37573b); this plan is TASK-057's grounded plan-of-record.
2. IMPL agent: build the guard; run it to confirm the violators; conform each (snippet + `.workshopping` fragment lockstep, parity); wire into the gate. Self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity; explicit staging.
3. Adversarial audit (separate agent): guard sound (passes 14/14; fails on an injected context-field-in-citable-position); all flagged specs conformed; parity 14/14; specs.py/apply/gate byte-unchanged; output contracts unchanged. Loop to zero.
4. Cascade: confirming full run completes past spec 07 + check_draft --strict PASS; VER + verification_verifies_item edges + complete-task TASK-056 AND TASK-057 + close FGAP-046/047 + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows + refresh the stale seq-6 focus item.
5. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- The guard passes 14/14 after edits; demonstrably fails on an injection (then reverted).
- `verify-render-parity` → GATE 14/14, exit 0.
- Full `run_workflow.mjs … --fresh --yes` (dev DB) completes 42/42 with no spec-07 abort; `check_draft.py --strict` PASS.
- `git diff main…HEAD` (non-.context): only `prompt-workshop/snippets/0{6,7}-*.md` + `10-*.md` + `13-*.md`, `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`, the new guard, the Makefile — never `planner/specs.py`, `apply_from_substrate.py`, gate/schemas.
- Full gate green; independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline (the failures this must not repeat)

- Investigate before any structural/destructive act; branch only from a porcelain-clean tree with a directive; explicit staging (never `git add -A`).
- Derive specs via agents from source + corroborate; never author them. Execute the mandate-determined path; never float a derivable choice.
- Snippet body + fragment land together (parity never red); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp` for shell-special chars; cleanup via `git restore`/`git clean`, never `git reset`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`.
- Do NOT weaken DEC-40 / DEC-53; no graceful-drop; no output-contract change. Close the cascade as part of the work (read the spine back).

## Critical files

- NEW `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` — the structurally-scoped sentinel-render guard; reuses the render harness + specs.py catalogue helpers.
- `prompt-workshop/snippets/06-propose-assignments.md`, `07-propose-responsibilities.md`, `10-propose-evidence.md`, `13-propose-accreditation-standards.md` — the 4 one-line conform edits; mirrored to `.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json`.
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/render_from_substrate.py` + `verify-render-parity.py` — render harness the guard reuses (read-only).
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — the admissible-set helpers the guard reuses (read-only; unchanged); `planner/_freetext_audit.py` — the other gate (out of the guard's scope).
- `school-improvement-plans/Makefile` — add the `test-citability` gate target.

allowedPrompts.prompt: run the workshop dispatch scripts (verify-citability.py, verify-render-parity.py, render_from_substrate.py, run_workflow.mjs, check_draft.py) + the project gate (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js / make test-citability) under uv from school-improvement-plans/ against the dev DB
pi-context CLI writes incl. context-switch to .workshopping and back; state.mjs append/upsert for VER/cascade/LOG
git add (explicit paths) / commit on the seed-leader-head-accountability branch

The FREE-TEXT audit (`planner/_freetext_audit.py`) governs entity names cited in prose rationale. `school.name`, cycle, and reference-catalogue labels cited only in free-text are NOT structured-parser citations. The guard's concern is ONLY the structured-parser citations — scoping it to "every rendered field" over-flags ~80 paths and is wrong.
- **The predicate (scoped).** For each spec, for each grounding section that spec STRUCTURALLY checks via `_check_in_catalogue`, every field of that section rendered in a citable (non-parenthetical) position must be the admissible identity field-path; any OTHER field of that section (a context field) rendered non-parenthetically is a violation. Sections the spec does not structurally check are out of scope. Parenthetical context `(...)` is safe (spec 13's parenthetical context never mis-copied); an em-dash/colon continuation is NOT (it is the spec-07 bug).
- **The complete violator set is 4** (corroborated against the snippet source): spec 06 (`06-propose-assignments.md:26` `"{{division.label}}" — {{division.scope_summary}}`, divisions checked), spec 07 (`07-propose-responsibilities.md:27` `Division "{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}`, atoms/divisions checked), spec 10 (`10-propose-evidence.md` divisions block `"{{d.label}}" — {{d.scope_summary}}`, divisions checked), spec 13 (`13-propose-accreditation-standards.md:28` `"{{s.code}}: {{s.name}}"`, accreditation_standards checked, admits code only). Each is one line.

Outcome: a deterministic guard that proves, corpus-wide, that no structurally-checked section renders a context field in a citable position; the 4 violators conformed; the workshop run no longer aborts at spec 07; the parser/apply/gate posture unchanged.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] A deterministic guard (`prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py`), for each of the 14 specs, renders the prompt with path-encoding sentinels substituted for each grounding leaf, detects which surfaced sentinels sit OUTSIDE parentheses, and — restricted to the grounding sections that spec structurally checks via `_check_in_catalogue` (reusing the specs.py helpers; not duplicating catalogue logic) — asserts every non-parenthetical surfaced field-path of those sections is the admissible identity field-path. It passes for all 14 after the edits and FAILS when a context field of a checked section is moved into a non-parenthetical position (proven by a temporary injection, reverted).
- [ ] The 4 violators (specs 06, 07, 10, 13) are conformed: the context field (`scope_summary`/`name`) moves inside parentheses; only the admissible identity (`label`/`code`) remains in the citable position. No output contract or instruction meaning changes.
- [ ] A full workshop generation no longer aborts at spec 07 on a context-value mis-citation; `check_draft.py --strict` PASSES on the assembled draft.
- [ ] Each edited snippet body and its `.workshopping` fragment render byte-identical (verify-render-parity 14/14, both cross-checks PASS); each edited fragment's content_hash re-stamped.
- [ ] No change to `planner/specs.py` parse/resolve, `apply_from_substrate.py`, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate posture, or any output contract; workshop and production share the identical parser.
- [ ] Project + workshop gate green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, verify-render-parity, the new guard); the guard wired into the gate.

4. **Untouched (explicit):** specs.py parse/resolve, apply_from_substrate.py, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate; no graceful-drop; no output-contract change.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline; branch seed-leader-head-accountability, clean)

2. IMPL agent: build the guard; run it to confirm the violators; conform each (snippet + `.workshopping` fragment lockstep, parity); wire into the gate. Self-contained directive; STOP on ambiguity; explicit staging.

input.content:
"""Citability guard (TASK-057 / DEC-56 / FGAP-047).

A grounding catalogue section that a spec STRUCTURALLY checks (via
`planner.specs._check_in_catalogue` and the responsibilities-only nested
helpers) has exactly ONE admissible identity field-path — the field the
parser verifies a value against. The LLM cites a row of such a section by
copying that identity. If the snippet renders ANY OTHER field of that
section in a CITABLE (non-parenthetical) position, the model is invited to
copy a value the parser will reject — a structural foot-gun (the class
DEC-56 closes). Parenthetical `(...)` context is safe (it reads as a gloss,
not a value-to-copy); an em-dash / colon continuation is not.

Mechanism (sentinel render-probe, NOT template parsing):

  For each of the 14 specs build the live grounding (the spec's
  `grounding_sections`), then DEEP-REPLACE every catalogue LEAF with a
  path-encoding sentinel string:

      grounding["divisions"][0]["label"]        -> "SENTINEL__divisions__label"
      grounding["divisions"][0]["scope_summary"]-> "SENTINEL__divisions__scope_summary"
      grounding["division_responsibility_atoms"][0]["positions"][0]["label"]
                                                -> "SENTINEL__division_responsibility_atoms__positions__label"

  (List indices are collapsed; the sentinel encodes section + dotted
  field-path. Several rows therefore carry identical sentinels — that is
  fine, we only care WHICH paths surface citably, not how many rows.)

  Render the assembled spec body against that sentinel grounding. Scan the
  rendered text line by line; each surfaced `SENTINEL__<section>__<path>`
  whose occurrence is NOT inside a `(...)` group on its line is a
  field-path rendered in a citable position.

  Restrict to the sections the spec STRUCTURALLY checks + their admissible
  identity field-path(s). That checked-section -> admissible-path map is
  DERIVED from `planner.specs` (not re-encoded here): we read each parse
  function's source and pull every

      _catalogue_members(grounding, "<section>", identity="<id>")

  call (-> section's admissible top-level path is `<id>`), plus the three
  responsibilities-only nested helpers, whose validated paths are their
  documented contract:

      _responsibility_statements_under_division  -> responsibilities.statement
      _position_labels_under                      -> positions.label
      _responsibility_statements_under_position  -> positions.responsibilities.statement

  A spec with no structured catalogue citation has an empty checked-section
  set -> nothing to flag (its free-text fields are governed by
  `planner._freetext_audit`, not this guard).

  ASSERT: for each checked section, every non-parenthetical surfaced
  field-path under that section equals the admissible identity field-path.
  Any other (context) field of a checked section in citable position FAILS,
  naming spec | field-path | rendered line. Exits non-zero on any violation.

Read-only against the substrate data files, the snippets, and the dev DB
grounding; mutates nothing.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import inspect
import re
import sys
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

# Resolve workshop helpers via the local package path; the helpers add
# Django to sys.path so the production imports work transparently.
sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).parent.parent))

from dispatch._workshop import (  # noqa: E402
    _PARSE_FN_NAMES,
    assemble_spec_body,
    flatten_draft_for_grounding,
    get_parse_function,
    get_tenant_school,
    load_draft,
    load_fragments,
    load_prompt_spec,
    load_snippet,
    setup_django,
)

# Sentinel prefix + path separator. The separator is a token that cannot
# appear in a section key or a Python identifier field name, so the
# decode (split on the separator) is unambiguous.
_SENTINEL_PREFIX = "SENTINEL"
_SEP = "__"

# Detector for the spec's `_catalogue_members` call sites in the parse
# source: `_catalogue_members(grounding, "<section>", identity="<id>")`.
_CATALOGUE_MEMBERS_RE = re.compile(
    r"_catalogue_members\(\s*grounding\s*,\s*"
    r'"(?P<section>[^"]+)"\s*,\s*identity="(?P<identity>[^"]+)"'
)

# The three responsibilities-only nested helpers and the (section,
# field-path) each one validates — its documented contract. Detected by
# presence of the helper name in the parse source.
_NESTED_HELPER_PATHS: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
    "_responsibility_statements_under_division": (
        "division_responsibility_atoms",
        "responsibilities.statement",
    ),
    "_position_labels_under": (
        "division_responsibility_atoms",
        "positions.label",
    ),
    "_responsibility_statements_under_position": (
        "division_responsibility_atoms",
        "positions.responsibilities.statement",
    ),
}


def _sentinelize(value: Any, section: str, path: tuple[str, ...]) -> Any:
    """Return a deep copy of `value` with every string/scalar leaf replaced
    by a path-encoding sentinel string. Dict keys are preserved; list
    indices are collapsed (each element re-uses the same path). Non-leaf
    containers recurse. Empty containers stay empty (no leaf -> no
    sentinel)."""
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return {k: _sentinelize(v, section, path + (k,)) for k, v in value.items()}
    if isinstance(value, list):
        return [_sentinelize(v, section, path) for v in value]
    # Leaf (str / int / float / bool / None). Encode section + dotted path.
    return _SEP.join((_SENTINEL_PREFIX, section, ".".join(path)))


def _surfaced_sentinels_by_line(rendered: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
    """Return `(section_dot_path, line)` for every sentinel occurrence whose
    position on its rendered line is NOT inside a `(...)` group.

    Paren-membership is computed per line by a left-to-right scan of round
    brackets: a character is "inside parens" when the running open-paren
    depth at its position is > 0."""
    token_re = re.compile(
        re.escape(_SENTINEL_PREFIX + _SEP) + r"([A-Za-z0-9_.]+)"
    )
    out: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
    for line in rendered.splitlines():
        # Precompute, for each char index, whether it sits inside parens.
        depth = 0
        inside: list[bool] = []
        for ch in line:
            if ch == "(":
                depth += 1
                inside.append(True)  # the "(" itself counts as inside
            elif ch == ")":
                inside.append(depth > 0)
                depth = max(0, depth - 1)
            else:
                inside.append(depth > 0)
        for m in token_re.finditer(line):
            start = m.start()
            if start < len(inside) and inside[start]:
                continue  # parenthetical context — safe
            # Decode `<section>__<dotted.path>`: section is the first
            # segment, the remainder (joined) is the dotted field-path.
            decoded = m.group(0)[len(_SENTINEL_PREFIX + _SEP) :]
            out.append((decoded, line))
    return out


def _decode(section_dot_path: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Split a decoded sentinel body `<section>__<dotted.path>` into
    `(section, dotted_path)`. Section is everything up to the first `__`;
    the remainder is the dotted field-path."""
    section, _, dotted = section_dot_path.partition(_SEP)
    return section, dotted


def _checked_sections_for(spec_key: str) -> dict[str, set[str]]:
    """Derive `{section: {admissible_field_path, ...}}` from the parse
    function's source in `planner.specs`. Empty dict when the spec checks
    no structured catalogue."""
    src = inspect.getsource(get_parse_function(spec_key))
    out: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
    for m in _CATALOGUE_MEMBERS_RE.finditer(src):
        out.setdefault(m.group("section"), set()).add(m.group("identity"))
    for helper, (section, fieldpath) in _NESTED_HELPER_PATHS.items():
        if helper in src:
            out.setdefault(section, set()).add(fieldpath)
    return out


def main() -> int:
    setup_django()

    from django.template import Context, Template  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

    from ai.services.grounding import build_grounding  # type: ignore[import-not-found]
    from ai.services.prompt_sanitizer import PromptSanitizer  # type: ignore[import-not-found]

    school = get_tenant_school()
    draft = load_draft()
    flat_draft_state = flatten_draft_for_grounding(draft)
    seed = (draft.get("meta") or {}).get("seed_text", "") or ""

    fragments = load_fragments()
    spec_keys = list(_PARSE_FN_NAMES.keys())

    passes = 0
    violations: list[str] = []

    for spec_key in spec_keys:
        frontmatter, _ = load_snippet(spec_key)
        spec = load_prompt_spec(spec_key)

        checked = _checked_sections_for(spec_key)

        grounding = build_grounding(
            school,
            draft_state=flat_draft_state,
            include=frontmatter.get("grounding_sections") or None,
        )
        grounding = PromptSanitizer.sanitize_data_dict(grounding)

        # Replace every leaf of each CHECKED section with a path-encoding
        # sentinel. (Unchecked sections are out of scope, so leaving them
        # as their real values is harmless — their leaves never carry the
        # SENTINEL prefix, so the scan ignores them.)
        sentinel_grounding = dict(grounding)
        for section in checked:
            if section in sentinel_grounding:
                sentinel_grounding[section] = _sentinelize(
                    sentinel_grounding[section], section, ()
                )

        context = {**sentinel_grounding, "seed": seed}
        body = assemble_spec_body(spec, fragments)
        rendered = Template(body).render(Context(context, autoescape=False))

        spec_violations: list[str] = []
        for decoded, line in _surfaced_sentinels_by_line(rendered):
            section, fieldpath = _decode(decoded)
            if section not in checked:
                continue  # not a structurally-checked section -> out of scope
            admissible = checked[section]
            if fieldpath in admissible:
                continue  # the admissible identity field-path -> citable OK
            spec_violations.append(
                f"  {spec_key} | section={section} | field-path={fieldpath!r} "
                f"(admissible={sorted(admissible)}) | line: {line.strip()}"
            )

        if spec_violations:
            print(f"FAIL  {spec_key}")
            # De-dup identical lines (a section iterates >1 row -> repeats).
            for v in sorted(set(spec_violations)):
                print(v)
            violations.extend(sorted(set(spec_violations)))
        else:
            passes += 1
            print(f"PASS  {spec_key}")

    total = len(spec_keys)
    print()
    print(f"GATE: {passes}/{total}")
    if violations:
        print(f"\n{len(violations)} citability violation(s):")
        for v in violations:
            print(v)
        return 1
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

python3 << 'EOF'
import json
data=json.load(open('.workshopping/prompt-fragment.json'))
frags={it['id']:it for it in data['fragments']}

edits = {
 'FRAG-gb-divisions-labels-only': [
   ('{% if division.scope_summary %} — {{ division.scope_summary }}{% endif %}',
    '{% if division.scope_summary %} ({{ division.scope_summary }}){% endif %}'),
 ],
 'FRAG-gb-divisions-inventory': [
   ('{% if d.scope_summary %} — {{ d.scope_summary }}{% endif %}',
    '{% if d.scope_summary %} ({{ d.scope_summary }}){% endif %}'),
 ],
 'FRAG-gb-divisions-owner': [
   ('{% if d.scope_summary %} — {{ d.scope_summary }}{% endif %}',
    '{% if d.scope_summary %} ({{ d.scope_summary }}){% endif %}'),
 ],
 'FRAG-gb-accreditation-standards-choose': [
   ('- "{{ s.code }}: {{ s.name }}" ({{ s.category }} — {{ s.text }})',
    '- "{{ s.code }}" ({{ s.name }} — {{ s.category }} — {{ s.text }})'),
 ],
 'FRAG-gb-planning-methods-recipes': [
   ('{% if m.rationale %} — {{ m.rationale }}{% endif %}',
    '{% if m.rationale %} ({{ m.rationale }}){% endif %}'),
   ('{% if m.steps %}  Ordered step templates:',
    '{% if m.steps %}  Ordered step templates (context to expand into concrete plan-specific steps — do not copy verbatim):'),
   ('{% for tpl in m.steps %}    {{ forloop.counter }}. {{ tpl }}',
    '{% for tpl in m.steps %}    {{ forloop.counter }}. ({{ tpl }})'),
 ],
}

for fid, repls in edits.items():
    body=frags[fid]['body']
    for old,new in repls:
        assert old in body, f"{fid}: OLD NOT FOUND: {old!r}"
        assert body.count(old)==1, f"{fid}: OLD not unique ({body.count(old)}): {old!r}"
        body=body.replace(old,new)
    open(f'/tmp/{fid}.body.txt','w').write(body)
    print(f"OK {fid} -> /tmp/{fid}.body.txt ({len(body)} bytes)")
EOF

input.content:
citability: conform 5 snippets+fragments to the DEC-56 guard; wire it into the gate (TASK-057 / FGAP-047)

Enacts the citability predicate built in verify-citability.py: for each spec,
each grounding-catalogue section the parser structurally checks (via
planner.specs._check_in_catalogue / _catalogue_members + the three
responsibilities-only nested helpers) may render only its admissible identity
field-path in a citable (non-parenthetical) position. Any other (context) field
of a checked section rendered non-parenthetically is a foot-gun: it invites the
model to copy a value the parser will reject. The guard ran 9/14 before this
change, naming 5 violating specs (12 violation lines).

Conformed the 5 violators — each edit applied to BOTH the snippet (the
human-readable source under prompt-workshop/snippets/) and its byte-identical
.workshopping prompt-fragment (the bytes the render path + the guard actually
assemble). The snippet/fragment parity is re-asserted green by
verify-render-parity (cross-check (a) snippet==spec, (b) gb-fragment subset).

  - 06-propose-assignments  / FRAG-gb-divisions-labels-only:
      division.scope_summary moved from an em-dash continuation into parens.
  - 07-propose-responsibilities / FRAG-gb-divisions-inventory:
      d.scope_summary moved into parens (Division "<label>" prefix kept).
  - 10-propose-evidence / FRAG-gb-divisions-owner:
      d.scope_summary moved into parens.
  - 13-propose-accreditation-standards / FRAG-gb-accreditation-standards-choose:
      reshaped the row so only s.code sits citably outside parens; s.name,
      s.category, s.text now read as a parenthetical gloss
      (- "<code>" (<name> — <category> — <text>)). Admissible identity is code.
  - 05-decompose-action-steps / FRAG-gb-planning-methods-recipes:
      planning_methods is structurally checked by name. Two context fields

READ-ONLY design. Produce a concrete, source-grounded implementation design (no code edits). Cite file:line. Recommend ONE approach decisively, with the rationale and the exact mechanism.

REPO: /Users/david/Projects/wasc-school-wide-improvement-plan ; guard prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py ; parser school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py.

CORROBORATED ROOT CAUSE: the citability guard's `_checked_sections_for` (verify-citability.py:173) derives each spec's structurally-checked grounding sections + admissible identity fields by (i) a regex `_CATALOGUE_MEMBERS_RE` (line 94) over the parse fn source that matches only STRING-LITERAL `_catalogue_members(grounding, "<section>", identity="<id>")` calls, plus (ii) 3 hardcoded responsibilities helpers in `_NESTED_HELPER_PATHS` (line 102). It MISSES two real validation-path classes, so it derives empty/partial checked-sets for two specs (both currently conformant but UNGUARDED — a future snippet edit there would re-open the citability gap undetected, defeating the guard's purpose for those specs):
- A3 (propose-domain-alignment): variable `section_key` from `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION` (dict, specs.py:3166) and `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` (tuple, 3148), used at `_catalogue_members(grounding, section_key, identity="label")` (3328-3329, 3337-3338, 3355-3356). Validated sections: learner_outcomes, stakeholder_groups, areas_for_improvement, policies — all identity=label.
- G1 (propose-review-loop): sub-validators `_g1_communication` (3537), `_g1_review_event` (3623) call `_check_in_catalogue` internally (3567/3578/3602/3654) with members passed as params populated by literal `_catalogue_members` at 3802-3804 (stakeholder_groups/divisions/frequencies). The guard sees the literal calls but not that the sub-validators consume them for additional fields.

GOAL: design how to make the guard's derivation COMPLETE and ROBUST — it must enforce the invariant for all 14 specs (incl. A3 + G1) and not silently miss a FUTURE spec that uses a variable section key or a sub-validator. The derivation must stay grounded in specs.py as the source of truth (no re-encoded catalogue duplication that can drift).

DESIGN — evaluate these candidate approaches against the facts and RECOMMEND one decisively:
1. **AST/dataflow derivation in the guard**: walk specs.py with `ast`, resolve dict/tuple literal maps (`_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION`/`_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED`), trace `_catalogue_members`/`_check_in_catalogue` calls (incl. inside sub-validators, scoped by the `spec=` arg), follow `members=` params back to their source. Assess whether this is sound, whether it changes parser behavior (it must not), and whether adding it is justified given TASK-057's "don't change specs.py logic" constraint (a pure additive declaration vs a logic change).
3. **Targeted extension**: extend the guard to also read `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION`/`_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` and the `_g1_*` helpers specifically. Assess whether this covers the general pattern or just patches A3/G1 by name (leaving the future-spec blind spot the guard exists to prevent).

For the recommended approach, specify: the exact derivation mechanism, where it lives, how it stays robust for a future spec, how it is itself TESTED (so the guard's own completeness is verifiable — e.g. a check that every `_check_in_catalogue(spec="X")` site in specs.py is reflected in the guard's derived map for X), and the verification that A3 + G1 become guarded (incl. a fails-on-injection per spec). Name the exact files/functions to change. Flag any choice that genuinely needs the human's call (e.g. touching specs.py additively).

REPORT: the corroborated path inventory, the recommended approach + why the others are inferior, the concrete mechanism + files, the self-test for guard completeness, and the per-spec verification. Do not write the code.

# TASK-057 iterate-to-zero: complete the citability guard's derivation (AST-based) so it enforces the invariant for all 14 specs

## Context

TASK-057 (DEC-56 / FGAP-047) built the citability guard + conformed 5 violators (06/07/10/13 + 05) — committed e2afd5b; that work stands (5 snippets parenthesized, parity 14/14, gate green). A separate adversarial audit then found the guard's DERIVATION incomplete: `_checked_sections_for` (verify-citability.py:173) derives each spec's structurally-checked sections by a regex over string-literal `_catalogue_members(grounding, "<section>", …)` calls (line 94) plus 3 hardcoded responsibilities helpers (line 102). It MISSES two real validation-path classes, so it derives an empty/partial checked-set for two specs — both currently conformant but UNGUARDED, which defeats the guard's non-whack-a-mole purpose there:
- **A3 (propose-domain-alignment):** validates 4 sections (learner_outcomes, stakeholder_groups, areas_for_improvement, policies; all identity=label) via a VARIABLE `section_key` from `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION` (specs.py:3166) / `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` (3148) at calls 3328-3356 → regex matches none → A3 derives `{}`.
- **G1 (propose-review-loop):** the authoritative `_check_in_catalogue` raise-sites live in sub-validators `_g1_communication` (3537) / `_g1_review_event` (3623); members flow in as params from the literal calls at 3802-3804. The guard captures the literals only by coincidence; a future G1 edit routing a different identity through a sub-validator would silently under-derive.

Root cause (Phase-1 + Phase-2 agent-derived, source-corroborated): the guard text-matches member-*production* sites, but the invariant is defined by member-*consumption* sites (`_check_in_catalogue`), which use variable section keys and live in helpers reached via parameters. The fix is to replace the regex/hardcoded derivation with an AST/dataflow pass that traces every `_check_in_catalogue` raise-site (scoped by its `spec=` arg) back to its catalogue section + identity — covering variable keys, sub-validators, and nested helpers uniformly, grounded in specs.py (no re-encoded catalogue that can drift), with zero parser-logic change.

Outcome: the guard enforces the citability invariant for all 14 specs (incl. A3 + G1) and cannot silently miss a future spec; its own completeness is self-tested against the raw raise-site enumeration.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] The guard's per-spec checked-section derivation is AST-based: it traces every `_check_in_catalogue(value, members=…, spec=<code>)` raise-site reachable from each parse function (including inside sub-validators via parameter-flow, and via variable/looped section keys resolved from module-level literal maps) back to its `(grounding section, identity field-path)`. No regex-on-literals and no hardcoded per-spec section lists (a small helper-return-path registry for the nested responsibilities helpers may remain only if covered by the self-test below).
- [ ] A self-test (run in the gate) independently enumerates every `_check_in_catalogue(spec=<code>)` site in specs.py and asserts the AST derivation produced a non-empty `(section, identity)` for each catalogue-backed raise-site of that spec; it FAILS by name on any raise-site the tracer missed, any unresolved section-key constant, any `spec=` tag inconsistent with its enclosing parse fn, and any consumed nested helper absent from the registry.
- [ ] `_checked_sections_for` now derives A3 = {learner_outcomes, stakeholder_groups, areas_for_improvement, policies}→label and G1 = {stakeholder_groups→label, divisions→label, frequencies→code} (the latter traced THROUGH the sub-validator params), confirmed by assertion. It reproduces the prior-correct maps for the already-covered specs (propose-responsibilities incl. the 3 nested-helper paths; the literal-only specs).
- [ ] Per-spec fails-on-injection for A3 AND G1: surfacing a non-identity context field of a checked section in a citable position makes the guard report a violation naming that spec+field-path; the clean snippets still pass (no false positive).
- [ ] The guard still reports GATE 14/14 on the live (conformant) corpus; verify-render-parity stays 14/14; no snippet/fragment change is needed (A3/G1 conform today).
- [ ] No change to specs.py (parse/resolve), apply_from_substrate.py, .workflows/*, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate, or any output contract. Touch only verify-citability.py (+ a self-test file if separate) and the Makefile gate wiring.
- [ ] Full gate green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, make test-citability incl. the self-test).

## The fix (verify-citability.py only)

Rewrite `_checked_sections_for` (drop `_CATALOGUE_MEMBERS_RE` + `_NESTED_HELPER_PATHS`'s role as the primary mechanism): parse specs.py once (cache); build a module-level literal-constant table (`ast.literal_eval` of dict/tuple/list assignments — resolves `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION`/`_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED`; unresolved constants surfaced by the self-test, never dropped). For each parse fn and its transitively-reachable helpers within specs.py: model each `_catalogue_members(grounding, <sectionarg>, identity=<id>)` result (resolving `<sectionarg>` literal / module-map-subscript value-set / loop-target value-set); then walk every `_check_in_catalogue(value, members=<expr>, spec=<code>, field=<f>)`, resolve `<expr>` (local from a members call, a helper PARAMETER traced to the call-site argument, or a nested-helper return via the registry) → emit `(section, identity_path)` into the spec's map, scoped by `spec=<code>`. Output type unchanged (`{section: set[identity_path]}`) so `main()` is untouched. Add `_self_test()` (the independent raise-site enumeration in the criteria) invoked by the gate. Wire the self-test into the `make test-citability` target.

## Procedure (canonical pipeline; branch seed-leader-head-accountability)

1. This plan is the iterate-to-zero refinement of TASK-057's guard, derived via Phase-1 Explore + Phase-2 Plan agents, source-corroborated.
2. IMPL agent: rewrite the derivation + add the self-test + wire the gate. Self-contained; STOP on ambiguity; explicit staging; touch only verify-citability.py (+ self-test) + Makefile.
3. Re-run the SEPARATE adversarial audit on the rewritten guard: A3 + G1 now derived + injection-failing; self-test catches a removed raise-site; covered-specs map preserved; specs.py untouched; gate green. Loop to zero.
4. Cascade (once the guard is clean): the confirming full run (past spec 07) + check_draft --strict PASS; VER + verification_verifies_item edges + complete-task TASK-056 AND TASK-057 + close FGAP-046/047 + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows + refresh the stale seq-6 focus item.
5. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- Assertions: `_checked_sections_for("propose-domain-alignment")` and `("propose-review-loop")` derive the expected non-empty maps; covered-specs maps unchanged.
- The self-test fails when a `_check_in_catalogue` raise-site is removed from the tracer's reach (prove by a temporary in-test fixture), and passes on the real specs.py.
- Guard GATE 14/14 on the live corpus; per-spec injection (A3 description; G1 divisions.scope_summary) → guard FAILs naming the field-path; reverted.
- verify-render-parity 14/14; full gate green; `git diff` shows only verify-citability.py (+ self-test) + Makefile.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- Investigate/derive via agents from source + corroborate; never author the design. Execute the mandate-determined path; don't float a derivable choice.
- Explicit staging (never `git add -A`); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp`; cleanup via `git restore`/`git clean`, never `git reset`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`.
- Do NOT change specs.py logic, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate, or any output contract. Close the cascade as part of the work (read the spine back).

## Critical files

- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` — rewrite `_checked_sections_for` (AST/dataflow tracer); add `_self_test`; drop the regex/hardcoded primary mechanism.
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — READ-ONLY source of truth: `_check_in_catalogue:483`, `_catalogue_members:448`, A3 `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION:3166`/`_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED:3148`/calls `3328-3356`/`_a3_*` raise-sites `3220,3261`, G1 `_g1_communication:3537`/`_g1_review_event:3623`/literals `3802-3804`, responsibilities helpers `2580+`.
- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/_workshop.py` — `_PARSE_FN_NAMES:341`, `get_parse_function:365` (enumeration + source access the tracer + self-test reuse).
- `school-improvement-plans/Makefile` — `test-citability` target: add the self-test.

allowedPrompts.prompt: run verify-citability.py (incl. its self-test), verify-render-parity.py, and the project gate (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, make test-citability) under uv from school-improvement-plans/ against the dev DB
git add (explicit paths) / commit on the seed-leader-head-accountability branch

It MISSES (a) A3's variable `section_key` (from `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION` dict specs.py:3166 + `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` tuple :3148, used at `_catalogue_members(grounding, section_key, …)` :3328-3356; the actual `_check_in_catalogue(spec="A3")` raise-sites are in `_a3_selection_list`/`_a3_not_addressed_list` ~:3220/:3261) → A3 derives `{}`; and (b) G1's sub-validators `_g1_communication`(:3537)/`_g1_review_event`(:3623) whose `_check_in_catalogue(spec="G1")` raise-sites (:3567/:3578/:3602/:3654) consume members passed as PARAMETERS from the literal `_catalogue_members` at :3802-3804. Both A3 and G1 currently conform but are UNGUARDED.

THE FIX (approved design) — rewrite the derivation to be AST/dataflow over specs.py, grounded in the `_check_in_catalogue` raise-sites (member-CONSUMPTION), not the member-production text-match. Mechanism:
1. Parse specs.py once (cache via `inspect.getsourcefile`/`ast.parse`). Build a module-level literal-constant table by `ast.literal_eval` of top-level dict/tuple/list assignments (resolves `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION`, `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED`). An unresolved constant referenced as a section-key source is surfaced by the self-test, never silently dropped.
2. For each parse fn (via `_workshop.py` `_PARSE_FN_NAMES`/`get_parse_function`) and every function it transitively calls within specs.py: model each `_catalogue_members(grounding, <sectionarg>, identity=<id>)` result, resolving `<sectionarg>` = string literal | a Name bound to a module-map subscript (`_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION[k]` → the map's VALUE-set) | a loop target over a module tuple-of-tuples (`for …, …, section_key in _A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` → the value-set at that position). Bind each result to the variable it's assigned to.
3. Walk every `_check_in_catalogue(value, members=<expr>, spec=<code>, field=<f>)` reachable from the parse fn (incl. inside sub-validators). Resolve `<expr>` to its member-source: a local from a `_catalogue_members` result (2); a helper PARAMETER traced to the call-site argument in the parent (this catches G1's `stakeholder_members=`→`_g1_communication`); or a nested-helper return via a small helper-return registry (the 3 responsibilities helpers — `responsibilities.statement`, `positions.label`, `positions.responsibilities.statement`). Emit `(section, identity_path)` into the spec's map keyed by `spec=<code>`. Skip `members=None`/index/non-catalogue sources.
4. Keep `_checked_sections_for`'s return type `{section: set[identity_path]}` so `main()` is unchanged. Drop the regex + the hardcoded-as-primary mechanism (the registry remains ONLY for nested-helper return paths, covered by the self-test).
5. Add `_self_test()` (invoked by the gate, before/around the render loop OR as a sibling test run by make test-citability): independently enumerate every `_check_in_catalogue(spec=<code>)` AST node and the parse fn it is reachable from; assert the tracer emitted a non-empty `(section,identity)` for each catalogue-backed raise-site of that spec; FAIL by name on any missed raise-site, any unresolved section-key constant, any `spec=` tag inconsistent with its enclosing parse fn, any consumed nested helper absent from the registry.

EXPECTED RESULTS (assert these): `_checked_sections_for("propose-domain-alignment")` == {learner_outcomes,stakeholder_groups,areas_for_improvement,policies}→{label}; `("propose-review-loop")` ⊇ {stakeholder_groups→label, divisions→label, frequencies→code} traced through the sub-validators; the already-covered specs (propose-responsibilities incl. the 3 nested-helper paths; the literal-only specs F1/A2/etc.) reproduce their prior maps. If A3/G1 expected maps don't materialize from your tracer, STOP and report (don't hardcode them to pass).

Restrict to the sections the spec STRUCTURALLY checks + their admissible
  identity field-path(s). That checked-section -> admissible-path map is
  DERIVED from `planner.specs` by AST/dataflow (not re-encoded here, and not
  a text-match over the member-PRODUCTION call). The derivation is grounded
  in the member-CONSUMPTION sites — every

      _check_in_catalogue(value, members=<expr>, spec=<code>, field=<f>)

  raise-site reachable from the parse function — because a `_catalogue_members`
  result that is never consumed by a `_check_in_catalogue` checks nothing, and
  a member can reach a raise-site through a variable, a sub-validator
  parameter, or a nested-helper return that a literal text-match never sees.
  For each raise-site we resolve `<expr>` back to its member source:

    * a local bound to a `_catalogue_members(grounding, <sectionarg>, identity=<id>)`
      result — `<sectionarg>` is a string literal | a Name bound to a
      module-map subscript (`_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION[k]` -> the map's value-set)
      | a loop target over a module tuple-of-tuples
      (`for …, …, section_key in _A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` -> the value-set at that
      tuple position). The admissible path is the `<id>` identity.
    * a helper PARAMETER traced to the call-site argument in the parent parse
      function (this is how G1's `_g1_communication(stakeholder_members=…)` and
      A3's `_a3_selection_list(catalogue_members=…)` resolve).
    * a nested-helper return via the responsibilities-only helper-return
      registry, whose validated paths are their documented contract:

        _responsibility_statements_under_division  -> responsibilities.statement
        _position_labels_under                      -> positions.label
        _responsibility_statements_under_position  -> positions.responsibilities.statement

  (`members=None` / index sources / non-catalogue sources are skipped.)

  A spec with no catalogue-backed raise-site reachable from its parse
  function has an empty checked-section set -> nothing to flag (its free-text
  fields are governed by `planner._freetext_audit`, not this guard).

  `_self_test()` independently re-enumerates every `_check_in_catalogue(spec=…)`
  AST node + the parse function it is reachable from, and asserts the tracer
  emitted a non-empty `(section, identity)` for each catalogue-backed
  raise-site — failing by name on a missed raise-site, an unresolved
  section-key constant, a `spec=` tag inconsistent with its enclosing parse
  function, or a consumed nested helper absent from the registry.

# Detector for the spec's `_catalogue_members` call sites in the parse
# source: `_catalogue_members(grounding, "<section>", identity="<id>")`.
_CATALOGUE_MEMBERS_RE = re.compile(
    r"_catalogue_members\(\s*grounding\s*,\s*"
    r'"(?P<section>[^"]+)"\s*,\s*identity="(?P<identity>[^"]+)"'
)

# The three responsibilities-only nested helpers and the (section,
# field-path) each one validates — its documented contract. Detected by
# presence of the helper name in the parse source.
_NESTED_HELPER_PATHS: dict[str, tuple[str, str]] = {
    "_responsibility_statements_under_division": (
        "division_responsibility_atoms",
        "responsibilities.statement",
    ),
    "_position_labels_under": (
        "division_responsibility_atoms",
        "positions.label",
    ),
    "_responsibility_statements_under_position": (
        "division_responsibility_atoms",
        "positions.responsibilities.statement",
    ),
}

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, "../prompt-workshop")
import importlib.util
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("vc", "../prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py")
vc = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(vc)

# Injection fixture: append a citable non-identity field reference to the
# rendered body for ONE spec only, via the guard's own assemble_spec_body seam.
orig_assemble = vc.assemble_spec_body
INJECT = {
    # spec_key : a (section, non-identity field) cited in a NON-parenthetical position
    "propose-domain-alignment": ("learner_outcomes", "description"),
    "propose-review-loop": ("divisions", "scope_summary"),
}
# Track which spec is being assembled by matching the spec dict's spec_key.
def make_assemble(target_spec_key):
    def _assemble(spec_item, fragments):
        body = orig_assemble(spec_item, fragments)
        if spec_item.get("spec_key") == target_spec_key:
            section, field = INJECT[target_spec_key]
            # A citable (em-dash continuation, not parenthetical) reference.
            body += ("\n{%% for _r in %s %%}- cite this: {{ _r.%s }}\n{%% endfor %%}"
                     % (section, field))
        return body
    return _assemble

for target in INJECT:
    vc.assemble_spec_body = make_assemble(target)
    rc = vc.main()
    print(f"\n=== injected {target} {INJECT[target]} -> main() exit {rc} ===\n")
vc.assemble_spec_body = orig_assemble

def _checked_sections_for(spec_key: str) -> dict[str, set[str]]:
    """Derive `{section: {admissible_field_path, ...}}` for `spec_key` from the
    AST/dataflow trace of every `_check_in_catalogue` raise-site reachable
    from its parse function. Empty dict when the spec checks no catalogue."""
    # Resolve the parse function's NAME via the registry (the source-of-truth
    # bridge); `get_parse_function` confirms it resolves in planner.specs.
    get_parse_function(spec_key)
    out: dict[str, set[str]] = {}
    for section, path in _checked_section_paths_for_fn(_PARSE_FN_NAMES[spec_key]):
        out.setdefault(section, set()).add(path)
    return out

prompt-workshop: replace verify-citability checked-section derivation with an AST/dataflow tracer over the _check_in_catalogue raise-sites (TASK-057 / DEC-56 / FGAP-047)

Aim: close the FGAP-047 defect where verify-citability.py derived each
spec's structurally-checked grounding sections from a regex over
string-literal `_catalogue_members(grounding, "<section>", identity="<id>")`
calls (the member-PRODUCTION text) plus three hardcoded responsibilities
helper names. That text-match could not see two consumption paths and so
left two specs unguarded:

  - A3 (`parse_propose_domain_alignment`) builds its section key as a
    variable (`section_key = _A3_GROUNDING_SECTION[json_key]`, and the
    `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` tuple loop) and passes the resulting members as a
    PARAMETER into `_a3_selection_list` / `_a3_not_addressed_list`. With no
    string-literal section in the parse-fn source, the regex derived `{}` —
    A3's catalogue sections were checked by nothing.
  - G1 (`parse_propose_review_loop`) consumes its catalogue members inside
    the sub-validators `_g1_communication` / `_g1_review_event` via passed
    parameters; the regex happened to derive G1's sections only because the
    literal `_catalogue_members` calls sit in the parse fn body, not because
    consumption was traced. Both A3 and G1 conform on the live corpus today,
    so the gap was latent, not a current violation.

Change (read-only against planner.specs; no specs.py edit):

  - Parse planner.specs once (cached) into an AST + a module-level
    literal-constant table (`ast.literal_eval` of top-level dict/tuple/list
    assigns — resolves `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION`, `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED`).
  - Derive `{section: {identity-path}}` from the member-CONSUMPTION sites:
    every `_check_in_catalogue(members=<expr>, spec=<code>, field=<f>)`
    raise-site reachable (transitive intra-module call graph) from the
    parse function. For each raise-site the `members=` expression is
    resolved to its source — a local bound to a `_catalogue_members(...)`
    result (section key = string literal | module-map subscript value-set |
    for-loop target resolved POSITIONALLY over a module tuple-of-tuples), a
    helper PARAMETER traced to the call-site argument in the parent, or a
    nested-helper return via a small registry (the 3 responsibilities
    helpers). `members=None` / index / non-catalogue sources are skipped.
  - `_checked_sections_for` keeps its `{section: set[identity-path]}` return
    so `main()` is unchanged; the regex + hardcoded-as-primary mechanism is
    dropped (the registry remains only for nested-helper return paths).
  - Add `_self_test()` (invoked at the top of `main()`, so `make
    test-citability` runs it): independently re-enumerates every
    `_check_in_catalogue(spec=…)` AST node + the parse fn it is reachable
    from, and fails by name on a missed/unresolvable raise-site, an
    unresolved section-key constant, a `spec=` tag inconsistent with its
    enclosing parse fn (`_EXPECTED_SPEC_CODE`; note `spec="F1"` lives in
    `parse_decompose_action_steps`, so the code is not the registry
    spec_key), or a consumed nested helper absent from the registry.

Observed before commit (dev DB DSN):
  - Tracer reproduces the prior maps for the 12 already-covered specs
    verbatim; A3 now derives {learner_outcomes, stakeholder_groups,
    areas_for_improvement, policies}->{label}; G1 derives {stakeholder_groups
    ->label, divisions->label, frequencies->code}.
  - verify-citability GATE 14/14 exit 0; verify-render-parity 14/14 exit 0.
  - `_self_test()` passes on real specs.py; proven to bite via two in-test
    fixtures (drop a nested helper from the registry; blind the tracer to

    then reverts to PASS. specs.py untouched in both.
  - Per-spec injection (via the `assemble_spec_body` seam, no snippet edit):

    Success 325 files; pytest 3214 passed; make test-js 48/48.

EOF

input.content:
# TASK-057 iterate-to-zero (round 2): clear the 5 audit findings on the AST citability guard

## Context

The AST/dataflow citability guard rewrite (commit 7298dd5) is correct and sound — A3 + G1 are now guarded, the self-test bites, per-spec injection bites, the 12 covered specs reproduce their prior maps, gate green. A separate adversarial audit returned 5 findings (no correctness defect; quality/precision/record + one pre-existing gate gap). Iterate-to-zero clears them. Each is corroborated against the guard source.

- **F1 — dead code.** `verify-citability.py:572 _enclosing_function_name` is defined, never called (confirmed: only the `def`). ruff default rules + the `mypy .` gate (which excludes the workshop dir, see F5) don't catch a module-level unused function; PyRight flagged it.
- **F2 — A3 section_key over-approximation (harmless, imprecise).** `_resolve_name_section_keys:305` unions every binding of the name `section_key` across A3's three loops (the `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION` subscript loops + the `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` positional loop), so the not-addressed raise-site resolves to all 4 A3 sections rather than position-2's `{learner_outcomes, areas_for_improvement}`. The audit confirms it cannot false-positive or hide a violation (label is A3's sole admissible identity), but it mismatches the docstring's "value-set at that tuple position" claim.
- **F4 — multi-line parenthetical over-flag (latent false-positive).** `_surfaced_sentinels_by_line:156` resets paren `depth=0` per line (:167), so a sentinel on a continuation line inside a `(` opened on a prior line is treated as citable. Conservative (over-reports, never hides), not currently triggered.
- **F3 — framing overstates G1.** The records say "A3 + G1 were unguarded"; A3 was genuinely unguarded (regex derived `{}`), but G1 was already regex-mapped via its literal `_catalogue_members` calls — the rewrite makes G1 robust (traces the real raise-sites), it did not close an active G1 gap. Code correct; the narrative needs accuracy.
- **F5 — pre-existing gate-coverage gap.** `make typecheck` (`mypy .` from the Django root) excludes `../prompt-workshop/dispatch`, so verify-citability.py's mypy state (incl. 3 pre-existing `# type: ignore[import-not-found]` that are `unused-ignore` when mypy runs on the file directly) is never gated. Predates this work; broader than TASK-057 (a gate-policy change over the whole workshop dispatch dir).

Outcome: F1/F2/F4 fixed in the guard; F3 corrected in the records; F5 filed as a first-class gap for a separate scoped decision (mandate-007). A re-audit returns zero on the TASK-057 surface.

## Success Criteria (the contract)

- [ ] `_enclosing_function_name` is removed; no other dead code/unused symbol remains in verify-citability.py (PyRight clean on the file for unused symbols).
- [ ] A3's `section_key` resolution is per-binding: the `_A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` raise-site resolves to exactly position-2's `{learner_outcomes, areas_for_improvement}` (not all 4), and the docstring matches the implementation; the A3 spec map is unchanged overall (raise-site 3220 still legitimately contributes all 4 at identity `label`), and the guard still GATE 14/14 + self-test passes + A3 injection still bites.
- [ ] Paren detection carries depth across lines, so a sentinel inside a multi-line `(...)` is treated as context (not citable); proven by a targeted fixture; the live corpus stays GATE 14/14 (no false-positive introduced or removed on the real snippets).
- [ ] FGAP-047's description is corrected: A3 was unguarded; G1 was regex-mapped and is now robust (not an active gap closed). No false "G1 unguarded" claim remains in the live records.
- [ ] F5 is filed as a new framework-gap (the workshop-dispatch mypy gate-coverage gap) — investigated and source-corroborated — with its fix left as a separately-scoped decision; not folded into this task.
- [ ] No change to specs.py, apply_from_substrate.py, .workflows/*, snippets/fragments, output contracts, the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate. Touch only verify-citability.py (F1/F2/F4) + the .context records (F3/F5).
- [ ] Full gate green (manage.py check, ruff, mypy ., pytest, make test-js, make test-citability incl. self-test); re-audit returns `Total findings: 0`.

## The fix

1. **verify-citability.py (F1/F2/F4):** remove `_enclosing_function_name`; scope `_resolve_name_section_keys` to the specific binding/loop of `section_key` (so the NOT_ADDRESSED loop yields only its positional value-set) and align the docstring; change `_surfaced_sentinels_by_line` to carry paren depth across lines (initialize once, not per line). Re-run guard + self-test + the A3/G1 injection + verify-render-parity + full gate, all green.
2. **FGAP-047 (F3):** `pi-context update-block-item` to correct the description's framing (A3 unguarded; G1 regex-mapped→robust). Record only.
3. **FGAP-048 (F5):** file the gate-coverage gap (investigated): `make typecheck`/`mypy .` excludes `../prompt-workshop/dispatch`; the dir's type state (incl. the 3 unused-ignore) is ungated; fix candidates = add the dir to the mypy gate + resolve the ignores, OR a dedicated workshop-typecheck target — left for a scoped decision. Link to nothing (standalone gap).

## Procedure (canonical pipeline; branch seed-leader-head-accountability)

1. This plan is the round-2 iterate-to-zero refinement (audit findings), derived + corroborated against source.
2. IMPL agent: the F1/F2/F4 guard edits. Self-contained; STOP on ambiguity; explicit staging; touch only verify-citability.py.
3. Re-run the SEPARATE adversarial audit on the guard → must return 0 (the 5 prior findings resolved: F1 gone, F2 precise, F4 cross-line, F3 corrected in records, F5 filed-not-open-against-TASK-057). Loop if not.
4. Records: correct FGAP-047 (F3); file FGAP-048 (F5).
5. Cascade (once audit is zero): the confirming full run (past spec 07) + check_draft --strict PASS; VER + verification_verifies_item edges + complete-task TASK-056 AND TASK-057 + close FGAP-046/047 + context-validate + LOG/dispatch rows + refresh the stale seq-6 focus item.
6. Merge to main only on the user's explicit authority.

## Verification

- guard GATE 14/14; self-test passes + still bites (drop a raise-site in-test → fails); A3 + G1 injection still bite; A3 NOT_ADDRESSED resolves to position-2 only (assert); multi-line-paren fixture treated as context.
- verify-render-parity 14/14; full gate green; `git diff` for the guard commit shows only verify-citability.py.
- FGAP-047 reads accurately; FGAP-048 exists; context-validate 0 errors.
- Independent adversarial-audit agent returns `Total findings: 0`.

## Discipline

- Derive/corroborate via agents from source; execute the mandate-determined path; don't float derivable choices.
- Explicit staging (never `git add -A`); commit via `git commit -F` from `/tmp`; cleanup via `git restore`/`git clean`; `.pi-context.json` ends on `.context`.
- Do NOT change specs.py logic / the DEC-40/DEC-53 gate / output contracts. Close the cascade as part of the work (read the spine back).

## Critical files

- `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py` — F1 (`_enclosing_function_name:572`), F2 (`_resolve_name_section_keys:305` + `_resolve_section_keys:277`/`_const_tuple_position_values:346` + docstring ~:51), F4 (`_surfaced_sentinels_by_line:156`).
- `.context` framework-gaps — FGAP-047 (correct framing), FGAP-048 (new gate-coverage gap).
- `school-improvement-plans/planner/specs.py` — READ-ONLY source the guard traces (unchanged).

allowedPrompts.prompt: run verify-citability.py (+ self-test), verify-render-parity.py, and the project gate (manage.py check, ruff, mypy, pytest, make test-js, make test-citability) under uv from school-improvement-plans/ against the dev DB
pi-context CLI writes (update FGAP-047, append FGAP-048); state.mjs for cascade/LOG
git add (explicit paths) / commit on the seed-leader-head-accountability branch

You are an IMPL agent clearing 3 adversarial-audit findings on an approved task (TASK-057 iterate-to-zero round 2; DEC-56). STOP only if genuinely blocked.

BRANCH: seed-leader-head-accountability (checked out, clean; commit here; do NOT merge). Stage EXPLICIT paths only. Touch ONLY `prompt-workshop/dispatch/verify-citability.py`.

The file is an AST/dataflow citability guard over planner/specs.py; it currently passes GATE 14/14, self-test, and per-spec injection. Three audit findings to fix WITHOUT regressing any of that:

**F1 — remove dead code.** `_enclosing_function_name` (def at line ~572) is unused (zero call sites; confirmed). Delete the function. Confirm nothing else references it.

**F2 — make A3 `section_key` resolution per-binding (precise), and align the docstring.** `_resolve_name_section_keys` (~line 305) currently unions EVERY binding of the name `section_key` across A3's three loops (the two `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION[...]` subscript loops at specs.py:3328/3337 and the `for …, …, section_key in _A3_NOT_ADDRESSED` positional loop at specs.py:3355). Effect: the not-addressed raise-site (`_a3_not_addressed_list`, specs.py:~3261) over-resolves to all 4 A3 sections instead of position-2's `{learner_outcomes, areas_for_improvement}`. Fix: resolve `section_key` for a given `_catalogue_members`/raise-site to the binding that actually reaches THAT use (the specific loop), not the union of all same-named bindings in the function — so the NOT_ADDRESSED-derived members resolve to exactly position-2's value-set, and the subscript-loop-derived members resolve to the `_A3_GROUNDING_SECTION` value-set. Update the docstring (~line 51) to describe the actual behavior accurately. NOTE: A3's overall spec map must stay {learner_outcomes, stakeholder_groups, areas_for_improvement, policies}→label (the gate-required raise-site at specs.py:~3220 legitimately contributes all 4 at identity label) — this fix only tightens the not-addressed site's resolution, it must not drop a real section.

**F4 — carry paren depth across lines.** `_surfaced_sentinels_by_line` (~line 156) resets the open-paren `depth` to 0 at the start of each line, so a sentinel rendered on a continuation line inside a `(...)` opened on a PRIOR line is wrongly treated as non-parenthetical (citable). Fix: track `depth` across the whole rendered text (initialize once before the line loop; do not reset per line), so a multi-line parenthetical's interior is correctly treated as context. Keep the per-char "inside parens" determination otherwise identical. The live corpus must stay GATE 14/14 (this only affects multi-line-paren cases, none currently on the real snippets).

VERIFY (observed-green before commit), from school-improvement-plans/ under uv + DSN:
- `verify-citability.py` → GATE 14/14, exit 0; its `_self_test()` passes; prove the self-test still BITES (in-test/monkeypatch make the tracer miss a raise-site → fails; revert).
- Assert `_checked_sections_for("propose-domain-alignment")` == {learner_outcomes,stakeholder_groups,areas_for_improvement,policies}→{label} (unchanged overall), AND that the not-addressed site now resolves to exactly position-2 (dump the per-site resolution to confirm F2). G1 map unchanged.
- A3 injection (citable learner_outcomes.description) and G1 injection (citable divisions.scope_summary) still bite (violation named). A targeted multi-line-paren fixture: a sentinel inside a `(...)` spanning two lines is treated as context (not flagged).
- verify-render-parity 14/14, exit 0.
- Full gate: manage.py check; ruff check . (and the dispatch dir); mypy .; pytest; make test-js; make test-citability. All green. Also run `pyright`/confirm no unused-symbol on verify-citability.py if available; at minimum confirm `_enclosing_function_name` is gone and no new unused symbol introduced.

CONSTRAINTS: touch ONLY verify-citability.py. READ-ONLY against specs.py (no edit). No `git add -A`. `.pi-context.json` ends on .context.

COMMIT after green: `git commit -F /tmp/<msg>` (forensic; aims not certainties; no author-credit; explicit path).
